title: A2-MAE: A Spatial-temporal-spectral Unified Remote Sensing Pre-training Method Based on Anchor-aware Masked Autoencoder

architecture.notes: Built on the MAE framework with a unified single backbone. Adds an Anchor-Aware Masking (AAM) strategy and a Geographic Encoding Module (GEM). Uses patch size 16x16 and selects three images per structured image set during pretraining.

pretraining.method_details: A unified masked autoencoding approach for remote sensing that jointly exploits spatial, temporal, and spectral complementarity across multi-source image sets. The key novelties are anchor-aware masking, which adapts masking based on a selected anchor image to avoid feature leakage across sources/times, and a geographic encoding module that injects latitude, longitude, and GSD priors.
pretraining.objective: masked patch reconstruction

key_contribution: The paper introduces STSSD, a large global spatial-temporal-spectral structured remote sensing pretraining dataset, and A2-MAE, a unified MAE-based model that can learn from diverse multi-source remote sensing imagery within a single backbone. Its anchor-aware masking and geographic encoding improve representation learning and generalization across classification, segmentation, and change detection tasks.

title: A SENTINEL-3 FOUNDATION MODEL FOR OCEAN COLOUR

architecture.notes: Based on the Prithvi-EO architecture and pretrained with a masked autoencoder. Uses 1×42×42 inputs with 1×2×2 patch size for Sentinel-3 OLCI/SLSTR data. Fine-tuning uses the pretrained encoder plus a U-Net-based decoder and task-specific pixel-wise regression head. The paper mentions 50 million parameters in the methods, though the conclusion appears to contain a likely typo ('50,000 parameters').

pretraining.method_details: Self-supervised masked autoencoder pretraining adapted from Prithvi-EO for ocean colour remote sensing. The model reconstructs masked Sentinel-3 OLCI reflectance bands, optionally with SLSTR sea surface temperature, using smaller image sizes and finer patches suited to 300 m ocean data and non-temporal single-image inputs.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction

datasets: Sentinel-3 A and B L2 OLCI full resolution water reflectances
Sentinel-3 L2 SLSTR sea surface temperature
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global ocean coverage sampled across Longhurst regions, excluding polar regions (APLR, ANTA) and inland water (LAKE)

downstream_datasets: A compilation of global bio-optical in situ data for ocean-colour satellite applications - version 3
Bermuda Atlantic Time-Series Study (BATS) primary production
A database of ocean primary productivity from the 14C method
Combined constraints on global ocean primary production using observations and models
California Current Ecosystem LTER primary production estimates from 14C uptake
key_contribution: The paper introduces the first Vision Transformer-based foundation model for ocean colour remote sensing using Sentinel-3 OLCI/SLSTR data, adapting the Prithvi-EO masked autoencoder framework to 300 m marine observations. It shows that self-supervised pretraining improves data efficiency and yields more realistic spatial patterns for downstream chlorophyll-a and primary production estimation, especially under very limited labeled data.

title: AnySat: One Earth Observation Model for Many Resolutions, Scales, and Modalities

architecture.backbone: Vision Transformer-style transformer with scale-adaptive patch encoder and cross-attention modality combiner
architecture.notes: Main model has 125M parameters. Architecture includes modality-specific MLP projectors (33M total for 11 projectors), a shared spatial transformer patch encoder (45M), a modality combiner with self-attention and cross-attention blocks (49M), and a JEPA student-side predictor transformer (29M). Uses scale-adaptive sub-patch encoding, LTAE for temporal modalities, and a teacher-student EMA setup during pretraining. More than 75% of learnable parameters are shared across modalities and resolutions.

pretraining.method_details: The paper introduces a multimodal JEPA for Earth observation that predicts teacher multimodal patch embeddings for dropped spatial regions instead of reconstructing pixels. It combines this with a contrastive loss enforcing cross-modal consistency for spatially aligned patches, plus scale-adaptive patch encoders so one model can ingest heterogeneous resolutions, scales, and sensor combinations without rescaling.
pretraining.objective: latent feature prediction of dropped patches plus contrastive alignment across modalities for the same spatial patch

pretraining_data.num_images: about 1.9M+ samples/tiles total (including 1.3M PLANTED, 515k S2NAIP-URBAN, 78k FLAIR, 50k TreeSatAI-TS, 2.4k PASTIS-HD)

key_contribution: The paper proposes AnySat, a single self-supervised Earth observation model that can handle heterogeneous resolutions, scales, and sensor modalities using a scale-adaptive transformer architecture and a multimodal JEPA objective. It also introduces GeoPlex, a curated multimodal pretraining collection, and shows that one pretrained model achieves state-of-the-art or near state-of-the-art performance across diverse EO tasks and unseen sensor configurations.

title: Bridging Remote Sensors with Multisensor Geospatial Foundation Models

architecture.notes: Built on SIMMIM with a shared Swin Transformer encoder across sensor modalities, separate trainable patch embedding layers for each sensor, and separate decoders per sensor. Uses sparsely gated Mixture-of-Experts to replace MLP layers in the encoder; experiments use patch size 16x16 and 8 experts.

pretraining.method: masked image modeling with cross-sensor reconstruction
pretraining.method_details: The model supports both paired and unpaired multisensor data. For paired samples from the same geolocation, it reconstructs either the same sensor or the corresponding paired sensor, enabling joint cross-sensor representation learning. For unpaired samples, it falls back to standard masked self-reconstruction. It also uses MoE within the encoder to better handle multisensor heterogeneity.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction / cross-sensor masked reconstruction

key_contribution: The paper proposes msGFM, a multisensor geospatial foundation model that unifies four remote sensing modalities through a shared encoder and cross-sensor masked image modeling. Its main novelty is learning from both paired and unpaired multisensor data, enabling stronger joint representations and improved performance across multiple single-sensor and multisensor downstream tasks.

title: Change-Aware Sampling and Contrastive Learning for Satellite Images

architecture.notes: Built on a contrastive learning framework using MoCo v2; experiments use ResNet-18 and ResNet-50 backbones, with ResNet-50 as the primary/larger CNN variant. The method modifies SeCo by adding long-term temporal negatives and a change-aware conditional contrastive loss. Also shown to generalize to SimCLR.

pretraining.method: contrastive learning (MoCo v2) with change-aware contrastive loss
pretraining.method_details: The proposed Change-Aware Contrastive (CACo) loss leverages both short-term and long-term temporal pairs from the same location. Short-term temporal differences are treated as seasonal augmentations, while long-term pairs are pushed apart only when an estimated meaningful change has occurred. Change is estimated during training by comparing long-term feature differences to short-term seasonal variation using a ratio, smoothed over epochs and clustered with a GMM. The paper also introduces improved geography-aware sampling near cities and rejects all-ocean samples.
pretraining.objective: contrastive instance discrimination with seasonal invariance and conditional sensitivity to long-term change

key_contribution: The paper proposes CACo, a self-supervised contrastive learning method for satellite imagery that uses long-term temporal information to distinguish meaningful permanent change from short-term seasonal variation. It also introduces change-aware conditional negatives and improved geographic sampling, yielding stronger representations across classification, segmentation, change detection, and change-event retrieval.

title: CROMA: Remote Sensing Representations with Contrastive Radar-Optical Masked Autoencoders

architecture.notes: Primary model uses three transformers: a unimodal radar ViT encoder, a unimodal optical ViT encoder, and a multimodal radar-optical transformer encoder with self-attention and cross-attention. Largest variant is CROMA-L with ViT-L backbone; optical encoder has 24 layers, radar encoder and multimodal encoder each use half-depth. Uses 8x8 patches, default 120x120 inputs, and a lightweight 1-layer 512-d transformer decoder during pretraining only. Introduces 2D-ALiBi for self-attention and X-ALiBi for cross-attention instead of standard positional embeddings.

pretraining.method_details: CROMA jointly combines radar-optical cross-modal InfoNCE contrastive learning on masked unimodal representations with multimodal masked autoencoding that reconstructs masked radar and optical patches from fused representations. The novelty is designing a CoCa-inspired but remote-sensing-specific framework for spatially aligned Sentinel-1/Sentinel-2 data, plus new 2D-ALiBi and X-ALiBi positional biasing for self- and cross-attention.
pretraining.objective: cross-modal radar↔optical contrastive alignment and masked patch reconstruction of radar and optical data

key_contribution: The paper proposes CROMA, a self-supervised multimodal remote sensing foundation model that jointly learns from aligned Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 multispectral imagery using complementary contrastive and masked autoencoding objectives. It also introduces 2D-ALiBi and X-ALiBi positional biases, improving multimodal fusion and enabling strong extrapolation to much larger test-time image sizes.

title: CSP: Self-Supervised Contrastive Spatial Pre-Training for Geospatial-Visual Representations

architecture.backbone: Dual-encoder with Space2Vec grid location encoder and image encoder based on InceptionV3 (iNat2018) or ResNet-50 (fMoW); primary image backbone for remote sensing experiments is Geo-SSL pretrained ResNet-50
architecture.notes: Separate location encoder e() and image encoder f(). The image encoder uses an off-the-shelf backbone plus a projection MLP; the location encoder is primarily Space2Vec grid. The paper states InceptionV3 has 41.8M trainable parameters and the Space2Vec location encoder has 0.4M, for about 42.2M total in that setup. During CSP pretraining, the image backbone is locked and only the projection layer is trained alongside the location encoder.

pretraining.method_details: CSP aligns geo-locations and images using a dual-encoder and three contrastive pair construction strategies: in-batch negative sampling, random negative location sampling, and SimCSE-style dropout-based positive/negative sampling for locations. It freezes a pretrained image encoder and trains the location encoder (plus image projection head) to match image embeddings.
pretraining.objective: contrastive alignment between location embeddings and image embeddings

pretraining_data.num_images: approximately 436K geo-tagged training images for iNat2018 and 363,570 location-image pairs for fMoW

key_contribution: The paper proposes CSP, a self-supervised contrastive spatial pretraining framework that explicitly aligns geo-locations with images using a dual-encoder, enabling geospatial information to be used during pretraining, finetuning, and inference. It shows that learned location representations improve few-shot and fully supervised classification on iNat2018 and fMoW.

title: Changen2: Multi-Temporal Remote Sensing Generative Change Foundation Model

architecture.backbone: RS-DiT (resolution-scalable diffusion transformer), based on DiT-B/2
architecture.type: conditional latent diffusion transformer / encoder-decoder generative model
architecture.notes: Changen2 implements the Generative Probabilistic Change Model (GPCM) with a latent diffusion model. Its noise predictor is RS-DiT, which removes absolute positional embeddings, adds 3x3 depthwise convolutions in FFN blocks, uses mostly local window attention instead of global self-attention, and includes a dense embedding network for dense conditional inputs. It uses a frozen VAE encoder/decoder and masked change diffusion at inference to preserve unchanged regions while generating changed content.

pretraining.method: latent diffusion with self-supervised synthetic change data generation
pretraining.method_details: The paper proposes generative change modeling via GPCM, factorizing change generation into condition-level change event simulation and image-level semantic change synthesis. Changen2 can be trained on labeled single-temporal images, or self-supervised on unlabeled single-temporal images using SAM-derived object contours as editable conditions; it then synthesizes multi-temporal image pairs and change labels to pre-train task-specific change detection models.
pretraining.objective: conditional denoising / masked image generation for semantic change synthesis; downstream synthetic change supervision for change detection

pretraining_data.num_images: about 1.2M unlabeled fMoW crops for self-supervised training; synthetic datasets generated include Changen2-S1-15k, Changen2-S9-27k, and Changen2-S0-1.2M

key_contribution: The paper introduces Changen2, a generative change foundation model that synthesizes multi-temporal remote sensing images and change labels from single-temporal data using a resolution-scalable diffusion transformer and a probabilistic change model. It enables scalable self-supervised generation of synthetic change supervision from unlabeled imagery, leading to strong transferability and zero-shot change detection performance across multiple real-world benchmarks.

title: Cross-Scale MAE: A Tale of Multi-Scale Exploitation in Remote Sensing

architecture.notes: Built on Masked Autoencoder (MAE). The encoder uses a ViT backbone (paper describes ViT-Base with 12 self-attention blocks in the method figure, but experiments state pre-training uses ViT-Large unless otherwise noted). The decoder is a lightweight ViT with 8 self-attention blocks. Uses shared-weight multi-branch processing of scale-augmented views, a projection head for contrastive learning, and an MLP predictor for cross-scale decoder prediction.

pretraining.method_details: Introduces scale augmentation to synthesize multiple scales from a single image without requiring aligned multi-scale imagery, and enforces cross-scale consistency at both encoder and decoder levels. Encoder uses InfoNCE contrastive loss between different-scale views; decoder combines masked reconstruction with cross-scale prediction of decoder features.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction, cross-scale contrastive alignment, and cross-scale decoder feature prediction

datasets: Functional Map of the World (fMoW) RGB training set

key_contribution: The paper proposes Cross-Scale MAE, a self-supervised remote sensing representation learning framework that learns scale-consistent features without requiring aligned multi-scale image pairs. It combines scale augmentation, encoder-level contrastive consistency, and decoder-level cross-scale prediction with masked reconstruction, yielding stronger multi-scale representations than SatMAE and Scale-MAE on classification and segmentation benchmarks.

title: Cross-sensor self-supervised training and alignment for remote sensing

architecture.notes: Primary experiments use a Vision Transformer with DINO-style student-teacher training; also evaluated with ResNet-50 and smaller ViT variants. Uses sensor-specific linear projection heads for the MSAD alignment loss. In continual pretraining, a frozen pretrained domain teacher backbone is used for cross-sensor distillation.

pretraining.method: contrastive self-supervised learning with cross-sensor alignment
pretraining.method_details: X-STARS combines an off-the-shelf SSL objective (DINO in this paper) with a new Multi-Sensor Alignment Dense loss (MSAD). MSAD aligns representations from paired images across sensors using patch-wise contrastive matching and knowledge distillation, with label smoothing to handle imperfect temporal/geographic correspondence and differing resolutions.
pretraining.objective: DINO self-distillation plus patch-wise cross-sensor contrastive alignment of paired acquisitions

pretraining_data.num_images: 4496 triplets in MSC-France; 45,200 pairs in Multi-View Ile-de-France
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: France; MSC-France covers 12 major French cities and suburbs, Multi-View Ile-de-France covers Ile-de-France

key_contribution: The paper proposes X-STARS, a self-supervised cross-sensor pretraining framework for remote sensing that learns sensor-agnostic representations by combining DINO with a new patch-wise cross-sensor alignment loss, MSAD. It also introduces the MSC-France dataset and shows that X-STARS can outperform prior SSL baselines while using substantially less data and can adapt pretrained models to new sensors via continual pretraining.

title: CrossEarth: Geospatial Vision Foundation Model for Domain Generalizable Remote Sensing Semantic Segmentation

architecture.notes: Uses a frozen shared DINOv2 backbone with two training branches: a semantic segmentation branch and a masked image modeling branch. The segmentation branch includes a Geospatial Semantic Extractor (5-layer CNN), cross-attention Injectors, and a Mask2Former decoder. The MIM branch uses an ASPP decoder over multi-stage backbone features. Also includes an Earth-Style Injection augmentation pipeline with a frozen Style Predictor and Style Transfer module.

pretraining.method: multi-task masked image modeling with style-based augmentation
pretraining.method_details: CrossEarth is built on a pretrained DINOv2 backbone and trained for domain generalization using two key ideas: Earth-Style Injection, which injects remote-sensing style embeddings from Million-AID into training images to broaden domain coverage, and Multi-Task Training, which jointly optimizes semantic segmentation and masked image modeling to learn more domain-invariant geospatial features.
pretraining.objective: semantic segmentation plus masked image reconstruction

key_contribution: The paper introduces CrossEarth, the first vision foundation model tailored to remote sensing domain generalization for semantic segmentation. Its main contribution is combining Earth-Style Injection and multi-task segmentation+MIM training to improve cross-domain generalization across region, spectral band, platform, and climate shifts, together with a new 32-scenario RSDG benchmark.

title: CtxMIM: Context-Enhanced Masked Image Modeling for Remote Sensing Image Understanding

architecture.notes: Uses an asymmetric Siamese framework with a reconstructive branch and a context-enhanced generative branch. The encoder is a Swin Transformer shared across both branches, and the decoder is a lightweight MLP prediction head following SimMIM-style reconstruction.

pretraining.method: masked image modeling with context-enhanced Siamese reconstruction
pretraining.method_details: CtxMIM treats original image patches as a reconstructive template and adds a context-enhanced generative branch that provides contextual information during reconstruction. A context consistency loss constrains the reconstructive branch to match predictions from the context-enhanced branch, aiming to mitigate missing contextual information caused by high object density in remote sensing imagery.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction with reconstruction loss, predictive reconstruction loss, and context consistency loss

datasets: custom large-scale unlabeled remote sensing dataset collected using Google Earth Engine

key_contribution: The paper proposes CtxMIM, a context-enhanced masked image modeling framework tailored to remote sensing images, where high object density makes standard MIM less effective. By adding a Siamese context-enhanced generative branch and a context consistency constraint, the method learns more transferable object- and pixel-level representations and achieves state-of-the-art results across classification, segmentation, detection, and instance segmentation.

title: Extending global-local view alignment for self-supervised learning with remote sensing imagery

architecture.notes: Built on DINO with student and teacher networks of the same backbone updated via EMA. The paper evaluates ViT-small, Swin-tiny, ResNet-50, and WRN-50-2; the primary/largest reported variant is WRN-50-2. DINO-MC modifies DINO by using multi-sized local crops instead of fixed-size local crops, with global views for the teacher and both global/local views for the student.

pretraining.method: DINO-style self-distillation with global-local view alignment
pretraining.method_details: The proposed DINO-MC extends DINO by introducing multi-sized local crops to better reflect limited object-scale variation in remote sensing imagery, plus different color transformations for global and local crops. The paper also studies DINO-TP using temporal positives, but DINO-MC is the main proposed method.
pretraining.objective: alignment of global and local view representations via self-distillation / knowledge distillation

key_contribution: The paper extends DINO for remote sensing self-supervised learning by proposing DINO-MC, which replaces fixed-size local crops with multi-sized local crops to create a more effective global-local alignment pretext task. Pretrained on only SeCo-100K, DINO-MC achieves competitive or state-of-the-art results on EuroSAT, BigEarthNet-S2, and OSCD while using less data and compute than prior SSL remote sensing methods.

title: Neural Plasticity-Inspired Multimodal Foundation Model for Earth Observation

architecture.notes: Unified Transformer with a wavelength-conditioned dynamic hypernetwork that generates modality-specific patch embedding and decoder weights from band central wavelengths. Uses a shared ViT backbone across modalities and a dynamic decoder for masked image modeling. Primary reported variants are ViT-Base and ViT-Large; largest/primary variant is ViT-Large. DOFA+ initializes from DINOv2 and adds hierarchical feature distillation.

pretraining.method: masked image modeling with continual pretraining and knowledge distillation
pretraining.method_details: Novelty lies in wavelength-conditioned dynamic weight generation: a hypernetwork maps spectral-band wavelengths to patch embedding/decoder weights, allowing one model to flexibly handle varying numbers of bands and unseen sensors. Continual pretraining uses proxy RGB-based distillation from an ImageNet/DINOv2 teacher; DOFA+ combines MIM with multi-layer hierarchical distillation from DINOv2 for efficient EO adaptation.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction plus feature distillation/alignment

pretraining_data.num_images: 11.5M for DOFA; 410K for DOFA+ compact pretraining

key_contribution: The paper proposes DOFA, a unified multimodal EO foundation model that uses a wavelength-conditioned dynamic hypernetwork to adapt a single Transformer to heterogeneous sensors, spectral bands, and even unseen modalities. It also introduces DOFA+, a more compute-efficient variant that combines masked image modeling with hierarchical DINOv2 distillation, achieving strong cross-modal transfer and state-of-the-art results on diverse EO benchmarks.

title: Decoupling Common and Unique Representations for Multimodal Self-supervised Learning

architecture.type: dual-encoder with modality-specific encoders and projectors
architecture.notes: Primary setup uses separate modality-specific ResNet-50 encoders plus 3-layer MLP projectors with output dimension 8192. Embedding dimensions are split into common and unique parts for cross-modal learning. Optional residual deformable attention is added to the last two stages of ConvNet backbones. Additional experiments also use MiT-B2/B5 and ViT-S/16.

pretraining.method: redundancy reduction / Barlow Twins-style multimodal self-supervised learning
pretraining.method_details: DeCUR extends Barlow Twins to multimodal learning by explicitly decoupling embedding dimensions into cross-modal common and modality-unique subspaces. It aligns common dimensions across modalities, decorrelates unique dimensions across modalities, and adds intra-modal redundancy reduction on full embeddings to prevent collapse and strengthen modality-specific learning.
pretraining.objective: cross-modal common representation alignment, cross-modal unique representation decorrelation, and intra-modal redundancy reduction between augmented views

pretraining_data.num_images: 251k image pairs (SSL4EO-S12), 111k patches from 6,942 training scenes (GeoNRW), 10,335 RGB-D pairs (SUN-RGBD)
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: 10 m for SSL4EO-S12, 1 m for GeoNRW, mixed for SUN-RGBD
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global for SSL4EO-S12; North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany for GeoNRW; indoor scenes for SUN-RGBD

key_contribution: The paper proposes DeCUR, a multimodal self-supervised learning method that explicitly separates shared cross-modal information from modality-specific information in the embedding space. By combining cross-modal decoupling with intra-modal redundancy reduction, it improves multimodal and missing-modality transfer performance across SAR-optical, RGB-DEM, and RGB-depth settings.

title: DeepAndes: A Self-Supervised Vision Foundation Model for Multi-Spectral Remote Sensing Imagery of the Andes

architecture.notes: DINOv2-based vision transformer adapted for 8-band multispectral imagery; student-teacher networks share ViT architecture with EMA teacher updates; input patch embedding modified to accept 8 channels via 2D convolution; 24 transformer layers, hidden size 1024, patch size 14, outputs 1 CLS token + 256 patch tokens.

pretraining.method_details: Customized DINOv2 self-supervised pipeline for 8-band WorldView multispectral imagery, including adapted preprocessing/augmentation for multi-channel input and modified global-local crop scales ((0.2, 0.5) local and (0.5, 1) global) to better match sparse archaeological and architectural features.
pretraining.objective: self-distillation contrastive image-level alignment via DINO loss plus masked patch-level prediction via iBOT loss

pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Central Andes / Peruvian Andes, including western Peru and parts of northern Chile and northwestern Bolivia

key_contribution: The paper introduces DeepAndes, the first vision foundation model specifically designed for 8-band multispectral remote sensing imagery in the Andes, pretrained self-supervised on 3 million WorldView image patches. By adapting DINOv2 to multispectral archaeological remote sensing, it shows strong few-shot gains on imbalanced classification, image retrieval, and semantic segmentation.

title: DiffusionSat: A Generative Foundation Model for Satellite Imagery

architecture.backbone: Stable Diffusion 2.1 latent diffusion U-Net with CLIP text encoder and VAE; extended with a 3D ControlNet for conditional tasks
architecture.notes: Initialized from Stable Diffusion 2.1. Uses SD VAE encoder/decoder and CLIP text encoder. For single-image generation, only the denoising U-Net and metadata/timestep embeddings are updated. Numerical metadata (latitude, longitude, GSD, cloud cover, year, month, day) is embedded via sinusoidal projection plus per-field MLPs and combined with timestep embeddings. For downstream conditional generation, adds a novel 3D ControlNet with 3D zero convolutions, temporal transformer/attention layers, and per-image metadata conditioning for temporal sequences, super-resolution, and inpainting.

pretraining.method_details: Pretrains a latent diffusion model for satellite image generation conditioned on text and freely available numerical metadata rather than relying only on captions. The key novelty is metadata-aware conditioning and a subsequent 3D conditioning extension for inverse problems such as temporal prediction, multispectral super-resolution, and inpainting.
pretraining.objective: denoising diffusion noise prediction for conditional image generation

pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: mixed (about 0.3m-1.5m for fMoW; Sentinel-2 10m-60m also used in the broader framework)

key_contribution: The paper introduces DiffusionSat, the first large-scale generative foundation model for satellite imagery built on latent diffusion and conditioned on both text and numerical satellite metadata. It also proposes a novel 3D ControlNet-style conditioning module that extends the pretrained model to remote-sensing inverse problems including temporal prediction, multispectral super-resolution, and disaster inpainting.

title: EarthDial: Turning Multi-sensory Earth Observations to Interactive Dialogues

architecture.type: vision-language multimodal LLM with vision encoder + MLP projector + autoregressive decoder LLM
architecture.notes: Built on InternVL with modifications for Earth observation. Uses InternViT-300M as visual encoder, Phi-3-mini as LLM, and an MLP projector. Includes Adaptive High Resolution tiling for multi-resolution imagery and a Data Fusion module for multispectral, SAR, RGBI, hyperspectral, and multi-temporal inputs. Three-stage training progressively adds RGB, temporal, multispectral, and SAR capabilities.

pretraining.method: autoregressive image-text instruction tuning / conversational pretraining
pretraining.method_details: Stage 1 aligns remote sensing imagery and text by training the vision encoder, MLP projector, and LLM to generate text autoregressively from EO image-text pairs. Novelty lies in adapting a VLM to multi-resolution, multi-sensor EO data and later extending it through staged finetuning to temporal, multispectral, and SAR modalities with a dedicated data fusion module.
pretraining.objective: autoregressive text generation from image-text instruction pairs

pretraining_data.num_images: 7.6M image-text pairs for Stage 1 pretraining; 11.11M instruction pairs overall in EarthDial-Instruct

key_contribution: EarthDial is a domain-specific conversational vision-language model for Earth observation that unifies multi-resolution, multi-spectral, SAR, and multi-temporal remote sensing understanding in a single assistant. The paper also introduces EarthDial-Instruct, a 11.11M-pair instruction-tuning dataset, and shows strong generalization across 44 downstream EO tasks and datasets.

title: EarthGPT: A Universal Multi-modal Large Language Model for Multi-sensor Image Comprehension in Remote Sensing Domain

architecture.type: instruction-tuned multimodal LLM with hybrid visual encoder and autoregressive language model
architecture.notes: Uses a hybrid visual-enhanced perception module combining a frozen ViT encoder for multi-layer global features and a frozen CNN encoder for multi-scale local features; concatenated visual tokens are projected and fused with language tokens. The language model is LLaMA-2. During cross-modal mutual comprehension, self-attention and RMSNorm layers are unfrozen for alignment; during RS instruction tuning, linear layers are adapted with bias and shift parameters inspired by LLaMA-Adapter V2.

pretraining.method_details: EarthGPT first performs cross-modal mutual comprehension by concatenating projected visual tokens with text tokens and partially unfreezing LLaMA-2 self-attention and RMSNorm layers for vision-language alignment on natural image-text datasets. It then performs RS-domain unified instruction tuning using bias/shift adaptation of linear layers on the MMRS-1M multi-sensor remote sensing instruction-following dataset.
pretraining.objective: autoregressive next-token prediction for image-text alignment and instruction-following response generation

key_contribution: The paper proposes EarthGPT, a universal remote sensing multimodal large language model that unifies multi-sensor image understanding and diverse RS tasks within a single instruction-following framework. It also introduces MMRS-1M, a large-scale multi-sensor remote sensing instruction dataset with over 1M image-text pairs spanning optical, SAR, and infrared imagery.

title: EarthPT: a time series foundation model for Earth Observation

architecture.notes: Primary model is EarthPT-700M with 36 layers, 20 heads, embedding dimension 1280, block size 256. Based on the GPT/Radford et al. decoding transformer. Uses an MLP instead of word embeddings to embed multispectral time-series tokens, and includes date embeddings for the current and next observation.

pretraining.method_details: The model is pretrained on Earth observation pixel time series, treating each temporal observation as a token and predicting the next multispectral observation. It is designed as a large observation model for EO, using multispectral reflectance values plus seasonal/date embeddings.
pretraining.objective: next-observation prediction of pixel-level surface reflectance time series

pretraining_data.num_images: 14B tokens used for EarthPT-700M training; dataset described as just over 100 million individual pixels
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: UK-only (100 x 100 km region in the TL square of the British National Grid for the experiments)

key_contribution: The paper introduces EarthPT, a 700M-parameter autoregressive transformer foundation model for Earth observation time series, pretrained self-supervised on large-scale multispectral pixel sequences. It shows strong long-horizon forecasting of future surface reflectance and vegetation indices, while also learning semantically meaningful embeddings that could support downstream tasks such as dynamic land use classification.

title: EarthView: A Large Scale Remote Sensing Dataset for Self-Supervision

architecture.notes: Masked Autoencoder with asymmetric ViT encoder-decoder. Uses distinct tokenizers for each source/modalities, resizes all inputs to 224x224, and adds positional, source, and temporal encodings. Temporal encoding is built from year/month/day/hour embeddings; source embeddings distinguish modalities such as multispectral, RGB, hyperspectral, etc.

pretraining.method_details: EarthMAE extends MAE/SpectralMAE-style pretraining to heterogeneous remote sensing data by combining multiple sources, modalities, and timestamps. It introduces source-specific tokenizers, temporal/source encodings, and explores masking strategies, with tube masking across time and sources performing best.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction with MSE on masked patches

pretraining_data.num_images: about 15 terapixels total; Satellogic: 2,967,663 patches / 6,165,992 images including revisits; Sentinel: over 1M unique regions; NEON: 35,501 patches
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global for Satellogic and Sentinel; US forests for NEON

key_contribution: The paper introduces EarthView, a large-scale multimodal remote sensing dataset for self-supervised learning that combines Satellogic, Sentinel, and NEON data across space, time, and sensing modalities. It also proposes EarthMAE, a masked autoencoder adapted to heterogeneous Earth observation data with source-aware tokenization and temporal/source encodings, showing that pretraining with high-resolution Satellogic imagery improves downstream performance.

title: Embedding Earth: Self-supervised contrastive pre-training for dense land cover classification

architecture.notes: Uses UNet3Df, a variation of UNet3D, as the dense feature extractor backbone. During pretraining, a projection head with two 1x1 convolution layers and ReLU is added. The method uses a momentum contrast setup with separate query and key encoders and a queue of key embeddings.

pretraining.method_details: Dense self-supervised instance discrimination at the pixel level for satellite image time series. Positive pairs are pixels corresponding to the same geographic location across two augmented spatiotemporal views; negatives include other pixels from the same sample and a momentum-encoder queue. The method explicitly tracks augmentations to compute pixel correspondences and extends dense contrastive learning to image time series.
pretraining.objective: pixel-level contrastive alignment of corresponding locations across augmented spatiotemporal views

pretraining_data.num_images: about 12k-352k samples depending on dataset; largest mentioned is 351,947 samples

downstream_datasets: France crop type dataset from RPG
Germany crop type dataset from Rußwurm and Körner
Ghana dataset from Radiant Earth Foundation
South Sudan dataset from Radiant Earth Foundation
key_contribution: The paper proposes Embedding Earth, a self-supervised dense contrastive pretraining method for satellite image time series that learns pixel-level representations by matching corresponding locations across space-time augmentations. It significantly improves downstream land cover semantic segmentation over random initialization and supervised pretraining, with gains up to 25% absolute mIoU and good transfer across regions.

title: FLAVARS: A Multimodal Foundational Language and Vision Alignment Model for Remote Sensing

architecture.type: encoder-decoder multimodal encoder with contrastive alignment and masked modeling
architecture.notes: Built on the FLAVA framework with image and text encoders, masked-image modeling, masked-language modeling, multimodal image-text matching, global image-text contrastive loss, and an additional image-location contrastive alignment loss. The location encoder is initialized from SatCLIP weights. For segmentation evaluation, the pretrained vision encoder is paired with a UperNet decoder.

pretraining.method: multi-task masked modeling + contrastive vision-language-location pretraining
pretraining.method_details: FLAVARS adapts FLAVA to remote sensing by combining masked-image modeling, masked-language modeling, image-text matching, and global image-text contrastive learning, while adding geospatial awareness through a contrastive image-location alignment objective. This is designed to improve dense visual representation quality without losing zero-shot vision-language capability.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction, masked language modeling, image-text matching, image-text contrastive alignment, and image-location contrastive alignment

key_contribution: The paper proposes FLAVARS, a remote sensing multimodal pretraining framework that extends FLAVA with geospatial image-location contrastive alignment. It shows improved vision-only representations and dense prediction performance over SkyCLIP while retaining zero-shot vision-language capability.

title: Falcon: A Remote Sensing Vision-Language Foundation Model

architecture.backbone: Transformer-based encoder-decoder with an image encoder
architecture.notes: Unified sequence-to-sequence architecture for image-, region-, and pixel-level tasks. Combines an image encoder with a multimodality transformer encoder-decoder and a visual adapter. Initialized from Florence-2 pretrained weights; output token length increased to 4096.

pretraining.method_details: Falcon is trained in a unified prompt-based seq2seq manner over 14 remote sensing tasks using the Falcon SFT dataset. A key novelty is dynamic prompt training, where multiple differently phrased instructions with the same semantics are sampled from a prompt pool to improve instruction understanding and reduce reliance on task-specific tokens.
pretraining.objective: instruction-conditioned sequence generation with cross-entropy over textual outputs encoding labels, captions, boxes, polygons, and change masks

key_contribution: The paper introduces Falcon, a unified remote sensing vision-language foundation model that handles 14 tasks spanning image, region, and pixel levels with only 0.7B parameters. It also contributes Falcon SFT, a large-scale 78M-sample multi-task instruction-tuning dataset built from 67 remote sensing datasets to enable broad instruction-following and strong zero-shot performance.

title: Feature Guided Masked Autoencoder for Self-supervised Learning in Remote Sensing

architecture.notes: Based on MAE with an asymmetric ViT encoder and lightweight ViT decoder. Default model is ViT-S/16 for experiments; scalability is demonstrated up to ViT-H/14 with 658M parameters. FGMAE-MS uses two parallel decoder heads to reconstruct HOG and NDI; FGMAE-SAR reconstructs HOG only.

pretraining.method: MAE-style masked image modeling with feature reconstruction
pretraining.method_details: Instead of reconstructing raw pixels like MAE, FG-MAE reconstructs remote sensing features as targets. For multispectral imagery it reconstructs a combination of HOG and NDI, while for SAR imagery it reconstructs HOG to reduce sensitivity to speckle noise and encourage more semantic representations.
pretraining.objective: masked feature reconstruction

key_contribution: The paper proposes FG-MAE, a simple MAE variant that replaces pixel reconstruction with remote sensing feature reconstruction, using HOG+NDI for multispectral imagery and HOG for SAR. It shows that feature-guided masking improves transfer performance across classification and segmentation tasks, with especially strong gains for SAR, and scales to large ViTs up to 0.7B parameters.

title: FlexiMo: A Flexible Remote Sensing Foundation Model

architecture.notes: Built on top of the DOFA ViT-Base pretrained model. Adds a lightweight Transformer-based dynamic weight generator for wavelength-guided channel adaptation and a plug-and-play parameter-free spatial resolution-aware module (PI-Resize) to adapt patch embeddings to arbitrary image sizes, resolutions, and patch sizes. Uses a classification head for image-level tasks and UperNet for pixel-level tasks.

pretraining.method: foundation model adaptation / fine-tuning of pretrained DOFA
pretraining.method_details: FlexiMo itself is presented primarily as a flexible adaptation framework over pretrained DOFA weights rather than a new standalone pretraining regime. Its novelty lies in combining wavelength-guided dynamic channel adaptation with a parameter-free spatial resolution-aware patch embedding alignment module so the pretrained model can handle arbitrary channel counts, spatial resolutions, and patch sizes.
pretraining.objective: not a new pretraining objective; adapts a pretrained remote sensing foundation model for downstream tasks

key_contribution: The paper proposes FlexiMo, a flexible remote sensing foundation model adaptation framework that enables pretrained ViT-based RSFMs to handle arbitrary spatial resolutions, image sizes, patch sizes, and channel configurations. Its main contribution is the combination of a parameter-free spatial resolution-aware PI-Resize module and a wavelength-guided dynamic channel adaptation mechanism, yielding strong generalization across RGB, multispectral, and SAR downstream tasks.

title: FoMo: Multi-Modal, Multi-Scale and Multi-Task Remote Sensing Foundation Models for Forest Monitoring

architecture.notes: Primary model is FoMo-Net1, which uses a single shared linear projection for all spectral bands. Backbone uses a ViT encoder with 12 layers and 12 attention heads, trained with an MAE-style decoder for reconstruction. Spectral bands are tokenized independently with trainable spectral and positional embeddings; maximum of 4 sampled spectral bands during pretraining; input resolution 64x64. FoMo-Netm is an alternative variant with separate per-band projections, but FoMo-Net1 is the better-performing primary variant.

pretraining.method_details: FoMo-Net proposes a sensor-agnostic masked autoencoding framework that can process arbitrary combinations of remote sensing modalities/bands, including unaligned paired and unpaired data. Its novelty is random dataset and spectral-band sampling during training, treating each spectral band as a modality, plus trainable spectral and positional embeddings and gradient accumulation to stabilize optimization over heterogeneous batches.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction over randomly sampled spectral bands/modalities

key_contribution: The paper introduces FoMo-Bench, the first unified global benchmark for forest monitoring spanning diverse remote sensing modalities, scales, and tasks, and adds TalloS, a new global multimodal tree-genus classification dataset. It also proposes FoMo-Net, a flexible self-supervised pretraining framework that learns a single sensor-agnostic backbone capable of handling arbitrary combinations of common remote sensing bands and resolutions.

title: FUS-MAE: A CROSS-ATTENTION-BASED DATA FUSION APPROACH FOR MASKED AUTOENCODERS IN REMOTE SENSING

architecture.notes: MAE-based multimodal transformer for SAR-optical fusion. Uses a ViT encoder with a cross-attended patch projection module (XAttnEncoder) for early fusion, and lightweight modality-specific decoders with optional cross-attention in decoding (XAttnDecoder) for feature-level fusion. Two variants are studied: Fus-MAE XAE (early fusion in encoder) and Fus-MAE XAD (feature-level fusion in decoder); the paper indicates XAE as the stronger primary variant.

pretraining.method_details: A self-supervised masked image modeling framework for multimodal SAR-optical fusion that introduces cross-attention between modality-specific token streams to better model the large domain gap between SAR and multispectral optical imagery. It studies both early fusion via cross-attended patch projection and feature-level fusion via cross-attention in the decoder, with independent and consistent masking strategies.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction

key_contribution: The paper proposes Fus-MAE, a multimodal masked autoencoder for SAR-optical remote sensing that uses cross-attention to fuse modality-specific token streams, especially at an early stage through cross-attended patch projection. It shows that this early-fusion MAE strategy can outperform prior masked-autoencoding approaches and compete with specialized contrastive SAR-optical fusion methods on BigEarthNet-MM and SEN12MS.

title: GAIR: Improving Multimodal Geo-Foundation Model with Geo-Aligned Implicit Representations

architecture.type: multimodal contrastive tri-encoder with implicit neural representation module
architecture.notes: Uses three factorized encoders: a ViT-based remote sensing image encoder, a ViT-based street view image encoder, and a geolocation encoder based on Random Fourier Features from GeoCLIP. Adds a linear projection head for image encoders. Includes a novel INR module over RS patch features with feature unfolding (3x3 neighborhood concatenation) and local ensemble interpolation via a shared single-layer perceptron to extract geolocated RS embeddings.

pretraining.method: contrastive multi-object pretraining with INR-based geo-alignment
pretraining.method_details: GAIR aligns three modalities—remote sensing imagery, street view imagery, and geolocation—by first converting RS patch features into a continuous spatial representation using an implicit neural representation module, then querying a localized RS embedding at the exact street-view geolocation. Training combines Implicit Neural Contrastive Learning between localized RS and street-view embeddings with Spatially Explicit Contrastive Learning that also aligns both with location embeddings via a memory bank.
pretraining.objective: contrastive alignment of geolocated remote sensing, street view, and location embeddings

key_contribution: The paper proposes GAIR, a multimodal geo-foundation model that jointly learns from overhead remote sensing imagery, street-view imagery, and geolocation metadata. Its main novelty is an INR-based geo-alignment mechanism that extracts location-specific RS embeddings for contrastive training, enabling strong transfer across 10 geospatial tasks spanning image, remote sensing, and location benchmarks.

title: Galileo: Learning Global & Local Features of Many Remote Sensing Modalities

architecture.notes: Primary model is Galileo-ViT-Base with 12 transformer blocks, 768 hidden dimension, and 12 heads. Uses a ViT-based online encoder, an EMA target encoder, and a predictor transformer with cross-attention. Supports flexible tokenization over space, time, and channel groups, with sinusoidal spatial/temporal positional embeddings, learnable channel embeddings, and month embeddings. Designed for multimodal inputs including image time series, pixel time series, and single-timestep imagery.

pretraining.method: masked latent modeling with dual contrastive objectives
pretraining.method_details: Galileo introduces a dual global-local self-supervised pretraining scheme. The global objective uses structured space-time masking and deep EMA-encoder targets to learn global features, while the local objective uses unstructured masking and shallow linear-projection targets to learn local features. Both objectives use token-level contrastive losses, with the global branch using a batch-aware PatchDiscB loss and the local branch using PatchDisc. The novelty is jointly learning representations at different latent depths and spatial-temporal scales in a single multimodal model.
pretraining.objective: masked token prediction in latent space with contrastive alignment of predicted and target tokens

key_contribution: The paper proposes Galileo, a single highly multimodal remote sensing foundation model that flexibly handles many modalities, spatial-temporal input shapes, and scales. Its main innovation is a dual self-supervised global-local masked latent modeling objective that learns both global semantic structure and fine local detail, leading to state-of-the-art performance across 11 remote sensing benchmarks.

title: Generic Knowledge Boosted Pre-training For Remote Sensing Images

architecture.type: teacher-student dual-network pretraining framework with supervised and self-supervised branches
architecture.notes: Uses a student network and an EMA-updated teacher network. Remote sensing contrastive learning (RSCL) is performed with MoCo-style contrastive learning, dynamic queue, and InfoNCE loss; natural image auxiliary learning (NIAL) is supervised on labeled natural images using the student only. Features are pooled with GAP; RSCL uses a 2-layer MLP projector (2048 hidden, 128 output), and NIAL uses a linear predictor/classifier.

pretraining.method_details: GeRSP jointly learns from unlabeled remote sensing images and labeled natural images in a unified teacher-student framework. It combines RS-domain knowledge from self-supervised contrastive learning with generic visual knowledge from supervised ImageNet training, rather than sequentially pretraining and risking forgetting.
pretraining.objective: InfoNCE contrastive alignment on remote sensing image augmentations plus cross-entropy classification on natural images

pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: mixed; Million-AID ranges from 0.5 to 153 m per pixel

key_contribution: The paper proposes GeRSP, a unified remote sensing pretraining framework that combines supervised learning on natural images with self-supervised contrastive learning on remote sensing images using a teacher-student design. This enables simultaneous acquisition of generic visual knowledge and remote sensing-specific knowledge, improving transfer on scene classification, object detection, and semantic segmentation.

title: GeoChat: Grounded Large Vision-Language Model for Remote Sensing

architecture.type: instruction-tuned vision-language model with image encoder + MLP projector + LLM; grounded conversational VLM
architecture.notes: Built on LLaVA-v1.5. Uses frozen CLIP-ViT(L-14) visual encoder and frozen 2-layer MLP adaptor, with Vicuna-v1.5-7B as the LLM. Positional embeddings are interpolated to support 504x504 inputs (1296 patches instead of 576 at 336x336). LoRA is applied to the LLM. Adds task-specific tokens ([grounding], [identify], [refer]) and textual spatial box representations for region input/output grounding.

pretraining.method: vision-language instruction tuning / domain adaptation via LoRA fine-tuning
pretraining.method_details: GeoChat does not pretrain from scratch; it adapts pretrained LLaVA-v1.5 to remote sensing using a newly constructed 318k remote-sensing multimodal instruction-following dataset. The novelty lies in unifying image-level conversation, region-level reasoning, and visual grounding through task tokens and textual spatial coordinates.
pretraining.objective: multitask instruction following, conversational response generation, region-aware reasoning, and grounded text generation

key_contribution: The paper introduces GeoChat, the first remote sensing vision-language model that supports unified multitask conversations with both image-level and region-level reasoning, including visual grounding via spatial coordinates. It also creates a new 318k-sample remote-sensing multimodal instruction-following dataset and benchmark to adapt LLaVA-style models to remote sensing.

title: DOFA-CLIP: Multimodal Vision–Language Foundation Models for Earth Observation

architecture.backbone: SoViT-400m (large primary variant), dynamic DOFA ViT
architecture.notes: Uses a single unified wavelength-aware dynamic vision transformer adapted from DOFA, with dynamic patch embedding generated from input channel wavelengths. Base variant uses ViT-Base/16; large variant uses SoViT-400m with patch size 14 and input size 384. Includes a Modality-aware Knowledge Agglomeration (MaKA) module for modality-conditioned feature distillation. Vision encoder initialized from SigLIP weights; paired with a CLIP text encoder.

pretraining.method: CLIP/SigLIP-style contrastive vision-language pretraining with multi-teacher distillation
pretraining.method_details: Introduces VECT (Vision-models Enhanced Contrastive Text-image pretraining), which combines pairwise sigmoid contrastive loss with feature-matching distillation from multiple frozen vision foundation models (SigLIP, DINOv2, ImageNet ViT) to improve spatial awareness. Also introduces MaKA for modality-aware distillation and progressive weight merging across RGB and non-RGB modality subsets.
pretraining.objective: image-text contrastive alignment with pairwise sigmoid loss plus intermediate feature-matching distillation

key_contribution: The paper proposes DOFA-CLIP, a unified multimodal EO vision-language foundation model that handles heterogeneous modalities and varying spectral bands with a single wavelength-aware transformer. It also contributes the GeoLangBind-2M dataset, the VECT multi-teacher distillation strategy for spatially aware CLIP features, and the MaKA module for modality-aware knowledge transfer, achieving strong zero-shot performance across seen and unseen EO modalities.

title: GeoViT: A Versatile Vision Transformer Architecture for Geospatial Image Analysis

architecture.type: encoder with task-specific heads; multimodal multi-task model
architecture.notes: GeoViT uses a ViT-based backbone that patchifies inputs, linearly projects patches with positional embeddings, and processes them with multi-head self-attention and feed-forward layers. The CO2 variant uses Sentinel-2 imagery plus a weather vector and adds a SegFormer-based segmentation head with additional fully connected classification and regression heads. The NO2 variant uses Sentinel-2 and Sentinel-5P inputs with a cross-attention fusion mechanism and a regression head for scalar NO2 concentration prediction.

pretraining.method_details: The paper explicitly states that for the NO2 estimation task they do not pre-train on BigEarthNet and compare against a non-pretrained baseline. No dedicated pretraining strategy for GeoViT is proposed.

key_contribution: The paper proposes GeoViT, a compact ViT-based geospatial model for greenhouse gas monitoring from satellite imagery, with variants for multimodal CO2 analysis and NO2 regression. It shows improved performance over prior CNN-based approaches while using substantially smaller model sizes, aided by transformer-based global context modeling and cross-attention fusion for Sentinel-2/Sentinel-5P data.

title: Global and Local Contrastive Self-Supervised Learning for Semantic Segmentation of HR Remote Sensing Images

architecture.notes: Built on DeepLabV3+ for semantic segmentation. The encoder is pretrained with a global style contrastive module, and the full encoder-decoder is additionally trained with a local matching contrastive module. Uses MLP projection heads for global and local contrastive learning.

pretraining.method_details: Proposes Global style and Local matching Contrastive learning Network (GLCNet), combining a global style contrastive module using channel-wise mean and variance as style features instead of only global average pooled features, and a local matching contrastive module that aligns corresponding local regions across augmented views to learn pixel/local-region-discriminative representations for semantic segmentation.
pretraining.objective: global image-level contrastive alignment plus local region matching contrastive alignment

pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Germany (Potsdam) and China (Hubei, Xiangtan); DGLC geographic coverage unclear

key_contribution: The paper introduces GLCNet, a self-supervised contrastive pretraining framework tailored for remote sensing semantic segmentation by jointly learning global style-aware and local region-matching representations. It shows that this design improves segmentation performance under limited labels and often surpasses ImageNet pretraining and prior self-supervised baselines.

title: HyperFree: A Channel-adaptive and Tuning-free Foundation Model for Hyperspectral Remote Sensing Imagery

architecture.type: promptable encoder-decoder segmentation model based on SAM
architecture.notes: Built on the meta-architecture of Segment Anything Model (SAM) with two custom components: (1) a channel-adaptive embedding layer using a learnable full-spectrum wavelength weight dictionary to dynamically construct the embedding layer for varying hyperspectral channels, and (2) prompt-mask-feature (PMF) interaction for task-specific tuning-free inference. Uses SAM-style prompt encoder and mask decoder.

pretraining.method_details: Instead of MAE or contrastive pretraining, HyperFree is trained in a SAM-like prompt engineering paradigm on a large annotated hyperspectral segmentation dataset. During training, random subsets of channels from the full spectrum are selected so the learned wavelength dictionary becomes wavelength-aware and can generalize to unseen channel configurations. Point prompts are randomly sampled and the model is trained to segment corresponding masks.
pretraining.objective: prompt-conditioned mask segmentation with focal loss and Dice loss

key_contribution: The paper introduces HyperFree, a tuning-free hyperspectral foundation model that can directly handle unseen hyperspectral images with varying channel numbers using a learned full-spectrum wavelength dictionary and SAM-style promptable segmentation. It also proposes PMF interaction to turn one prompt into multiple semantic-aware masks across several hyperspectral tasks, supported by a large new annotated training dataset, Hyper-Seg.

title: Multi-Modal Multi-Objective Contrastive Learning for Sentinel-1/2 Imagery

architecture.notes: Uses separate ResNet-18 encoders for Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2. Includes four projection heads total: inter-modality heads and intra-modality heads for each modality. Encoder features are 512-D and projected to 128-D latent space.

pretraining.method_details: Proposes Intra-and Inter-modality SimCLR (IaI-SimCLR), combining inter-modality contrastive alignment between co-located Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 patches with intra-modality contrastive learning on augmented views for each modality. Also introduces local batch sampling using geocoded proximity to create harder negatives.
pretraining.objective: contrastive alignment of co-located cross-modal pairs plus intra-modal augmentation-invariant representation learning

key_contribution: The paper introduces IaI-SimCLR, a multi-modal multi-objective contrastive learning method for Sentinel-1/2 imagery that preserves both inter-modality and intra-modality information during self-supervised pretraining. It also proposes local batch sampling based on geographic proximity to create harder negatives and shows benefits across multiple remote sensing downstream tasks.

title: IN-DOMAIN REPRESENTATION LEARNING FOR REMOTE SENSING

architecture.notes: Single-backbone CNN used for all experiments. Representations are obtained by supervised upstream training/fine-tuning on one remote sensing dataset and then transferred to other remote sensing datasets. Input preprocessing uses resize or crop-and-rotate augmentation.

pretraining.method_details: The paper studies generic remote sensing representations learned by supervised training on one remote sensing dataset and transferring them to other remote sensing tasks, comparing against ImageNet pretraining and training from scratch. It is not a novel SSL/VLM foundation model, but a benchmark and transfer-learning study establishing in-domain baselines.
pretraining.objective: image classification / multi-label classification supervision on upstream remote sensing datasets

pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: mixed (0.3 m to 60 m+ depending on dataset/band)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: mixed/global and regional; includes Europe, multiple continents, and US-only subsets depending on dataset

key_contribution: The paper establishes a standardized benchmark for remote sensing representation learning using five diverse datasets and a common evaluation protocol. It shows that supervised in-domain pretraining generally transfers better than ImageNet pretraining, especially in low-label regimes, and releases standardized datasets plus trained representations via TensorFlow Datasets and TensorFlow Hub.

title: LHRS-Bot: Empowering Remote Sensing with VGI-Enhanced Large Multimodal Language Model

architecture.type: vision-language multimodal large language model with vision encoder + perceiver + LLM
architecture.notes: Uses a frozen pre-trained CLIP ViT-L/14 vision encoder (224x224 input), a trainable vision perceiver with stacked cross-attention and MLP layers, and LLaMA2-7B as the language model. It retains multi-level hidden states from the vision encoder (layers L/3, 2L/3, L-1) and summarizes them with learnable queries using a descending query allocation strategy [64, 48, 32]. LoRA is applied to the LLM during later training stages.

pretraining.method: multi-stage vision-language alignment and instruction tuning
pretraining.method_details: Three-stage curriculum learning: (1) caption-generation pretraining on LHRS-Align to align remote sensing images and language using image-caption pairs; (2) multi-task pretraining with instruction-formatted RS datasets for classification, VQA, visual grounding, and captioning, with LoRA on the LLM; (3) supervised fine-tuning on RS-specific instruction-following and reasoning data plus sampled LLaVA reasoning data. Novelty lies in VGI-enhanced data creation and a multi-level vision-language alignment strategy that summarizes features from multiple encoder depths.
pretraining.objective: autoregressive next-token prediction for caption generation and instruction-following conditioned on visual tokens

pretraining_data.num_images: LHRS-Align: 1.15M image-text pairs; LHRS-Instruct includes ~39.8K samples; multi-task instruction dataset: 42,322 samples; plus 20K sampled from LLaVA complex reasoning

key_contribution: The paper introduces LHRS-Bot, a remote sensing multimodal LLM trained with a novel multi-level vision-language alignment strategy and curriculum learning. It also contributes large-scale RS-specific resources—LHRS-Align, LHRS-Instruct, and LHRS-Bench—built using global Google Earth imagery and OpenStreetMap VGI to improve RS image understanding and reasoning.

title: Masked Angle-Aware Autoencoder for Remote Sensing Images

architecture.notes: Asymmetric MAE-style architecture. Uses a plain ViT-B encoder and an 8-block ViT decoder with 512-dimensional embeddings. Adds learnable angle embeddings to rotated-crop patches; separately masks rotated crop and background patches.

pretraining.method: MAE-style masked image modeling with angle-aware reconstruction and optimal transport
pretraining.method_details: MA3E introduces explicit angle variation by inserting a randomly rotated crop created via a scaling center crop operation into the input image, then reconstructs the original image. It adds angle embeddings for rotated regions and uses an optimal transport loss to match rotated-crop predictions with similar original patches, encouraging angle perception and rotation-invariant representation learning.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction plus angle restoration of rotated crops

key_contribution: The paper proposes MA3E, a masked autoencoder tailored for remote sensing that explicitly models object orientation during self-supervised pretraining. By reconstructing original images from inputs containing randomly rotated crops and using an optimal transport loss for rotated-region reconstruction, it learns stronger rotation-invariant representations and improves performance across classification, rotated detection, and segmentation benchmarks.

title: Towards Knowledge Guided Pretraining Approaches for Multimodal Foundation Models: Applications in Remote Sensing

architecture.notes: Heavy encoder and lightweight decoder. A shared Vision Transformer encodes multi-temporal Sentinel-2 image patches; a sequence-to-sequence unidirectional LSTM encodes weather time series; day-of-year and delta-time embeddings are added; a multimodal sequence encoder uses forward-only (causal) attention across time; a forecaster predicts future image embeddings conditioned on weather up to the forecast date; a lightweight shared MLP decoder reconstructs/forecasts spectral imagery. Hidden size 256, patch size 8.

pretraining.method_details: Proposes forecasting as a conditional generation task that explicitly encodes causal driver-response structure: weather variables act as drivers and satellite imagery as response. Pretraining is done in two phases: (1) masked reconstruction to learn modality-specific and multimodal representations, then (2) forecasting to inject causal cross-modal dynamics into the embedding space.
pretraining.objective: Forecast future satellite imagery from past satellite imagery plus weather up to the future date

pretraining_data.num_images: Global: 50k train / 10k val / 10k test samples; US Cropland: 60k train / 20k val / 20k test samples

key_contribution: The paper introduces KG-VSF, a self-supervised multimodal pretraining objective that embeds domain knowledge of causal relationships by conditioning future satellite-image prediction on weather drivers. It shows that this knowledge-guided forecasting pretraining yields better embeddings than masked reconstruction or standard forecasting for remote sensing tasks where weather-driven land-surface dynamics matter.

title: MTP: Advancing Remote Sensing Foundation Model via Multi-Task Pretraining

architecture.notes: Shared backbone encoder with task-specific decoders for semantic segmentation, instance segmentation, and rotated object detection. Evaluated with RVSA-based vision transformers and InternImage-XL CNNs; primary large variant is ViT-L + RVSA. Uses UperNet, Mask R-CNN, and Oriented R-CNN as task heads during multi-task pretraining, and keeps only the backbone for downstream finetuning.

pretraining.method_details: A stage-wise pretraining paradigm applied on top of existing pretrained backbones (e.g., MAE or ImageNet-pretrained models) to reduce task discrepancy between pretraining and downstream remote sensing tasks. It jointly optimizes semantic segmentation, instance segmentation, and rotated object detection using a shared encoder and multiple task-specific decoders.
pretraining.objective: multi-task supervised learning with semantic segmentation, instance segmentation, and rotated object detection losses

key_contribution: The paper proposes Multi-Task Pretraining (MTP), a stage-wise supervised pretraining framework for remote sensing foundation models that jointly learns semantic segmentation, instance segmentation, and rotated object detection on SAMRS. It shows that this multi-task pretraining substantially improves transfer across 14 remote sensing benchmarks and supports both large vision transformers and CNN backbones.

title: MetaEarth: A Generative Foundation Model for Global-scale Remote Sensing Image Generation

architecture.backbone: U-Net-like diffusion backbone with RRDBNet conditional image encoder
architecture.notes: A unified diffusion model is shared across cascading stages for multi-resolution generation. The denoising network is a U-Net-like architecture with 5 encoder and 5 decoder blocks, 4 rounds of 2x down/up-sampling, channel multipliers [1,2,4,8,8], 3 ResBlocks per block, and AttentionBlocks at 8x channel multiplier stages. Conditional low-resolution images are encoded by RRDBNet and fused with noisy inputs; spatial resolution and timestep are embedded via frequency encoding plus MLP.

pretraining.method_details: MetaEarth introduces a resolution-guided self-cascading framework that recursively generates images from low to high resolution using a single shared model across stages, plus a novel noise sampling strategy for continuous unbounded generation. Training also uses high-order degradation to build robust low-/high-resolution pairs.
pretraining.objective: conditional denoising diffusion noise prediction for remote sensing image generation

datasets: Custom Google Earth global multi-resolution dataset

downstream_datasets: Custom 7-class remote sensing scene classification dataset
key_contribution: The paper proposes MetaEarth, a 600M-parameter generative foundation model that enables global-scale remote sensing image generation with controllable multi-resolution and arbitrary-size unbounded outputs. Its main innovations are a resolution-guided self-cascading diffusion framework and a noise sampling strategy that makes large tiled generations spatially continuous, while also serving as a useful data engine for downstream tasks.

title: OReole-FM: successes and challenges toward billion-parameter foundation models for high-resolution satellite imagery

architecture.notes: Family of vanilla Vision Transformers paired with an MAE decoder. Primary/largest variant is ViT-e with width 2816, depth 32, MLP 11264, 32 attention heads, using 14x14 patches; decoder follows original MAE. Variants include OReole-MR and OReole-HR.

pretraining.method_details: Masked autoencoding with vanilla ViTs, used to systematically study billion-scale model scaling for remote sensing. The paper also introduces a transfer initialization strategy for 4-band models by inflating pretrained 3-band RGB weights with a randomly initialized 4th band, and studies the interaction of model scaling with data scaling.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction

pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: mixed; MillionAID: 0.5-153 m/px, ORBITaL-Net: ~0.47 m/px, TIU: 0.3-0.8 m/px
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global for MillionAID; ORBITaL-Net spans North America, Asia, Africa, South America, and the Middle East; TIU covers Taiwan, India, and Ukraine

key_contribution: The paper introduces OReole, a family of billion-parameter remote sensing foundation models based on vanilla ViTs pretrained with MAE, and studies their scaling behavior on high-resolution satellite imagery using HPC resources including Frontier. It also contributes the TIU pretraining dataset and shows that effective model scaling in remote sensing requires corresponding data scaling, alongside a practical 3-band-to-4-band weight inflation strategy for multispectral initialization.

title: OlmoEarth: StableLatentImageModeling for Multimodal Earth Observation

architecture.notes: Vision Transformer-based spatio-temporal multimodal encoder-decoder. Uses a FlexiViT-style projection layer with variable patch size, 2D sinusoidal positional embeddings, sinusoidal temporal embeddings, and learnable modality embeddings. The primary/largest variant uses a 24-layer encoder with hidden dim 1024 and 16 heads; decoder has matching feature dimension and heads but depth 4, with cross-attention from masked tokens to visible encoder tokens.

pretraining.method: Latent MIM Lite with contrastive objectives
pretraining.method_details: A simplified latent masked image modeling approach where target tokens are produced by randomly initialized, frozen linear projections from image patches instead of a learned target encoder, improving training stability and avoiding representation collapse. It also introduces modality-aware masking, modality-restricted patch discrimination, and an instance contrastive loss across two differently masked views of the same sample. Supervised map layers and observational modalities are unified by projecting both through the same frozen random projection into token space.
pretraining.objective: masked latent token prediction with modality-aware patch discrimination contrastive loss plus instance-level contrastive alignment

key_contribution: The paper introduces OlmoEarth, a multimodal spatio-temporal Earth observation foundation model trained with a new stable self-supervised formulation called Latent MIM Lite. Its main innovations are frozen random latent targets, modality-aware masking, and modality-aware contrastive losses, yielding strong transfer and fine-tuning performance across a broad set of remote sensing benchmarks and real-world partner tasks.

title: OmniSat: Self-Supervised Modality Fusion for Earth Observation

architecture.backbone: Modality-specific CNN image encoders + LTAE temporal encoders + transformer-based modality combiner
architecture.notes: Uses dedicated encoders/decoders per modality: CNN-based encoder-decoder for images with max-pool index bypass to decoder (SegNet-style unpooling), and Lightweight Temporal Attention Encoder (LTAE) for optical/SAR time series. Modalities are fused with a transformer combiner composed of 6 residual self-attention blocks followed by a cross-attention block using learned modality-combining tokens. Embedding dimension d=256. Reported parameter counts: image encoder 3.6M, image decoder 1.8M, optical TS encoder 0.403M, optical TS decoder 0.096M, radar TS encoder 0.402M, radar TS decoder 0.095M, combiner 3.6M.

pretraining.method_details: OmniSat jointly applies patch-level cross-modal contrastive learning and multimodal masked reconstruction on aligned EO modalities. Its novelty is to exploit georeferenced alignment to contrast corresponding patches across modalities, exclude nearby same-tile negatives to avoid false negatives in spatially regular EO data, and reconstruct masked modality-specific inputs from fused multimodal patch representations. It also filters optical time-series reconstruction to the top 25% most informative dates based on LTAE attention to mitigate cloud contamination.
pretraining.objective: cross-modal patch alignment with InfoNCE-style contrastive loss plus masked multimodal reconstruction of modality-specific inputs

pretraining_data.num_images: approximately 50k TreeSatAI-TS tiles, 2.4k PASTIS-HD time series tiles, and 77k FLAIR tiles

key_contribution: The paper proposes OmniSat, a self-supervised multimodal EO fusion architecture that combines heterogeneous aligned modalities such as VHR imagery, optical time series, and SAR time series through joint cross-modal contrastive learning and masked reconstruction. It also introduces two augmented multimodal EO datasets, TreeSatAI-TS and PASTIS-HD, and shows state-of-the-art results in semi- and fully supervised classification while improving even unimodal inference through multimodal pretraining.

title: Panopticon: Advancing Any-Sensor Foundation Models for Earth Observation

architecture.notes: Built on DINOv2 with a ViT-B backbone (embedding dim 768). Replaces the standard patch embedding with a flexible channel-wise patch embedding: shared per-channel patchifier followed by cross-attention over channels using a single learned query. Uses spectral embeddings for optical wavelengths and learned SAR embeddings for transmit/receive polarization and orbit. Channel-attention dimension is 2304; patch embedding module has 12.9M parameters.

pretraining.method_details: Extends DINOv2 by treating co-registered images of the same geographic footprint from different sensors as natural augmented views, adding spectral channel subsampling as augmentation, and using a cross-attention-over-channels patch embedding to support arbitrary channel combinations across multispectral, hyperspectral, and SAR sensors. Also uses two-stage spectral progressive pretraining: first lower spectral diversity, then all sensors/datasets.
pretraining.objective: self-distillation between multi-view crops of the same footprint plus patch-wise iBOT objective

key_contribution: The paper introduces Panopticon, an any-sensor Earth observation foundation model that can ingest arbitrary multispectral, hyperspectral, and SAR channel configurations without sensor-specific adaptation at inference. By extending DINOv2 with multi-sensor footprint-based augmentations, spectral channel subsampling, and channel cross-attention with sensor-aware embeddings, it achieves strong state-of-the-art results and superior generalization to unseen sensors and resolutions.

title: Lightweight, Pre-trained Transformers for Remote Sensing Timeseries

architecture.notes: Primary pretrained model uses a lightweight masked autoencoder with identical encoder and decoder configurations: 2 attention layers, 8 heads, embedding size 128, MLP ratio 4. The full encoder-decoder has 0.81M parameters, while the encoder alone used downstream is about 0.40M parameters. Operates on remote-sensing pixel-timeseries tokens grouped by sensor/channel type rather than image patches.

pretraining.method_details: Presto is a masked autoencoding approach specialized for multi-sensor remote sensing pixel-timeseries. It uses structured masking over random values, channel groups, contiguous timesteps, and timesteps to reconstruct missing observations across time and modalities, making it robust to missing sensors/timestamps and suitable for diverse remote sensing inputs.
pretraining.objective: masked reconstruction of continuous and categorical remote sensing timeseries tokens across sensors and time

datasets: custom global pretraining dataset constructed following the Dynamic World sampling strategy

downstream_datasets: Tick Tick Bloom harmful algal bloom detection challenge dataset
key_contribution: The paper introduces Presto, a lightweight self-supervised transformer tailored to remote sensing pixel-timeseries that jointly leverages temporal structure, multiple sensors, static metadata, and structured masking. It achieves strong performance across diverse globally distributed downstream tasks while using far fewer parameters and FLOPs than prior large vision-based remote sensing foundation models.

title: Prithvi-EO-2.0: A Versatile Multi-Temporal Foundation Model for Earth Observation Applications
