architecture.notes: Masked autoencoder with asymmetric ViT encoder-decoder; primary model uses 3D patch embeddings via Conv3D over spatiotemporal inputs, 3D sinusoidal positional embeddings, and optional temporal/location metadata embeddings (year, day-of-year, latitude, longitude) added as learned weighted biases with metadata dropout. Largest variant is based on ViT-H; also includes smaller ViT-L 300M variant.

pretraining.method_details: Extends masked autoencoding to multi-temporal Earth observation by jointly modeling spatial and temporal dimensions with 3D patch/position embeddings and incorporating optional time and geolocation metadata embeddings. Uses sequences of four images sampled months apart over a decade to emphasize seasonal and long-term dynamics.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction

key_contribution: The paper introduces Prithvi-EO-2.0, a large multi-temporal Earth observation foundation model pretrained with masked autoencoding on 4.2M global HLS time-series samples using explicit spatiotemporal modeling and optional temporal/geolocation embeddings. It demonstrates strong transfer across a wide range of remote sensing tasks and resolutions, outperforming prior Prithvi and several competing geospatial foundation models in GEO-Bench and SME-led applications.

title: Foundation Models for Generalist Geospatial Artificial Intelligence

architecture.notes: Transformer-based masked autoencoder adapted for multi-temporal, multispectral satellite imagery. Uses asymmetric MAE with ViT backbone, 3D patch embedding via 3D convolutions, and 3D sine-cosine positional encoding. Input size 224x224 with patch size 1x16x16 (time x x x y). Largest/primary open-sourced model is Prithvi-100M.

pretraining.method_details: Self-supervised masked autoencoding on spatiotemporal multispectral satellite data. Novel aspects include 3D positional embeddings and 3D patch embeddings to handle two spatial dimensions plus time for irregular, low-frequency satellite observations.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction

downstream_datasets: Harmonized Landsat Sentinel-2 (HLS) cloud gap imputation dataset
key_contribution: The paper introduces Prithvi, a 100M-parameter geospatial foundation model and end-to-end framework for scalable pretraining and fine-tuning on raw multispectral, multi-temporal satellite imagery. It shows that MAE-style self-supervised pretraining on HLS data improves fine-tuning speed, data efficiency, and competitive performance across diverse Earth observation tasks such as cloud gap imputation, flood mapping, wildfire scar mapping, and crop segmentation.

title: Prithvi WxC: Foundation Model for Weather and Climate

architecture.notes: 2D scalable/flexible ViT that alternates local attention within windows and global attention across windows, inspired by Hiera and MaxViT. Final model uses 25 encoder blocks and 5 decoder blocks, hidden size 2560, 16 attention heads, MLP ratio 4. Uses 2x2 token size, 30x32 pixel windows, Fourier positional encoding, learned lead-time/input-time embeddings, and Swin-shift in decoder during pretraining; Swin-shift also added to encoder in forecasting phase.

pretraining.method_details: Pretraining combines MAE-style masking with forecasting on reanalysis data, using variable lead times and input time deltas. The target is anomaly relative to climatology rather than raw state or tendency, enabling unified handling of zero-lead and non-zero-lead tasks. Training uses both local and global masking strategies and a two-phase protocol, with a second phase focused on forecasting via dense autoregressive rollout tuning.
pretraining.objective: masked reconstruction and forecasting of climatology-normalized atmospheric state anomalies

key_contribution: The paper introduces Prithvi WxC, a 2.3B-parameter foundation model for weather and climate built on MERRA-2 and pretrained with a novel mixed objective that unifies masked reconstruction and forecasting through climatology-normalized anomaly prediction. It shows that a single pretrained model can be adapted across diverse weather/climate tasks including forecasting, downscaling, hurricane tracking, and gravity-wave parameterization, with released code and checkpoints.

title: RS5M and GeoRSCLIP: A Large Scale Vision-Language Dataset and A Large Vision-Language Model for Remote Sensing

architecture.notes: Primary model is a CLIP model fine-tuned on RS5M for remote sensing. Experiments include CLIP ViT-B/32, ViT-B/16, ViT-L/14, and ViT-H/14, with the largest/primary reported variant being ViT-H/14. They also evaluate parameter-efficient tuning variants (Pfeiffer, LoRA, Prefix-tuning, UniPELT), but full fine-tuning gives the best main results.

pretraining.method_details: The paper proposes a Domain Vision-Language Model (DVLM) framework that adapts a general VLM (CLIP) to the remote sensing domain using the newly constructed RS5M image-text dataset. The novelty is mainly in domain adaptation at scale plus the RS5M dataset construction pipeline, including filtering web image-text pairs and generating captions for label-only remote sensing datasets.
pretraining.objective: contrastive image-text alignment (InfoNCE)

key_contribution: The paper introduces RS5M, the first large-scale remote sensing image-text paired dataset with about 5 million pairs, built by filtering web-scale image-text corpora and captioning large remote sensing image datasets. It also presents GeoRSCLIP, a CLIP-based domain vision-language model adapted to remote sensing, showing strong gains on zero-shot classification, cross-modal retrieval, and semantic localization.

title: RSGPT: A Remote Sensing Vision Language Model and Benchmark

architecture.type: vision-language encoder-bridge-decoder with frozen image encoder and LLM connected by instruction-aware Q-Former
architecture.notes: Built on InstructBLIP. Uses a frozen EVA-G image encoder and frozen Vicuna LLM (vicuna7b/vicuna13b mentioned), with an instruction-aware Q-Former and a linear projection layer as trainable components. The primary/largest variant mentioned uses Vicuna-13B.

pretraining.method: instruction tuning / domain adaptation on top of InstructBLIP
pretraining.method_details: RSGPT is initialized from InstructBLIP pretrained weights and adapted to remote sensing by fine-tuning only the Q-Former and linear projection layer on the human-annotated RSICap dataset, keeping the image encoder and LLM frozen for data-efficient alignment.
pretraining.objective: image-text alignment and instruction-following generation for remote sensing image captioning and visual question answering

key_contribution: The paper introduces RSGPT, a remote sensing vision-language model adapted from InstructBLIP using parameter-efficient fine-tuning on a new high-quality human-annotated caption dataset, RSICap. It also releases RSIEval, a benchmark for remote sensing image captioning and VQA, and shows that RSGPT outperforms prior VLM baselines and achieves strong results on several remote sensing benchmarks.

title: RemoteCLIP: A Vision Language Foundation Model for Remote Sensing

architecture.notes: Primary/largest model uses a ViT-L-14 image encoder (304M params, 24 layers, 16 heads, 14x14 patches) and a Transformer text encoder (124M params, 12 layers, 8 heads, max length 77). The model is initialized from CLIP and continually pretrained for remote sensing.

pretraining.method_details: The paper performs continual pretraining of CLIP on a newly scaled remote sensing image-text corpus created by unifying heterogeneous annotations. It introduces Box-to-Caption (B2C) to convert detection boxes into captions and Mask-to-Box (M2B) to convert segmentation masks into boxes before caption generation, enabling much larger in-domain image-text pretraining.
pretraining.objective: bidirectional image-text contrastive alignment with InfoNCE loss

key_contribution: The paper introduces RemoteCLIP, the first general-purpose vision-language foundation model for remote sensing, built by continual pretraining of CLIP on a large in-domain image-text corpus. Its main novelty is a data scaling pipeline that converts detection and segmentation annotations into captions via B2C and M2B, enabling strong zero-shot, retrieval, few-shot, and counting performance across 16 datasets.

title: Representation Learning for Remote Sensing: An Unsupervised Sensor Fusion Approach

architecture.notes: Shared-weight Siamese ResNet encoder trained on multi-sensor inputs with random channel/sensor dropout. Contrastive loss is applied at multiple layers, specifically the last layers of the final two residual stacks. Inputs from three optical sensors are upsampled to a common 0.5 m resolution and concatenated depth-wise into a 12-channel input.

pretraining.method_details: CSF creates two augmented views of the same scene by randomly dropping channels/sensors and applying spatial and photometric augmentations, then trains a single shared encoder so representations remain similar across any subset of available sensor channels. It extends Contrastive Multiview Coding by using arbitrary channel dropout instead of fixed view partitions, weight sharing across views, and multi-layer contrastive loss.
pretraining.objective: contrastive alignment of representations from two views of the same coterminous multi-sensor scene using InfoNCE

datasets: custom coterminous image triplets from Airbus SPOT
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: mixed (0.5 m, 1.0 m, 1.5 m; resampled to 0.5 m)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: contiguous United States over the intersection of sensor coverage

downstream_tasks: representation quality evaluation via k-nearest neighbors classification
key_contribution: The paper introduces Contrastive Sensor Fusion, a self-supervised contrastive learning method that learns sensor-invariant representations from any subset of co-registered remote sensing channels using random channel dropout and a shared encoder. Trained on 47 million unlabeled multi-sensor scenes, CSF produces semantically meaningful fused representations that outperform ImageNet-supervised features on a remote sensing classification benchmark.

title: RoMA: Scaling up Mamba-based Foundation Models for Remote Sensing

architecture.notes: Uses a standard Mamba architecture following ARM without modifying the backbone. The primary model is Mamba-B with 12 layers, width 768, and Mamba+MLP blocks. Pretraining uses a Mamba encoder over all image patches plus an autoregressive decoder/cross-attention style next-token prediction with KV-cache-based prediction. Larger variant mentioned is Mamba-L (297M), but the main reported model is Mamba-B.

pretraining.method_details: RoMA introduces Rotation-aware Multi-scale Autoregressive learning for remote sensing. It combines an adaptive rotation encoding strategy using feature-based adaptive crop selection and learnable angle embeddings, with a multi-scale token prediction objective to better model arbitrary object orientations, sparse foregrounds, and large scale variation in remote sensing imagery.
pretraining.objective: next-token prediction with multi-scale reconstruction loss

key_contribution: The paper proposes RoMA, the first self-supervised autoregressive pretraining framework for Mamba-based remote sensing foundation models, designed to scale efficiently to high-resolution imagery. Its key innovations are adaptive rotation-aware encoding and multi-scale prediction, which improve representation learning for arbitrarily oriented, sparsely distributed, and scale-varying objects while delivering better accuracy and efficiency than ViT-based counterparts.

title: S2MAE: A Spatial-Spectral Pretraining Foundation Model for Spectral Remote Sensing Data

architecture.notes: Masked autoencoder with a plain ViT encoder and lightweight ViT decoder using 3D spatial-spectral patching and random 3D masking. Uses non-overlapping 3D cube tokens (primary setting: patch size 8x8x3), separable learnable spectral-spatial positional embeddings, and a high masking ratio of 90%. Multiple scales are studied (ViT-Base, ViT-Large, ViT-Huge); primary/largest variant reported is ViT-Huge.

pretraining.method_details: Extends masked autoencoding to spectral remote sensing imagery with 3D random masking over spatial and spectral dimensions, designed to capture local spectral continuity and spatial invariance while handling arbitrary band counts. Also uses progressive pretraining across multiple Sentinel-2 datasets with different image sizes and geographic coverage.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction

key_contribution: The paper proposes S2MAE, a spectral remote sensing foundation model that adapts masked autoencoding to multispectral imagery using 3D spatial-spectral masking and learnable spectral-spatial embeddings. It shows that high-mask-ratio 3D MAE pretraining, combined with progressive pretraining on large Sentinel-2 datasets, improves performance on classification and change detection tasks.

title: S4: Self-Supervised Sensing Across the Spectrum

architecture.notes: Uses modality-specific first two convolutional layers for radar and optical encoders, followed by two shared convolutional layers. Encoders fr and fo are 3D U-Net backbones with convolution, batch normalization, max-pooling, and leaky ReLU operating jointly over temporal and spatial dimensions. A decoder h is used for cross-modal reconstruction and later adapted for segmentation. A small projection head g with two 1x1x1 3D convolutions is used only during contrastive pretraining.

pretraining.method: multi-task self-supervised learning with pixel-wise contrastive learning and cross-modal reconstruction
pretraining.method_details: S4 jointly optimizes a novel multi-modal spatio-temporal (MMST) pixel-wise contrastive loss and a cross-modal reconstruction loss on spatially aligned radar-optical satellite image time series. Positive pairs are corresponding space-time pixels across modalities, while negatives are mismatched pixels across space or time. It also reconstructs one modality from the other, leveraging georegistered alignment and temporal interpolation across modalities.
pretraining.objective: pixel-wise cross-modal spatio-temporal alignment via InfoNCE plus cross-modal image time-series reconstruction via L1 loss

pretraining_data.num_images: PASTIS-R region: 5314 time series, 731k Sentinel-1 images, 90k Sentinel-2 images; Africa region: 5941 time series, 193k Sentinel-1 images, 70k Sentinel-2 images
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: France (PASTIS-R region) and South Sudan/Africa crop type mapping region

key_contribution: The paper introduces S4, a self-supervised pretraining framework for multimodal satellite image time series that exploits cross-spectrum spatial alignment between radar and optical imagery using a joint pixel-wise spatio-temporal contrastive loss and cross-modal reconstruction. It also curates m2s2-SITS, a large aligned unlabeled pretraining dataset, and shows improved SITS semantic segmentation under limited-label settings with single-modality inference.

title: SARATR-X: Toward Building A Foundation Model for SAR Target Recognition

architecture.notes: Uses a hierarchical vision transformer (HiViT) as the backbone within a masked autoencoder-style masked image modeling framework. HiViT was chosen for high spatial resolution and better handling of small SAR targets. The paper also compares against ViT and ConvNeXt-V2, but the primary model is HiViT-Base.

pretraining.method: MAE-style masked image modeling with two-step self-supervised pretraining
pretraining.method_details: The method first pretrains on ImageNet with masked image modeling to obtain initialization weights, then continues pretraining on SAR data. During SAR pretraining, it reconstructs multi-scale gradient features instead of raw pixels to suppress speckle noise and improve feature diversity. The two-step strategy is designed to improve attention diversity and model scalability under noisy SAR imagery.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction

pretraining_data.num_images: about 1.4M ImageNet images plus 186,600 SAR samples (SARDet-180K); abstract also states 0.18M unlabeled SAR target samples
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: mixed (about 0.1 m to 25 m for SAR; ImageNet mixed natural image resolution)

key_contribution: The paper presents SARATR-X, described as the first foundation model for SAR automatic target recognition, trained with self-supervised learning on a large curated SAR target corpus. Its main novelty is a SAR-tailored two-step masked image modeling scheme that transfers from ImageNet pretraining and reconstructs multi-scale gradient features to better handle SAR speckle noise and support label-efficient classification and detection.

title: SARVLM: A Vision Language Foundation Model for Semantic Understanding and Target Recognition in SAR Imagery

architecture.notes: SARVLM comprises SARCLIP and SARCap. SARCLIP is built on the OpenCLIP framework with image backbones including ResNet-50, ViT-B-32, and ViT-L-14; the primary/largest reported SARCLIP variant uses ViT-L-14. SARCap uses a CoCa encoder-decoder style image captioning architecture with a ViT-L-14 image encoder.

pretraining.method: vision-language contrastive pretraining with domain transfer
pretraining.method_details: The model is trained in two stages: first on optical remote sensing vision-language data to learn remote sensing semantics, then fine-tuned on the newly constructed SARVLM-1M SAR image-text corpus to transfer knowledge from optical remote sensing to SAR. SARCap further uses CoCa-style progressive caption training.
pretraining.objective: image-text contrastive alignment (symmetric InfoNCE); for SARCap additionally image caption generation

pretraining_data.num_images: 1.7M image-text pairs in SARVLM-1M; 279,996 training images and 1,385,993 training captions

key_contribution: The paper introduces SARVLM, presented as the first vision-language foundation model specialized for SAR imagery, along with SARVLM-1M, a large-scale SAR image-text dataset containing about 1.7 million pairs. Its main novelty is a two-stage domain transfer training strategy that transfers semantic alignment from optical remote sensing data to SAR, improving retrieval, recognition, zero-shot classification, localization, and captioning.

title: SS-MAE: Spatial-Spectral Masked Auto-Encoder for Multi-Source Remote Sensing Image Classification

architecture.notes: Two-branch masked autoencoder with a spatial-wise branch and a spectral-wise branch. Each branch uses a Transformer encoder-decoder with spatial or spectral attention. The training stage adds two lightweight CNNs with inverted residual blocks; for HSI the CNN uses 3D convolution, and for LiDAR/SAR it uses depth-wise convolution. Best settings reported include 2 Transformer blocks and token dimension 256.

pretraining.method_details: Proposes joint spatial-wise and spectral-wise masking for multi-source remote sensing data. One branch masks spatial pixels/patches and reconstructs missing pixels, while the other masks entire spectral channels/bands and reconstructs missing channels. This is combined with a Transformer-CNN hybrid design to capture both global and local features.
pretraining.objective: masked reconstruction of spatial pixels and spectral channels using MSE loss on masked regions

pretraining_data.num_images: up to 200000 sampled patches/pixels for pretraining depending on dataset
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: 30 m for Augsburg; mixed/matched by interpolation for Berlin; null for Houston 2018
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Berlin, Germany; Augsburg, Germany; Houston, USA

key_contribution: The paper introduces SS-MAE, a spatial-spectral masked autoencoder for multi-source remote sensing classification that explicitly reconstructs both masked spatial regions and masked spectral bands. It further combines dual-attention Transformers with lightweight CNNs to jointly model global and local features, achieving strong results on HSI+SAR and HSI+LiDAR benchmarks.

title: SSL4EO-L: Datasets and Foundation Models for Landsat Imagery

architecture.notes: The paper pre-trains multiple backbones (ResNet-18, ResNet-50, ViT-S16) using contrastive SSL on separate Landsat sensor/product splits. The largest/primary backbone reported among these is ResNet-50 by parameter count (23.56M), but ViT-S16 is the main transformer backbone and is used prominently in results. Encoders are initialized from ImageNet weights adapted to multispectral inputs by repeating/scaling RGB weights. Downstream evaluation uses a frozen encoder with a U-Net decoder.

pretraining.method_details: The paper extends Seasonal Contrast-style pretraining to Landsat by constructing a large global 4-season dataset across multiple Landsat sensors and product levels. Positive views are formed using two randomly sampled seasonal images from the same location ('random season contrast'), enabling multispectral, multitemporal contrastive pretraining for Landsat imagery.
pretraining.objective: contrastive representation learning / instance discrimination with multiseason positive pairs

key_contribution: The paper introduces SSL4EO-L, the first large-scale self-supervised pretraining dataset for Landsat imagery, comprising 5 million image patches across multiple sensors and product levels. It also releases the first Landsat foundation models, modernizes legacy benchmark datasets, and creates new benchmark datasets for TM and ETM+ SR to evaluate transfer on semantic segmentation tasks.

title: SSL4EO-S12: A Large-Scale Multi-Modal, Multi-Temporal Dataset for Self-Supervised Learning in Earth Observation

architecture.notes: The paper does not propose a single new neural architecture; it introduces a pretraining dataset and benchmarks existing SSL methods (MoCo-v2/v3, DINO, MAE, data2vec) using ResNet-50 and ViT-S/16 backbones.

pretraining.method: self-supervised pretraining benchmark (contrastive, distillation, masked image modeling, feature prediction)
pretraining.method_details: The novelty is not a new SSL loss but a large-scale EO-specific pretraining corpus with global coverage, multimodal Sentinel-1/2 data, four seasonal timestamps, overlap filtering, and inclusion of both Sentinel-2 L1C and L2A products. The paper benchmarks MoCo-v2/v3, DINO, MAE, and data2vec on this dataset, including RS-specific augmentations such as RandomSeasonContrast and multimodal training with RandomSensorDrop.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction / feature-level prediction / contrastive representation learning / self-distillation, depending on the benchmarked SSL method

pretraining_data.num_images: about 3M images; about 1M Sentinel-1/2 triplets across 251,079 locations with 4 seasonal snapshots

key_contribution: The paper introduces SSL4EO-S12, a large-scale global Earth observation pretraining dataset with multimodal Sentinel-1/2 data and multi-seasonal coverage, designed for self-supervised learning. It shows that pretraining on this dataset consistently improves transfer performance across classification, semantic segmentation, and change detection compared with existing EO and ImageNet-based pretraining datasets.

title: SatMAE: Pre-training Transformers for Temporal and Multi-Spectral Satellite Imagery

architecture.notes: MAE-style asymmetric transformer with a ViT encoder and transformer decoder. Extended to temporal and multi-spectral satellite imagery via temporal/spectral positional encodings, independent masking across time or spectral groups, and separate patch embeddings for grouped spectral bands in the multi-spectral variant.

pretraining.method_details: Adapts masked autoencoding to satellite imagery by adding temporal encodings from timestamp components (year, month, hour), spectral/group encodings for band groups, and masking strategies tailored to temporal sequences and multi-spectral bands. For multi-spectral data, bands can be grouped with separate patch embeddings per group.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction

pretraining_data.num_images: fMoW Sentinel: about 713k training images (712,874 train; 84,939 val; 84,966 test)

key_contribution: The paper introduces SatMAE, a masked autoencoder pretraining framework tailored for temporal and multi-spectral satellite imagery using temporal/spectral encodings and specialized masking strategies. It also releases fMoW-Sentinel and shows strong gains over prior remote sensing SSL methods across classification and segmentation benchmarks.

title: Rethinking Transformers Pre-training for Multi-Spectral Satellite Imagery

architecture.notes: Built on the MAE/SatMAE framework with a ViT encoder-decoder and additional convolution-based upsampling blocks for multi-scale reconstruction. Uses standard sinusoidal positional encodings; for multispectral Sentinel-2 data it follows SatMAE channel grouping by GSD. Primary RGB setup uses 224x224 input with patch size 16; multispectral setup uses 96x96 input with patch size 8 and reconstructs at up to three scales.

pretraining.method_details: SatMAE++ revisits masked autoencoding for remote sensing by reconstructing the image not only at the base input scale but also at higher spatial scales using transpose-convolution upsampling blocks and residual convolution blocks. Unlike ScaleMAE, it does not rely on GSD-based positional encoding or a Laplacian pyramid decoder, making it applicable to both RGB and multispectral imagery.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction at the base scale with MSE plus higher-scale reconstruction with L1 loss

pretraining_data.num_images: fMoW-RGB: ~363k train + 53k test; fMoW-Sentinel: 712874 train + 84939 val + 84966 test
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: mixed; Sentinel-2 bands at 10m, 20m, and 60m GSD

downstream_tasks: land use and land cover classification
key_contribution: The paper proposes SatMAE++, a simple multi-scale masked autoencoder for remote sensing that augments SatMAE with convolutional upsampling-based reconstruction at higher resolutions. It shows that standard positional encodings plus multi-scale reconstruction are sufficient to learn stronger representations across both RGB and multispectral satellite imagery, achieving state-of-the-art results on six datasets.

title: SatMamba: Development of Foundation Models for Remote Sensing Imagery Using State Space Models

architecture.notes: Masked autoencoder framework with Mamba-based encoder and decoder. Primary model is SatMamba-B with 12-layer encoder, 8-layer decoder, encoder embedding dim 768, decoder embedding dim 512, state dimension 64, and head dimension 96. Uses multi-way Mamba blocks with up to four scanning directions: row-major forward/backward and column-major forward/backward. Maintains natural patch order and optionally uses fixed positional encodings; best results reported without positional encodings.

pretraining.method_details: Integrates State Space Models (Mamba 2) into a masked autoencoder for remote sensing. Unlike standard MAE with ViT, masking is applied before flattening, patch order is preserved rather than shuffled, and the encoder/decoder use multi-directional scanning Mamba blocks to model images as sequences with linear scaling in input length.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction

key_contribution: The paper proposes SatMamba, a remote sensing foundation model pretraining framework that replaces the usual ViT backbone in MAE with Mamba-based state space models for linear scaling with sequence length. It shows competitive or better performance than ViT-based MAE baselines on high-resolution image-to-image tasks such as semantic segmentation and building damage assessment.

title: SatSwinMAE: Efficient Autoencoding for Multiscale Time-series Satellite Imagery

architecture.notes: Hierarchical 3D masked autoencoder for multi-temporal satellite imagery. Uses 3D patch partitioning, Video Swin Transformer blocks, spatial-only patch merging/expanding, and a SwinUNet-like fine-tuning architecture with skip connections. A temporal modulation CNN head is added for downstream multi-temporal tasks. Pretraining uses both encoder and decoder weights for transfer.

pretraining.method_details: Extends SwinMAE to satellite time series with a hierarchical 3D masked autoencoder using Video Swin Transformer blocks. Applies window masking on each spatial slice, replaces masked tokens with a learnable vector instead of removing them, preserves temporal dimension through the hierarchy, and transfers both encoder and decoder pretrained weights into a SwinUNet-like downstream model with skip connections.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction

key_contribution: The paper proposes SatSwinMAE, a Swin-based 3D masked autoencoder for multi-scale, multi-temporal satellite imagery that captures spatio-temporal dependencies more efficiently than ViT-based MAE models. By combining Video Swin pretraining with SwinUNet-style skip connections and temporal modulation for fine-tuning, it achieves state-of-the-art or better results across several geospatial dense prediction tasks.

title: SATVISION-TOA: A GEOSPATIAL FOUNDATION MODEL FOR COARSE-RESOLUTION ALL-SKY REMOTE SENSING IMAGERY

architecture.notes: Masked-image-modeling pretraining on 128x128x14 MODIS TOA chips using 8x8 masked patches. The primary model is SVTOA-Giant; SwinV2 Huge (658M) is also studied. The paper describes hierarchical windowed attention in SwinV2 and uses the pretrained backbone for downstream fine-tuning with an FCN decoder for 3D cloud retrieval.

pretraining.method_details: Pretrained with a SimMIM-style masked-image-modeling setup on 14-band MODIS Level 1B Top-Of-Atmosphere all-sky imagery, explicitly including clouds and atmospheric variability rather than cloud-free imagery. This targets moderate/coarse-resolution multispectral Earth observation and cross-sensor transfer to MODIS-like instruments.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction

datasets: MOD021KM MODIS/Terra Calibrated Radiances 5-Min L1B Swath 1km
custom global daily composites of TERRA-MODIS TOA image chips

key_contribution: The paper introduces SatVision-TOA, a 3B-parameter SwinV2-based self-supervised foundation model pretrained on 100 million global 14-band MODIS all-sky TOA image chips for moderate/coarse-resolution remote sensing. By learning from cloud-inclusive multispectral imagery, it establishes a large-scale atmospheric-aware geospatial foundation model and substantially improves downstream 3D cloud retrieval over an FCN baseline.

title: Scale-MAE: A Scale-Aware Masked Autoencoder for Multiscale Geospatial Representation Learning

architecture.notes: MAE-style ViT encoder with Ground Sample Distance Positional Encoding (GSDPE) and a progressive Laplacian-pyramid decoder. The decoder uses fewer transformer layers than vanilla MAE (3 instead of 8 in the main setting), followed by deconvolution-based upsampling and Laplacian Blocks to reconstruct separate low-frequency and high-frequency outputs at different scales.

pretraining.method_details: A scale-aware masked autoencoding approach that injects absolute scale via GSD-based positional encoding and uses a Laplacian-pyramid decoder to reconstruct low-frequency and high-frequency targets at different scales. Unlike standard MAE, it explicitly learns cross-scale relationships during pretraining.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction with multiscale low-frequency and high-frequency reconstruction losses

key_contribution: The paper proposes Scale-MAE, a self-supervised geospatial pretraining method that makes MAE scale-aware through GSD-based positional encoding and a Laplacian-pyramid decoder for multiscale reconstruction. This yields more robust representations across varying ground sample distances and improves remote sensing classification and building segmentation over prior MAE-based methods such as SatMAE and ConvMAE.

title: SeaMo: A Season-Aware Multimodal Foundation Model for Remote Sensing

architecture.notes: Masked autoencoder-style architecture with modality-specific patch embedding modules for Sentinel-2 optical and Sentinel-1 SAR, a unified multimodal ViT encoder, Temporal-Multimodal (TM) fusion blocks using cross-attention across seasons and modalities, and modality-specific reconstruction decoders. After pretraining, only the encoder is used for downstream tasks.

pretraining.method_details: SeaMo uses a progressive masked image modeling strategy: first single-time-point multimodal learning, then multi-time-flow multimodal learning with Temporal-Multimodal fusion blocks. Novel elements include unaligned/partially overlapping spatial region selection, unified multimodal encoding of optical+SAR, and season-aware temporal-multimodal fusion via cross-attention.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction

key_contribution: The paper proposes SeaMo, a season-aware multimodal remote sensing foundation model that jointly exploits spatial, spectral, temporal, and cross-sensor information using masked image modeling. Its main innovations are progressive multimodal-to-seasonal pretraining, partially overlapping geospatial crop selection, and Temporal-Multimodal fusion blocks that explicitly model season-dependent multimodal relationships for improved transfer across optical and SAR tasks.

title: Seasonal Contrast: Unsupervised Pre-Training from Uncurated Remote Sensing Data

architecture.type: encoder with multiple contrastive projection heads (multi-head contrastive SSL)
architecture.notes: Primary evaluations use ResNet-18 and ResNet-50 backbones with a MoCo-v2 style momentum encoder setup. The encoder is followed by 3 separate 2-layer MLP projection heads corresponding to embedding sub-spaces that are invariant to all augmentations, invariant to seasonal changes only, or invariant to synthetic augmentations only. The transfer representation is taken from the shared backbone embedding.

pretraining.method: contrastive self-supervised learning (MoCo-v2-based, multi-embedding seasonal contrast)
pretraining.method_details: SeCo exploits temporally aligned satellite images from the same location across seasons as natural positive pairs, while using multiple embedding sub-spaces to learn both season-invariant and season-variant features. Unlike standard contrastive learning that enforces a single invariance structure, SeCo disentangles temporal and synthetic augmentation invariances through separate projection heads and losses.
pretraining.objective: contrastive alignment with InfoNCE over seasonal and artificial views

datasets: SeCo dataset (custom uncurated Sentinel-2 dataset collected with Google Earth Engine)

key_contribution: The paper introduces SeCo, a self-supervised remote sensing pretraining pipeline that combines automatic collection of large-scale uncurated multi-temporal Sentinel-2 imagery with a multi-subspace contrastive objective. Its main novelty is learning representations that preserve both seasonal invariances and temporal variations, improving transfer on land-cover classification and change detection over ImageNet and prior SSL baselines.

title: Self-Supervised Learning of Remote Sensing Scene Representations Using Contrastive Multiview Coding

architecture.notes: Uses Contrastive Multiview Coding (CMC) with two ResNet-50 encoder networks processing two channel-split views of the same image, followed by linear projection heads and L2 normalization. For RGB, views are L* and ab* in Lab color space; for multispectral, views are defined either by spectral-band grouping or PCA-based channel decorrelation.

pretraining.method_details: Applies Contrastive Multiview Coding to remote sensing imagery by splitting channels into two complementary views and maximizing agreement between views from the same image while contrasting against views from other images. The paper also extends CMC to multispectral Sentinel-2 imagery using two view-construction strategies: wavelength-based band grouping and PCA-based decorrelated views.
pretraining.objective: contrastive alignment between two channel-based views of the same image

key_contribution: The paper systematically evaluates contrastive self-supervised pretraining for remote sensing scene representation learning and shows that in-domain remote sensing pretraining can outperform supervised ImageNet pretraining when used as a feature extractor. It also extends CMC to multispectral Sentinel-2 data, demonstrating additional gains over RGB-only self-supervised pretraining.

title: Self-Supervised Material and Texture Representation Learning for Remote Sensing Tasks

architecture.notes: ResNet-34 backbone with a parameter-free Texture Refinement Network (TeRN) inserted after the first layer, followed by a patch-wise Surface Residual Encoder adapted from Deep-TEN. Uses densely sampled small image crops/patches and 64 learned residual clusters.

pretraining.method: contrastive self-supervised learning with residual cluster encoding
pretraining.method_details: MATTER learns material and texture representations from multi-temporal, spatially aligned imagery over unchanged regions. It combines a Texture Refinement Network to amplify low-level texture features with a Surface Residual Encoder that encodes soft affinity to multiple learned surface clusters, and contrastively enforces consistency between residual representations of temporally paired unchanged patches.
pretraining.objective: Noise Contrastive Estimation on patch-wise surface residual representations to make unchanged multi-temporal patches similar and patches from different regions dissimilar

datasets: Custom curated multi-temporal Sentinel-2 unchanged-regions dataset
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: 27 regions of interest spanning 1217 km2 across a variety of climates

key_contribution: The paper introduces MATTER, a self-supervised remote sensing pretraining method that explicitly learns material and texture representations using multi-temporal unchanged Sentinel-2 imagery. Its key novelty is combining low-level texture refinement with patch-wise residual cluster encoding to produce features that transfer strongly to change detection, land cover classification, and segmentation while converging much faster than prior self-supervised baselines.

title: Semantic Segmentation of Remote Sensing Images With Self-Supervised Multitask Representation Learning

architecture.type: encoder with multitask pretraining heads; triplet Siamese network for self-supervised pretraining
architecture.notes: Primary method uses a shared CNN backbone (experiments with VGG16 and ResNet50) with three branches for inpainting, augmentation transform prediction (ATP), and contrastive learning. The pretraining architecture is a triplet Siamese network with shared weights across input branches. For downstream semantic segmentation, five transposed convolution layers are added on top of the backbone.

pretraining.method_details: The method jointly optimizes three pretext tasks to capture both low-level and high-level remote sensing features: image inpainting for low-level detail recovery, augmentation transform prediction (ATP) for recognizing applied transforms, and contrastive learning for invariant high-level representation learning. A multitask loss combines all three objectives in a shared triplet Siamese framework.
pretraining.objective: masked/occluded image reconstruction, transformation classification, and contrastive instance discrimination

key_contribution: The paper proposes a self-supervised multitask pretraining framework tailored for remote sensing semantic segmentation that jointly learns low-level and high-level representations using inpainting, augmentation transform prediction, and contrastive learning. It shows stronger segmentation performance than random initialization, ImageNet pretraining, and several prior self-supervised baselines, especially in low-label regimes.

title: SkyEyeGPT: Unifying Remote Sensing Vision-Language Tasks via Instruction Tuning with Large Language Model

architecture.type: vision-language decoder-only MLLM with visual encoder + linear alignment layer + LLM decoder
architecture.notes: Uses frozen EVA-CLIP as visual encoder, a single linear alignment layer after concatenating every 4 adjacent visual tokens, and an LLaMA2-chat decoder. Fine-tuned with LoRA (rank 64) on the alignment layer and LLM. Input images are resized to 448x448. UAV video features are formed by concatenating features from multiple frame images. Initialized from MiniGPT-v2 checkpoint.

pretraining.method_details: Rather than proposing a new foundation pretraining objective, the paper builds a remote-sensing multimodal instruction-following dataset (SkyEye-968k) and performs two-stage tuning: first on single-task image-text instructions for RS image-text alignment, then on multi-task conversational instructions to improve multi-round, multi-task dialogue. Task-specific identifiers such as [caption], [vqa], [refer], and [detection] are used to unify diverse RS tasks.
pretraining.objective: autoregressive next-token prediction for instruction-following over multimodal inputs

key_contribution: The paper introduces SkyEyeGPT, a unified remote sensing multimodal large language model that handles diverse open-ended RS vision-language tasks within a single decoder-only framework using a simple linear visual-language connector. It also contributes SkyEye-968k, a manually verified 968k-sample remote sensing instruction-tuning dataset, and a two-stage tuning strategy that improves both task performance and multi-turn conversational ability.

title: SkyScript: A Large and Semantically Diverse Vision-Language Dataset for Remote Sensing

architecture.notes: Continual pre-training of CLIP on SkyScript. Primary reported model is SkyCLIP-50 with a ViT-L/14 image encoder initialized from OpenAI CLIP; the paper also evaluates a ViT-B/32 variant. Uses image-text contrastive learning.

pretraining.method_details: The paper's main novelty is constructing SkyScript, a 2.6M-pair remote sensing vision-language dataset by linking Google Earth Engine imagery with OpenStreetMap semantics via geo-coordinates, then continually pre-training CLIP on this dataset. It uses automatically assembled single-object and multi-object captions and filters noisy pairs using CLIP similarity.
pretraining.objective: image-text contrastive alignment

sensors: Planet SkySat Public Ortho Imagery, RGB
Planet SkySat Public Ortho Imagery, MS

key_contribution: The paper introduces SkyScript, a large-scale and semantically diverse remote sensing vision-language dataset built by geospatially aligning open remote sensing imagery with OpenStreetMap tags. Using SkyScript for continual CLIP pre-training yields SkyCLIP, which improves zero-shot scene classification, fine-grained attribute classification, and cross-modal retrieval in remote sensing.

title: Multi-label Guided Soft Contrastive Learning for Efficient Earth Observation Pretraining

architecture.type: Siamese momentum encoder with encoder-only backbone for contrastive pretraining
architecture.notes: Primary model uses MoCo-v3-style ViT-B/14 with two separate projectors for contrastive loss and soft contrastive loss. Continual pretraining initializes from DINOv2 weights; for non-RGB EO inputs the input embedding layer is randomly initialized. A frozen momentum encoder is updated by EMA, and Siamese masking masks 20% of patches on the trainable branch. The paper also studies ResNet-50 and ViT-S/14 variants, and both multispectral and SAR models.

pretraining.method: soft contrastive learning with continual pretraining
pretraining.method_details: The paper extends supervised contrastive learning to multi-label EO scenes by computing soft pairwise similarities from multi-hot land-cover labels and matching them with feature similarities via a soft contrastive loss. This is combined with standard contrastive loss, plus cross-domain continual pretraining from strong vision foundation models (DINO/DINOv2) and Siamese masking for efficient ViT training.
pretraining.objective: contrastive instance discrimination plus soft similarity alignment based on multi-label land-cover supervision

key_contribution: The paper proposes SoftCon, a multi-label guided soft contrastive learning framework that uses land-cover-derived scene-level multi-label annotations to model soft semantic similarity between EO images. Combined with simple continual pretraining from strong vision foundation models and Siamese masking, it produces efficient multispectral and SAR EO foundation models that achieve state-of-the-art results on 10 of 11 downstream tasks.

title: SpectralEarth: Training Hyperspectral Foundation Models at Scale

architecture.notes: Primary architecture family consists of Spectral ResNet-50 and Spectral ViT variants (ViT-S/B/L/H/g) augmented with a spectral adapter. The spectral adapter uses three 1D Conv+BN+ReLU layers (kernel sizes 7, 7, 5; strides 5, 5, 3) to process hyperspectral bands and produce 128-channel features. For ViT, the adapter is placed before patch embedding and uses 4x4 patches to preserve fine spatial detail. Largest primary variant discussed is Spectral ViT-L pretrained with MAE.

pretraining.method: multi-method SSL: MAE, DINO, and MoCo-V2
pretraining.method_details: The paper pretrains hyperspectral foundation models on the new SpectralEarth dataset using standard SSL methods adapted to HSI via a lightweight spectral adapter inserted into classical 2D backbones. Temporal overlapping EnMAP acquisitions are additionally used as positive pairs for joint-embedding methods (MoCo-V2, DINO).
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction for MAE; self-distillation/invariant representation learning for DINO; contrastive representation learning for MoCo-V2

key_contribution: The paper introduces SpectralEarth, a large-scale globally distributed EnMAP hyperspectral pretraining dataset with 538,974 patches, including multi-temporal coverage, and uses it to train hyperspectral foundation models with SSL. It also proposes a simple spectral adapter for adapting standard ResNet/ViT backbones to HSI and provides extensive benchmarks across nine hyperspectral downstream datasets, showing improved transferability and efficient fine-tuning.

title: TOV: The Original Vision Model for Optical Remote Sensing Image Understanding via Self-supervised Learning

architecture.notes: Contrastive self-supervised pretraining with a 2-layer MLP projection head; downstream adapters are task-specific: fully connected layer for scene classification, Faster R-CNN components (RPN + ROIHead) for object detection, and FCN decoder for semantic segmentation. Uses layer freezing in stage 2 as a memory retention strategy.

pretraining.method_details: A human-like two-stage SSL learning path: first pretrain on a large natural image dataset to learn general visual knowledge, then continue pretraining on remote sensing imagery to learn domain-specialized knowledge. To reduce catastrophic forgetting, shallow and middle layers learned in stage 1 are fixed during stage 2.
pretraining.objective: contrastive alignment of augmented views of the same image versus views from different images

pretraining_data.num_images: 1M natural images + 0.5M remote sensing images for the main balanced setting

key_contribution: The paper introduces TOV, a general optical remote sensing vision model trained in a label-free, task-independent way using contrastive self-supervised learning. Its main novelty is a human-like two-stage pretraining path from natural images to remote sensing images, combined with automated geographically guided dataset construction, yielding strong transfer across scene classification, object detection, and semantic segmentation.

title: TerraMind: Large-Scale Generative Multimodality for Earth Observation

architecture.notes: Symmetric Transformer-based encoder-decoder inspired by 4M; supports dual-scale early fusion of token-level and pixel-level inputs. Uses modality-specific tokenizers for image-like modalities with ViT-B encoders, finite-scalar quantization (FSQ), and diffusion decoders; captions and coordinates are tokenized with a modified WordPiece tokenizer. Primary large variant is TerraMindv1-L.

pretraining.method_details: Two-stage pretraining: first train unimodal modality-specific tokenizers, then pretrain TerraMind on both token-level and pixel-level inputs using dual-scale early fusion. The model reconstructs randomly sampled masked target tokens across modalities, framing pretraining as cross-modal patch-level classification. Novel aspects are any-to-any multimodal generation for EO and joint learning from token abstractions plus raw pixel patches.
pretraining.objective: masked target token reconstruction with cross-entropy loss; autoregressive decoding for sequence-like generation

downstream_datasets: South Africa Crop Type Mapping dataset
key_contribution: The paper introduces TerraMind, the first any-to-any generative multimodal foundation model for Earth observation, trained with a dual-scale token-level and pixel-level masked modeling strategy on a global multimodal dataset. It shows strong generation, zero-shot, few-shot, and fine-tuning performance, and proposes Thinking-in-Modalities (TiM), where generated modalities are fed back during fine-tuning or inference to improve downstream results.

title: The color out of space: learning self-supervised representations for Earth Observation imagery
model_name: Colorization pre-training for Earth Observation imagery

architecture.notes: Uses a ResNet-18 encoder over non-RGB spectral bands and a mirrored decoder with transposed convolutions to predict the a*b* chromatic channels in CIE Lab space. For downstream tasks, the encoder is reused as a feature extractor with a linear classification head. An inference-time ensemble combines a spectral branch pretrained by colorization with an RGB branch pretrained on ImageNet.

pretraining.method_details: The model is pretrained self-supervised by reconstructing visible color information from non-RGB Sentinel-2 spectral bands rather than from grayscale. It predicts chromatic a*b* channels in Lab space from spectral inputs, encouraging the encoder to learn semantically meaningful remote sensing representations.
pretraining.objective: masked-free image-to-image reconstruction of color channels from spectral bands

key_contribution: The paper proposes a self-supervised remote sensing pretraining strategy that learns representations by colorizing RGB channels from non-RGB spectral Sentinel-2 bands. The learned encoder transfers well to downstream classification, especially in low-label regimes, and an ensemble with an ImageNet-pretrained RGB branch further improves performance.

title: Tile2Vec: Unsupervised representation learning for spatially distributed data

architecture.notes: Uses a CNN encoder trained on tile triplets with triplet loss. For the main remote sensing experiments, the paper uses a ResNet-18 modified for 28x28 CIFAR-10 images, with an additional residual block to handle larger 50x50 multispectral inputs and with the final classification layer removed.

pretraining.method: triplet-based metric learning using spatial context
pretraining.method_details: The method extends the distributional hypothesis to geospatial data: geographically nearby tiles are treated as semantically similar, while distant tiles are treated as dissimilar. Anchor-neighbor-distant triplets are sampled based on spatial neighborhoods, and the encoder is trained to pull nearby tiles together and push faraway tiles apart in embedding space.
pretraining.objective: triplet loss on spatially sampled tiles

pretraining_data.num_images: 100k triplets for the main NAIP experiment; 300k NAIP tiles available; additional experiments use 100k triplets on Landsat data and 11,564 DigitalGlobe images
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Central Valley, California; San Francisco; New York City; Boston; Uganda; Addis Ababa, Ethiopia; global countries

key_contribution: The paper proposes Tile2Vec, a self-supervised representation learning method for spatially distributed data that uses geographic proximity as weak supervision via a triplet loss. It shows that spatial context alone can produce semantically meaningful embeddings that improve remote sensing classification and regression performance, even outperforming some supervised baselines with limited labels.

title: Towards Scalable Foundation Model for Multi-modal and Hyperspectral Geospatial Data

architecture.notes: ViT-Base-like model with 12 LESS attention blocks in the encoder and 8 blocks in the decoder for Hyper-MAE pretraining; embedding dimensions 768/512 for encoder/decoder; patch size 16; uses Tied Patch Embedding, Continuous Positional-Channel Embedding, Perception Field Mask, and low-rank spatial-spectral attention via Kronecker-product approximation. Supports modality-specific tied patch embeddings for optical and SAR, then concatenates tokens.

pretraining.method_details: Introduces Hyper-MAE, a hyperspectral masked autoencoder that decouples spatial and spectral masking. It masks 75% of spatial patches and 50% of spectral channels, applies identical spatial masks across unmasked channels, and reconstructs masked regions with separate spatial and spectral reconstruction losses to better learn inter-channel and spatial-spectral correlations.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction with decoupled spatial and spectral reconstruction losses

key_contribution: The paper proposes LESS ViT, a scalable and physics-informed transformer architecture for multi-modal and hyperspectral geospatial data that efficiently models spatial-spectral relationships through low-rank attention, continuous positional-channel embeddings, and locality-aware masking. It also introduces Hyper-MAE for hyperspectral self-supervised pretraining and GFM-Bench for standardized evaluation, showing competitive results and improved cross-satellite generalization.

title: Towards a Unified Copernicus Foundation Model for Earth Vision

architecture.notes: Vision Transformer encoder with dynamic hypernetwork-based patch embedding for arbitrary spectral and non-spectral modalities; uses a spectral hypernetwork conditioned on wavelength and bandwidth, a variable hypernetwork conditioned on LLM-encoded variable names, metadata encoding via Fourier features, and a lightweight Transformer decoder for MAE-style masked reconstruction. Primary model is pretrained with ViT-Base; patch sizes vary by modality (16 for Sentinel-1/2, 8 for Sentinel-3, 4 for Sentinel-5P, 64 for DEM).

pretraining.method: masked image modeling with continual distillation
pretraining.method_details: The model unifies all major Copernicus Sentinel modalities plus DEM using dynamic hypernetworks that generate modality-specific patch embeddings for both spectral and non-spectral inputs. It additionally injects metadata (geolocation, area, time) through Fourier encodings and uses auxiliary distillation from frozen DINOv2 and SoftCon teachers to improve representation quality and convergence.
pretraining.objective: MAE-style masked image reconstruction plus representation distillation

pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global land surface and near-land ocean, including polar regions

key_contribution: The paper introduces a unified Earth observation foundation model ecosystem consisting of Copernicus-Pretrain, Copernicus-FM, and Copernicus-Bench. Its main contribution is showing that dynamic hypernetworks and metadata-aware masked pretraining can jointly handle heterogeneous Copernicus modalities—from SAR and multispectral imagery to atmospheric variables and DEM—and improve both surface and atmospheric downstream tasks.

title: USat: A Unified Self-Supervised Encoder for Multi-Sensor Satellite Imagery

architecture.notes: USat is a Vision Transformer encoder modified for multi-sensor, multi-spectral imagery with varying ground sampling distances. It uses separate patch projection layers per spectral band, spectral group pooling, superpositional encodings, and optional spectral-group/sensor encodings. The MAE decoder is an 8-layer Transformer.

pretraining.method_details: Masked autoencoding over jointly paired multi-sensor imagery (NAIP + Sentinel-2) using a unified encoder that supports arbitrary subsets of spectral bands and varying spatial resolutions/GSDs. Novel components include per-band patch projection, spectral group pooling to reduce sequence length, and superpositional encodings to preserve geospatial relationships across bands/sensors with different GSDs.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction with mean squared error

key_contribution: The paper introduces USat, a unified self-supervised encoder for multi-sensor satellite imagery, and USatMAE, a masked autoencoding pretraining framework that jointly learns from imagery with different spectral bands and spatial resolutions. Its key innovation is handling arbitrary subsets of bands across sensors while preserving cross-scale geospatial alignment, yielding improved performance over prior remote-sensing MAE baselines on several benchmarks.

title: UrbanCLIP: Learning Text-Enhanced Urban Region Profiling with Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining from the Web

architecture.notes: Uses a ViT-based unimodal image encoder and a causal masking Transformer decoder for text; the first half of decoder layers acts as a unimodal text encoder and the remaining decoder layers perform multimodal interaction via cross-attention. Includes learnable [CLS] tokens, task-specific attention poolers, and a decoupled decoder mechanism for joint contrastive and generative training.

pretraining.method: joint contrastive language-image pretraining + language modeling
pretraining.method_details: Satellite images are first paired with LLM-generated and refined location descriptions. UrbanCLIP jointly learns image-text alignment with a bidirectional contrastive loss and deep multimodal fusion with an autoregressive language modeling loss. A key novelty is using LLM-generated urban descriptions to inject textual knowledge into urban visual representation learning, plus a decoupled decoder that supports both unimodal text encoding and multimodal cross-attention efficiently.
pretraining.objective: image-text contrastive alignment and autoregressive next-token language modeling

datasets: custom dataset from Baidu Map API
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: four cities in China: Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou, Shenzhen

key_contribution: The paper introduces UrbanCLIP, the first framework to incorporate LLM-generated text descriptions into urban region profiling from satellite imagery. By jointly optimizing image-text contrastive alignment and language modeling, it learns text-enhanced visual representations that improve prediction of urban indicators such as population, GDP, and carbon emissions across multiple cities.

title: WV-Net: A foundation model for SAR WV-mode satellite imagery trained using contrastive self-supervised learning on 10 million images

architecture.notes: Trained with the SimCLR framework. The encoder consists of a ResNet50 backbone followed by a smaller projection head during pretraining; only the backbone is used for transfer learning. Input images are grayscale Sentinel-1 WV SAR images repeated to 3 channels for augmentations/model input.

pretraining.method_details: WV-Net is pretrained on nearly 10 million Sentinel-1 Wave Mode SAR images using SimCLR, with an augmentation set adapted for SAR WV imagery. The final model extends baseline SimCLR augmentations with mixup, random rotation, random color inversion, and sharpness transforms after an augmentation study.
pretraining.objective: contrastive alignment of augmented views of the same image while separating views from different images

downstream_datasets: Wang et al. [61] labelled ocean SAR imagery dataset
Quach et al. [43] wave height regression dataset
Stopa et al. [52] air temperature regression dataset
key_contribution: The paper introduces WV-Net, the first foundation model for Sentinel-1 Wave Mode open-ocean SAR imagery, pretrained with SimCLR on 9.9 million unlabeled images. It shows that domain-specific self-supervised pretraining on SAR WV data yields better embeddings than ImageNet pretraining across classification, regression, and retrieval tasks, with improved data efficiency and strong performance even without full finetuning.

title: WildSAT: Learning Satellite Image Representations from Wildlife Observations

architecture.type: CLIP-style multi-modal contrastive fine-tuning framework with image encoder, text encoder, and location encoder
architecture.notes: WildSAT is a training framework rather than a single fixed backbone. It can be applied to ResNet50, Swin, and ViT backbones. The paper evaluates multiple variants; for the largest/primary evaluated variant, a ViT-L/16 backbone is used in some experiments (e.g. SatMAE baseline). The framework aligns satellite images with Wikipedia text and location embeddings from SINR using contrastive losses, plus an image-image temporal/geometric augmentation contrastive term.

pretraining.method_details: WildSAT jointly aligns satellite images, species occurrence locations, environmental covariates, and Wikipedia text in a shared embedding space. It uses wildlife observation locations from citizen science as supervision and adds an image-image contrastive term across temporally distinct and geometrically augmented satellite views of the same location. It can train from scratch or continually pretrain existing satellite and non-satellite models.
pretraining.objective: contrastive alignment of image-text-location embeddings plus image-image temporal/geometric contrastive learning

pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global, but overrepresented in the United States and Europe

key_contribution: The paper introduces WildSAT, a multi-modal contrastive framework that learns satellite image representations from geo-tagged wildlife observations, species-related Wikipedia text, and location/environment context. It shows that wildlife-derived supervision improves both randomly initialized and strong pretrained remote sensing models across classification, segmentation, species encounter prediction, and enables zero-shot text-based satellite image retrieval.

title: A generalizable and accessible approach to machine learning with global satellite imagery

architecture.backbone: Random convolutional features (random kitchen sinks) with ridge regression
architecture.notes: Uses random convolutional features (RCFs) generated by convolving randomly sampled 3x3 RGB patches over images, followed by ReLU and average pooling; main experiments use K=8,192 features per image. Predictions are made with ridge regression on the precomputed features. The paper also interprets MOSAIKS as related to a two-layer CNN with untrained filters or an approximation to kernel ridge regression with a convolutional kernel.

pretraining.method_details: A one-time, task-agnostic featurization based on random kitchen sinks adapted to satellite imagery via random convolutional features. Patches are randomly sampled from training images before labels are used, convolved over each image, passed through ReLU, and average pooled to form fixed embeddings shared across tasks.
pretraining.objective: task-agnostic image encoding for downstream linear prediction

pretraining_data.num_images: 100,000 in the main US experiment; 423,476 realized samples in the global experiment
sensors: Google Static Maps daytime satellite imagery

downstream_datasets: Gridded Population of the World, Version 4
Rwanda development survey tasks from Head et al. 2017
Haiti development survey tasks from Head et al. 2017
Nepal development survey tasks from Head et al. 2017
key_contribution: The paper introduces MOSAIKS, a simple unsupervised satellite image featurization method based on random convolutional features that supports many downstream geospatial prediction tasks using only linear regression. It shows that a single shared embedding of global satellite imagery can achieve performance competitive with task-specific deep CNNs while being much faster, more accessible, and scalable across users and tasks.

title: CONTRASTIVE SELF-SUPERVISED DATA FUSION FOR SATELLITE IMAGERY

architecture.notes: Primary proposed method uses two separate ResNet-18 encoders for Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2, replacing SimCLR weight sharing with modality-specific encoders. Each branch has a 2-layer MLP projection head with ReLU, producing 128-dimensional latent vectors; downstream classification concatenates encoder features from both modalities and applies a linear layer.

pretraining.method_details: Augmentation-free contrastive self-supervised data fusion using spatially aligned multi-modal satellite image pairs from the same location as positives. Unlike standard SimCLR, it uses dedicated encoders and projection heads for each modality (Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 multispectral) and contrasts matched cross-sensor pairs against samples from other locations.
pretraining.objective: contrastive alignment of cross-modal representations for the same geo-location

key_contribution: The paper proposes augmentation-free contrastive self-supervised data fusion for remote sensing by aligning Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 representations from the same location. Its main method, D-SimCLR, learns strong multimodal encoders from unlabeled data and outperforms supervised fusion baselines, especially when labeled data is scarce.

title: Geographical Supervision Correction for Remote Sensing Representation Learning

architecture.type: encoder-only mean-teacher framework with projection head
architecture.notes: Uses a student-teacher (mean-teacher) setup. Both student and teacher share the same backbone structure; a single fully connected layer is used as the projection head during pretraining. A learnable 8x8 correction matrix adaptively corrects geographical supervision during joint optimization.

pretraining.method: geographical supervision-based pretraining with adaptive correction
pretraining.method_details: Pretrains on supervision derived from global land cover (GLC) products, then adaptively corrects noisy/deviated supervision caused by time/resolution mismatch using a learnable correction matrix. Jointly optimizes network parameters and correction matrix, with regularization from two priors: 'minor changes' and 'spatial aggregation'.
pretraining.objective: predict corrected geographical supervision (land-cover proportion vector) with MSE, plus student-teacher consistency and regularization losses

pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: mixed (remote sensing images at 0.8m, 2m, 16m; GLC products at 30m)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global for GLC products; Levir-KR coverage unclear

key_contribution: The paper proposes GeCo, a remote sensing representation learning method that corrects noisy geographical supervision derived from global land cover products during pretraining. Its main novelty is a jointly optimized correction matrix constrained by land-cover change and spatial aggregation priors, which improves transfer performance across classification, segmentation, cloud/snow detection, and object detection.

title: Self-Supervised Learning for Invariant Representations from Multi-Spectral and SAR Images

architecture.type: student-teacher distillation / Siamese encoder with BYOL-style predictor-projector
architecture.notes: Based on BYOL. Uses identical student and teacher encoders, with 2-layer MLP projection head and 2-layer MLP prediction head on the student side. Hidden projection size is 256 with batch normalization and ReLU. Teacher weights are updated by exponential moving average with decay 0.9. Two variants are trained: single-channel and three-channel.

pretraining.method_details: The key novelty is treating multi-spectral Sentinel-2 and SAR Sentinel-1 images as two implicit augmented views of the same location, instead of relying only on standard image augmentations. The paper studies both multi-modal MS-SAR pretraining and single-modality MS-only pretraining, and compares single-band versus three-band feature learning.
pretraining.objective: Alignment of positive pairs via BYOL loss between student prediction and teacher projection across MS and SAR views; effectively invariant representation learning across modalities

pretraining_data.num_images: 90K pairs (50% of 180,662 triplets); also a 150K-pair variant

key_contribution: The paper proposes RS-BYOL, a BYOL-based self-supervised remote sensing model that learns invariant representations by aligning multi-spectral and SAR views of the same location. It shows that remote-sensing-specific SSL pretraining, especially with joint MS-SAR data, outperforms ImageNet-based transfer and that even single-band pretraining can yield strong downstream performance.

title: RingMo-Aerial: An Aerial Remote Sensing Foundation Model With Affine Transformation Contrastive Learning

architecture.notes: Built on RingMo with a Swin Transformer backbone. Introduces Frequency-Enhanced Multi-Head Self-Attention (FE-MSA), which adds a residual depthwise 7x7 convolution before window/self-attention to enhance small-object and high-frequency feature modeling. Uses an ARS-Adapter for parameter-efficient downstream fine-tuning; adapter tuning updates about 0.41% of parameters.

pretraining.method_details: Stage 1 uses SimMIM-style masked image modeling for pixel-level semantic association learning. Stage 2 adds an affine transformation-based contrastive learning framework (CLAF), where perspective/affine transforms create positive pairs that magnify distant small objects and encourage view/scale-invariant aerial representations.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction and contrastive alignment of original vs affine-transformed aerial images

datasets: custom aerial remote sensing pretraining dataset compiled from multiple collected open-source airborne remote sensing datasets

key_contribution: The paper proposes RingMo-Aerial, presented as the first aerial remote sensing foundation model, tailored to aerial imagery with oblique views, scale variation, and dense small objects. Its main innovations are FE-MSA for frequency-enhanced small-object representation, affine transformation contrastive pretraining (CLAF) for aerial viewpoint adaptation, and ARS-Adapter for efficient fine-tuning across diverse downstream ARS tasks.

title: RingMo-lite: A Remote Sensing Multi-task Lightweight Network with CNN-Transformer Hybrid Framework

architecture.notes: The encoder uses a dual-branch High-Low Frequency Information Fusion Block (FIFB) with a Swin Transformer low-frequency branch and a CNN high-frequency branch. It follows a 4-stage hierarchical design with block configuration (2, 2, 6, 2), patch size 4x4, window size 7, embed dim 96, and stage dimensions 96/192/384/768. Different task-specific decoder heads are used for classification, object detection, semantic segmentation, and change detection.

pretraining.method_details: The paper proposes Frequency-Domain Masked Image Modeling (FD-MIM), which analyzes randomly selected image patches in the frequency domain using DFT, separates them into high-frequency and low-frequency patches, applies high-pass/low-pass filtering accordingly, and then performs random pixel masking to improve reconstruction of remote sensing details and match the hybrid CNN-Transformer frequency-aware encoder.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction with L1 regression loss

datasets: custom visible remote sensing image dataset

key_contribution: The paper introduces RingMo-lite, a lightweight remote sensing foundation model that combines CNN and Swin Transformer branches to explicitly model high-frequency local details and low-frequency global context. It also proposes FD-MIM, a frequency-domain masked image modeling pretraining strategy, enabling strong multi-task performance across classification, detection, segmentation, and change detection while reducing parameters by over 60% relative to RingMo.

title: SELF-SUPERVISED VISION TRANSFORMERS FOR JOINT SAR-OPTICAL REPRESENTATION LEARNING

architecture.notes: Based on DINO with a ViT encoder backbone and a 3-layer MLP projection head (hidden dimension 2048), followed by L2 normalization and a weight-normalized fully connected layer of dimension 65,536. Input is formed by concatenating SAR and optical channels.

pretraining.method_details: Extends DINO to multimodal SAR-optical learning by concatenating Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 channels and applying a proposed RandomSensorDrop augmentation that randomly masks SAR channels, optical channels, or neither. This lets the model learn from SAR-only, optical-only, and joint SAR-optical views, enabling both intra- and inter-modality representation learning.
pretraining.objective: Contrastive/self-distillation alignment of augmented views using cross-entropy between teacher and student outputs

key_contribution: The paper proposes DINO-MM, a multimodal self-supervised learning method for joint SAR-optical representation learning using vision transformers. Its key innovation is RandomSensorDrop, which enables a single pretrained model to learn robust representations from SAR-only, optical-only, or fused inputs and improves transfer even to single-modality downstream tasks.

title: Self-supervised Audiovisual Representation Learning for Remote Sensing Data
model_name: Self-supervised audiovisual representation learning with Batch Triplet Loss

architecture.notes: Modality-specific dual encoders map aerial images and audio log-mel spectrograms into a shared embedding space. Visual encoder evaluated with ResNet-18 and ResNet-50; audio encoder uses ResNet-18. Global average pooling and a final fully connected layer produce single-vector embeddings.

pretraining.method_details: The method uses paired geo-tagged overhead imagery and co-located field recordings, training image and audio encoders to align corresponding pairs in a shared embedding space. Its main novelty is the use of remote-sensing audiovisual correspondence and a proposed batch-wise triplet loss that uses all pairings within a batch.
pretraining.objective: image-audio embedding alignment with Batch Triplet Loss

key_contribution: The paper introduces SoundingEarth, a large-scale dataset of 50K+ co-located aerial image and audio pairs, and proposes a self-supervised audiovisual pretraining framework for remote sensing. By aligning image and audio embeddings with a batch triplet loss, the learned visual backbones outperform common initialization and prior self-supervised baselines on multiple remote sensing downstream tasks.

title: SpectralGPT: Spectral Remote Sensing Foundation Model

architecture.notes: MAE-style asymmetric transformer with a 3D generative pretrained transformer design for spectral remote sensing. Uses 3D tensor tokens (e.g. 8x8x3), 3D masking over spatial-spectral cubes, separate spatial and spectral positional embeddings, a transformer encoder over visible tokens, and a lightweight transformer decoder with multi-target reconstruction. Model variants include Base (~86M), Large (307M), and Huge (632M); primary/largest variant is Huge.

pretraining.method_details: Novel spectral foundation pretraining with 3D spatial-spectral masking, 3D token generation for spatial-spectral coupling, multi-target reconstruction to preserve spectrally sequential patterns, and progressive pretraining across datasets with varying image sizes, regions, and characteristics.
pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction with token-to-token and spectral-to-spectral reconstruction losses

pretraining_data.num_images: about 1.47M total available; pretraining uses 712,874 fMoW-S2 images and 354,196 BigEarthNet-S2 images
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global for fMoW-S2 and Europe for BigEarthNet-S2

key_contribution: The paper introduces SpectralGPT, described as the first remote sensing foundation model purpose-built for spectral data, using a 3D MAE-like generative transformer that explicitly models spatial-spectral coupling and spectral sequentiality. Trained progressively on over one million Sentinel-2 spectral images, it achieves strong gains across scene classification, semantic segmentation, and change detection.

title: Geographical Knowledge-driven Representation Learning for Remote Sensing Images

architecture.notes: Mean-teacher framework with two identical networks (student and teacher) using ResNet50 or VGG16 as encoder, plus a single fully-connected projection head. Teacher weights updated via moving average from student.

pretraining.method_details: Uses GlobeLand30 global land cover product and image geolocation as supervision. For each remote sensing image, the matching land cover map is generated from GlobeLand30 and converted into a knowledge representation vector (proportions of land cover classes) used as supervision. A mean-teacher framework with student and teacher networks is trained by aligning network outputs with the knowledge representation via KL divergence loss, which mitigates label noise from time/resolution discrepancies between images and the land cover product.
pretraining.objective: KL divergence between predicted distribution and land-cover knowledge representation, plus KL between student and teacher outputs

pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global (Gaofen series acquisitions, GlobeLand30 global land cover)

key_contribution: Proposes GeoKR, a novel pre-training paradigm for remote sensing that uses global land cover products (GlobeLand30) and image geolocation as weak geographical-knowledge supervision instead of manual labels. Introduces a mean-teacher framework to suppress noise from time/resolution discrepancies between images and land cover maps, and constructs Levir-KR, a 1.43M image multi-resolution Gaofen pre-training dataset. Demonstrates improvements over ImageNet pre-training and self-supervised baselines (MoCo/SimCLR/BYOL) on multiple downstream RS tasks.

title: An Empirical Study of Remote Sensing Pretraining

architecture.notes: Evaluates ResNet-50 (CNN), Swin-T (transformer), and ViTAEv2-S (hybrid CNN+transformer with WMHSA); ViTAEv2-S is the primary/best model with ~18.8M params.

pretraining.method_details: Supervised classification pretraining on MillionAID scene labels, mirroring conventional ImageNet pretraining. Models trained from scratch for 40/100/120/300 epochs with AdamW, cosine LR schedule, AutoAugment, Random Erasing, Mixup, CutMix, color jitter.
pretraining.objective: Scene classification (cross-entropy)

key_contribution: First systematic empirical study of supervised remote sensing pretraining on the large-scale MillionAID dataset across CNN (ResNet-50), transformer (Swin-T), and hybrid (ViTAEv2-S) backbones, demonstrating RSP outperforms ImageNet pretraining on aerial scene tasks and that ViTAEv2-S achieves the best results across recognition, segmentation, detection, and change detection.

title: Advancing Plain Vision Transformer Towards Remote Sensing Foundation Model

architecture.backbone: Plain ViT-B and ViTAE-B with Rotated Varied-Size Attention (RVSA)
architecture.notes: Two backbones (~100M params each): plain ViT-B and ViTAE-B. During finetuning, MHSA layers are replaced by RVSA modules (full attention retained at 1/4 depth layers, similar to ViTDet). Patch size 16, embedding dim 768, 12 heads, depth 12.
