pretraining.method_details: Unsupervised MIM pretraining following original MAE recipe with normalized pixel loss. Mask ratio 0.75, 1600 epochs, batch size 2048 on 8 A100 GPUs, AdamW optimizer. Pretrained on MillionAID without using labels.
pretraining.objective: Masked image reconstruction (pixel reconstruction in normalized pixel space)

key_contribution: First work to pretrain plain ViT (~100M params) as an RS foundation model via MAE on MillionAID, and proposes Rotated Varied-Size Attention (RVSA) which adds a learnable rotation mechanism to varied-size window attention to better handle arbitrarily oriented objects in remote sensing imagery, achieving SOTA 81.24% mAP on DOTA-V1.0.

title: Consecutive Pre-Training: A Knowledge Transfer Learning Strategy with Relevant Unlabeled Data for Remote Sensing Domain

architecture.notes: ViT-B/L encoder with lightweight transformer decoder (depth=8) for MIM pretraining; encoder used as backbone for downstream fine-tuning

pretraining.method_details: Consecutive self-supervised pretraining: (1) MIM-based MAE pretraining on large-scale natural scene dataset (ImageNet-1K), then (2) further MIM-based self-supervised pretraining on relevant unlabeled remote sensing data to bridge domain gap, then fine-tune on downstream tasks. 75% random masking, 16x16 patches, L1 reconstruction loss on masked pixels.
pretraining.objective: Masked image reconstruction (L1 pixel loss on masked patches)

pretraining_data.num_images: 1.28M (ImageNet-1K) + remote sensing data for further pretraining

key_contribution: Proposes ConSecutive PreTraining (CSPT), a knowledge transfer learning strategy that does not stop pretraining: first MIM-based self-supervised pretraining on large-scale natural scene data (ImageNet), then continued self-supervised pretraining on relevant unlabeled remote sensing data to gradually bridge the domain gap, mitigate task-aware discrepancy, and unlock the potential of unlabeled RS data without expensive labeling.

title: Towards Geospatial Foundation Models via Continual Pretraining

architecture.notes: Swin Transformer Base initialized via continual pretraining from ImageNet-22k weights; teacher-student framework with frozen ImageNet-22k teacher and randomly initialized student

pretraining.method_details: Teacher-student paradigm: frozen ImageNet-22k pretrained Swin-B teacher provides intermediate features (at stage 3) to guide a randomly initialized student via cosine similarity feature distillation, while the student is also trained with masked image modeling (MIM) on GeoPile. Total loss = L_MIM + L_feat. Pretrained on 8 V100 GPUs, batch size 2048, image size 192x192, 100 epochs.
pretraining.objective: Masked image modeling (L1 pixel reconstruction) + feature distillation (cosine similarity to teacher features)

key_contribution: Proposes a multi-objective continual pretraining paradigm that leverages ImageNet-22k representations as a teacher (via feature distillation) combined with masked image modeling on a curated diverse remote sensing dataset (GeoPile, ~600k images). Achieves SOTA across 7 geospatial downstream tasks while requiring substantially less compute and carbon footprint (8x reduction vs SatMAE).

title: SwiMDiff: Scene-wide Matching Contrastive Learning with Diffusion Constraint for Remote Sensing Image

architecture.notes: Dual-branch momentum-updated CL encoders (MoCo-v2 style) with 2-layer MLP projection head, plus a U-Net diffusion network (DDPM) with encoder f_n and decoder g_n as auxiliary branch

pretraining.method_details: Combines scene-wide matching contrastive learning (built on MoCo-v2) with adaptive soft relabeling of false negatives from the same scene, jointly trained with an auxiliary DDPM diffusion constraint that predicts pixel-level noise to capture fine-grained features
pretraining.objective: Joint loss L = lambda_C * L_C (scene-wide matching contrastive loss) + lambda_D * L_D (diffusion noise prediction L2 loss), with lambda_C=1 and lambda_D=10

pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global (Sen12MS subset, 256x256 tiles sampled from each season)

key_contribution: First framework to integrate a diffusion model as an auxiliary pixel-level constraint with contrastive learning for RS SSL, combined with a scene-wide matching strategy that adaptively relabels false negatives from the same geographic scene to improve both global semantic and fine-grained representation learning.

title: Predicting Gradient is Better: Exploring Self-Supervised Learning for SAR ATR with a Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture

architecture.notes: ViT-B encoder with DNN predictor (LoMaR-SAR variant using 8-layer MAE decoder); target encoder uses multi-scale gradient-by-ratio (GR) features with convolutional kernels of sizes r=5,9,13,17

pretraining.method: Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture (JEPA) with Masked Image Modeling
pretraining.method_details: Local masked patches (LoMaR-style) predict multi-scale SAR gradient-by-ratio (GR) features of unseen patches in feature space. Combines MIM and physics-guided contrastive architecture (PGCA) ideas. ViT-B encoder with mask tokens and relative positional encoding; 8-layer ViT decoder/predictor. Target features computed via ratio-of-average (ROA) gradient at 4 scales.
pretraining.objective: MSE between predicted DNN features and multi-scale SAR gradient features for masked local patches

pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global / multiple scenes (ground, ocean, airports, harbors, nearshore, islands, distant seas, urban) plus simulated

downstream_tasks: Few-shot fine-grained SAR target classification (vehicle, ship, aircraft)
key_contribution: First joint-embedding predictive architecture for SAR ATR that uses multi-scale SAR gradient-by-ratio features as high-quality target signals to suppress speckle noise, combined with local masking (LoMaR) for small targets in remote sensing; demonstrates SSL scalability for building a SAR foundation model unified across targets, scenes, and sensors.

title: SkySense: A Multi-Modal Remote Sensing Foundation Model Towards Universal Interpretation for Earth Observation Imagery

architecture.backbone: Swin-H (HSROI) + ViT-L (TMsI) + ViT-L (TSARI) spatial encoders with Multi-Modal Temporal Fusion Transformer
architecture.type: Hybrid (Swin Transformer + ViT) factorized multi-modal spatiotemporal encoder
architecture.notes: Billion-scale model with 2.06B parameters; factorized multi-modal spatiotemporal encoder performing spatial feature extraction independently per modality, then multi-modal temporal fusion; modular design allowing flexible single- or multi-modal use

pretraining.method: Multi-Granularity Contrastive Learning (MGCL) + Cross-Modal Alignment (CMA) + Unsupervised Geo-Context Prototype Learning (GCPL)
pretraining.method_details: Teacher-student framework (BYOL-style with EMA updates) using only positive pairs. Multi-Granularity Contrastive Learning at pixel-, object-, and image-level across HSROI, TMsI, SAR modalities and fused features. Cross-Modal Alignment via multi-modal contrastive loss between modalities of same geo-location. Unsupervised Geo-Context Prototype Learning divides globe into 4096 regions with 100 prototypes each, learned via Sinkhorn-Knopp clustering on multi-modal features. Trained on 80 A100-80GB GPUs with batch size 240.
pretraining.objective: Combined loss L = alpha * L_MGCL + beta * L_align (multi-granularity contrastive + cross-modal alignment), with geo-context prototype learning incorporated implicitly via EMA updates

datasets: Curated multi-modal RSI dataset (HSROIs from WorldView-3, TMsI from Sentinel-2, TSARI from Sentinel-1)
pretraining_data.num_images: 21.5 million temporal sequences (each sample: 1 static HSROI + 20 Sentinel-2 multispectral images + 10 Sentinel-1 SAR images)
modalities: Multispectral optical time series (TMsI; B2-8, B8A, B11-12)
SAR time series (TSARI; VV, VH polarization)
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: High-resolution (WorldView-3, sub-meter) for HSROI; ~10m for Sentinel-2 TMsI; ~10m for Sentinel-1 TSARI
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global; globe partitioned into 4096 regions covering diverse scenarios across resolution, spectrum, and imaging mechanism

key_contribution: Largest (2.06B params) multi-modal remote sensing foundation model to date, featuring a factorized multi-modal spatiotemporal encoder, Multi-Granularity Contrastive Learning across pixel/object/image levels, and unsupervised Geo-Context Prototype Learning for region-aware representations. Achieves SOTA on 16 datasets across 7 tasks, outperforming 18 prior RSFMs.

title: Semantic-aware Dense Representation Learning for Remote Sensing Image Change Detection

architecture.notes: ResNet-18 backbone (without FC layers and global pooling) equipped with Feature Pyramid Network; output feature map at 1/4 spatial resolution with channel dimension C=256. 2-layer MLP projector (hidden 2048, output 1024) and 2-layer MLP predictor (hidden 256, output 1024).

pretraining.method_details: SimSiam-style Siamese self-supervised framework with semantic supervision. Generates three views per image (two via color/geometry augmentations, one via background swap with color transfer + context-aware blending). Performs class-balanced point sampling within the overlapped region using semantic masks, then enforces point-level cross-view similarity (negative cosine similarity with stop-gradient) between views 1/2 and views 1/3 plus a semantic dissimilarity loss pushing foreground vs. background point embeddings apart.
pretraining.objective: Combined loss: semantic dissimilar loss (L_sd) + cross-view similarity losses (L_s1 between view 1/2, L_s2 between view 1/3 for foreground)

pretraining_data.num_images: ~45k 256x256 patches cut from 180 images of size 5000x5000 (building/non-building masks)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Cities covered by Inria dataset (e.g., multiple urban regions across US and Europe)

key_contribution: Proposes SaDL, a semantic-aware dense self-supervised pre-training method for remote sensing change detection that combines class-balanced point sampling with semantic masks, point-level cross-view consistency, semantic dissimilarity loss, and a background-swap third view, yielding spatially-sensitive and foreground-discriminative features that significantly improve data efficiency on LEVIR-CD, WHU-CD, and Guangzhou-CD.

title: HyperSIGMA: Hyperspectral Intelligence Comprehension Foundation Model

architecture.backbone: ViT (Vision Transformer) with dual subnetworks (SpatViT spatial + SpecViT spectral) using Sparse Sampling Attention (SSA) and Spectral Enhancement Module (SEM)
architecture.notes: Two-branch architecture: spatial subnetwork (SpatViT) processes spatial patches and spectral subnetwork (SpecViT) processes spectral channel tokens. Full SA replaced with Sparse Sampling Attention (SSA) in most layers. Features fused via Spectral Enhancement Module (SEM). Scaled to over 1 billion parameters using ViT-Huge backbone for both subnetworks. Base/Large/Huge variants available.

pretraining.method_details: MAE-based self-supervised pre-training applied separately to both spatial and spectral subnetworks. Spatial mask ratio R_spat=0.75 and spectral mask ratio R_spec=0.75. Patch size P=8 for spatial ViT (64 tokens). Random channel selection used during preprocessing to handle varying channel counts. Pre-trained for 1,600 epochs on NVIDIA V100 GPUs.
pretraining.objective: Reconstruction of masked patches/spectral tokens

pretraining_data.num_images: ~450K hyperspectral images (447,072 HSI patches: 247,072 EO-1 + 200,000 GF-5), equivalent to over 20 million trispectral images
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global (EO-1 acquired 2011-2017 worldwide; GF-5 covering China)

key_contribution: First billion-parameter foundation model specifically designed for hyperspectral image (HSI) interpretation, introducing Sparse Sampling Attention (SSA) and Spectral Enhancement Module (SEM) within a dual spatial-spectral ViT architecture, pre-trained on the newly constructed HyperGlobal-450K dataset (largest global HSI dataset). Provides a unified solution for both high-level and low-level hyperspectral tasks.

title: RingMoE: Mixture-of-Modality-Experts Multi-Modal Foundation Models for Universal Remote Sensing Image Interpretation

architecture.notes: Sparse MoE architecture with modal-specialized, collaborative, and shared experts (RMoE layer replaces FFN in each Swin v2 block). Supports dynamic expert pruning to produce sub-models: RingMoE-Opt (4.3B), RingMoE-EP (4.3B), and dense 1B variants (RingMoE-KS, RingMoE-KA, RingMoE-KC). Largest RSFM to date at 14.7B parameters.

pretraining.method: Masked Image Modeling (MIM) with physics-informed self-supervised learning
pretraining.method_details: Random masking of multi-modal patches; modal-specific decoders reconstruct original targets. Pixel reconstruction for Opt/MS/SAR-L2 and power reconstruction (physics-informed) for complex-valued SAR-L1. Load-balance loss for collaborative and modal-specialized experts.
pretraining.objective: Masked reconstruction loss (pixel reconstruction + power reconstruction for SAR-L1) combined with expert load-balance loss

key_contribution: First sparse Mixture-of-Experts foundation model for remote sensing, scaled to 14.7B parameters across four modalities (Opt, MS, SAR-L1, SAR-L2). Introduces modal-specialized, collaborative, and shared experts (RMoE), physics-informed SSL with power reconstruction for complex-valued SAR, and dynamic expert pruning for efficient deployment (down to 1B). Achieves 23 SOTAs across 25 benchmarks on six RS tasks.

title: RingMo-Agent: A Unified Remote Sensing Foundation Model for Multi-Platform and Multi-Modal Reasoning

architecture.backbone: SigLIP-SO400M-384 visual encoder + DeepSeekMoE (3B) LLM with MLP projector and trajectory decoder
architecture.notes: Visual encoder based on SigLIP with three modality-specific patch embedding layers (optical, SAR, IR) projecting to 1152-dim space; frozen MLP projector (DeepSeek-VL2 style with 2x2 shuffling); LLM is 3B DeepSeekMoE variant from DeepSeek-VL2 fine-tuned with LoRA (rank 64, scaling 16) on Q/V projections; trainable GRU-based trajectory decoder for long-horizon spatial tasks via <trajectory> token.

pretraining.method: Two-stage training: vision-language generation stage then instruction-tuning stage
pretraining.method_details: Stage 1 (vision-language generation): adapts model to RS domain using paired image-text data from classification, captioning, and detection datasets, optimized via generative LLM loss; GPT-4 used to expand annotations. Stage 2 (instruction tuning): optimized to follow instructions across 8 RS tasks. Training on 8x A100 80GB GPUs with AdamW; loss combines cross-entropy text loss and MSE trajectory loss (lambda_txt=1, lambda_mse=5).
pretraining.objective: Generative language modeling (cross-entropy) + MSE trajectory regression

pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: variable; images dynamically tiled to multiples of 384
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global; satellite and UAV platforms over diverse urban and natural scenes

key_contribution: First unified RS vision-language foundation model handling multi-platform (satellite + UAV) and multi-modal (optical, SAR, IR) data across 8 perception and reasoning tasks, supported by the new 3M-pair RS-VL3M dataset, modality-specific embedding layers to mitigate cross-modal interference, and a token-based high-dimensional hidden state trajectory decoder enabling long-horizon spatial reasoning such as aerial navigation.

title: RS-DFM: A Remote Sensing Distributed Foundation Model for Diverse Downstream Tasks

architecture.notes: Distributed foundation model with Swin-Tiny encoder, Generalized BEV Generation (GBG) module, and High-Low Frequency Decoupled Collaboration (HLFDC) module for multi-platform collaborative perception

pretraining.method_details: Trained with AdamW optimizer (lr 2e-4), batch size 8, 20 epochs on four NVIDIA A40 GPUs. Uses BEVDET for 3D detection, Fiery for BEV instance segmentation, PowerBEV for trajectory prediction as downstream heads.
pretraining.objective: Multi-task collaborative perception loss across detection, segmentation, and trajectory prediction

pretraining_data.num_images: 38,000 synchronous images (170 training scenes, 30 validation scenes)
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: 1600x900 pixels per image; collaborative region ~150m x 150m
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: CARLA simulated urban scenes (33 locations from high-quality simulation maps)

key_contribution: A distributed foundation model for multi-platform (multi-UAV) collaborative remote sensing perception, introducing a Generalized BEV Generation module that uses geometric priors with relative depth estimation to unify features across altitudes/perspectives, and a High-Low Frequency Decoupled Collaboration module for bandwidth-efficient inter-platform feature transmission, along with the AirCo-MultiTasks simulation dataset.

title: HSIGene: A Foundation Model for Hyperspectral Image Generation

architecture.backbone: Latent Diffusion Model (UNet) with ControlNet encoder and VAE
architecture.notes: Based on CRS-Diff framework; consists of a pre-trained VAE encoder, UNet diffusion backbone, and ControlNet encoder for multi-condition control. Also includes a two-stage super-resolution framework: DSRNet (diffusion-based RGB super-resolution) and RGAN (Rectangular Guided Attention Network) for HSI super-resolution. UNet initialized from CRS-Diff pretrained weights.

pretraining.method: Latent Diffusion (DDPM training with DDIM sampling)
pretraining.method_details: Trained for 100k iterations using AdamW optimizer with learning rate 1e-5 and batch size 16. Forward diffusion process gradually adds noise to latent representations; model trained to predict noise via MSE loss. Supports six control conditions: HED (holistically-nested edge detection), segmentation, sketch, MLSD (multiscale line segment detection), content (CLIP image embeddings), and text. Conditions integrated via ControlNet encoder with zero-convolution modules.
pretraining.objective: Noise prediction (MSE between predicted and added Gaussian noise) in latent space for controllable HSI generation

pretraining_data.num_images: ~7k training patches (256x256, stride 128) plus ~40k augmented patches via super-resolution; ~55k high-resolution RGB images from Google Earth Engine for DSRNet training
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: 0.5m to 2.5m GSD (Xiongan 0.5m, Chikusei 2.5m, DFC2013 2.5m, DFC2018 1m, Heihe 1m)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: China (Xiongan, Heihe), Japan (Chikusei), and DFC2013/DFC2018 benchmark scenes; spectral range 380-1018nm aligned to 400-1000nm with 48 bands

key_contribution: First and largest HSI foundation model for hyperspectral image generation based on latent diffusion, supporting multi-condition composable control (HED, segmentation, sketch, MLSD, content, text). Introduces a novel two-stage super-resolution data augmentation framework (DSRNet + RGAN with rectangular guided attention) to alleviate HSI scarcity while preserving spectral fidelity, enabling generation of high-quality synthetic HSIs that improve downstream denoising and super-resolution performance.

title: RS-vHeat: Heat Conduction Guided Efficient Remote Sensing Foundation Model

architecture.backbone: vHeat-B with Heat Conduction Operator (HCO) and Spatial Correction Embeddings (SCEs)
architecture.type: Heat-conduction-based visual encoder (Swin-B-style 4-stage hierarchy with 2,2,18,2 blocks)
architecture.notes: Multi-modal (optical + SAR) encoder using HCO with O(N^1.5) complexity and global receptive field; learnable Frequency Value Embeddings (FVEs) predict heat diffusion coefficients; Spatial Correction Embeddings adjust object boundaries

pretraining.method: Self-supervised frequency domain hierarchical masking with multi-domain reconstruction and contrastive learning
pretraining.method_details: Inputs split via DCT into high/low-frequency components with sector-region masking (rate 20-30%); dual-stream optical and SAR pipelines feed heat-conduction-based encoder; multi-domain decoders reconstruct in spatial and frequency domains; contrastive loss across modalities
pretraining.objective: Combined loss: spatial domain reconstruction (L1) + frequency domain reconstruction (L1) + contrastive loss (L_total = L_Con + L_Spa + L_Fre)

datasets: RingMo-style multi-modal dataset (450k matched optical-SAR pairs, ~3M total entries)

key_contribution: First remote sensing foundation model based on heat conduction theory, using a Heat Conduction Operator with O(N^1.5) complexity and global receptive field; combined with frequency-domain hierarchical masking and multi-domain reconstruction self-supervised pretraining on multi-modal optical+SAR data, achieving 84% memory reduction, 24% FLOPs reduction, and 2.7x throughput improvement over attention-based RSFMs while matching or exceeding SOTA accuracy across 4 tasks and 10 datasets

title: Can Generative Geospatial Diffusion Models Excel as Discriminative Geospatial Foundation Models?

architecture.backbone: DiffusionSat (Stable Diffusion v2-1 based Latent Diffusion Model with U-Net)
architecture.type: Latent Diffusion Model (U-Net with ResNet, self-attention, and cross-attention blocks)
architecture.notes: Built on DiffusionSat, a satellite-adapted LDM based on Stable Diffusion v2-1. Extracts multi-scale, multi-timestep features from the U-Net decoder (ResNet and self-attention outputs at timesteps {1, 100, 200}) and aggregates them via three proposed fusion strategies: Global Weighted Fusion, Localized Weighted Fusion, and Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) Joint Fusion (E=8 experts, top-k=2).

pretraining.method: Diffusion-based generative self-supervised learning (repurposed as SSL feature extractor)
pretraining.method_details: Leverages a pretrained generative diffusion model (DiffusionSat) as a frozen backbone; only SatDiFuser fusion components and task-specific decoders (linear head for classification, UPerNet for segmentation) are trained downstream. Multi-scale, multi-timestep features are obtained via DDIM inversion from clean latents.
pretraining.objective: Denoising diffusion (noise prediction) objective inherited from DiffusionSat / Stable Diffusion v2-1

datasets: Stable Diffusion v2-1 web-scraped data (for SD v2-1 backbone variant)
sensors: multi-spectral satellite (RGB bands used in SatDiFuser experiments)

key_contribution: First comprehensive adaptation of a large-scale diffusion-based generative model as a self-supervised geospatial foundation model for discriminative remote sensing tasks, introducing three multi-stage feature fusion strategies (global weighted, localized weighted, and mixture-of-experts) that enable diffusion features to rival or exceed state-of-the-art GFMs on GEO-Bench (gains up to +5.7% mIoU in segmentation and +7.9% F1 in classification).

title: CMID: A Unified Self-Supervised Learning Framework for Remote Sensing Image Understanding

architecture.notes: Teacher-student self-distillation with three branches (MIM, global CL, local CL). Reported with ResNet-50 (23.55M) and Swin-B (87.77M) backbones; ViT-B/ViTAE-B variants also discussed.

pretraining.method_details: Unified SSL framework combining contrastive learning (CL) with masked image modeling (MIM) via teacher-student self-distillation. Three branches: MIM branch (SimMIM-style with mean spectral value masking and learnable [MASK] token, using spatial L1 loss and focal frequency loss), global CL branch (MoCo-style InfoNCE on globally pooled features), and local CL branch (position-matched local prototype assignment consistency). Teacher updated via EMA of student.
pretraining.objective: L_spat (L1 spatial reconstruction) + L_freq (focal frequency loss) + L_NCE (global contrastive InfoNCE) + L_local (local prototype assignment consistency)

key_contribution: First unified SSL framework for remote sensing that combines contrastive learning (CL) with masked image modeling (MIM) via teacher-student self-distillation, learning representations that are simultaneously globally semantic separable and locally spatially perceptible. Architecture-agnostic (works with both CNNs and ViTs), enabling strong transfer across classification, segmentation, detection, and change detection.

title: Remote Sensing Vision-Language Foundation Models without Annotations via Ground Remote Alignment

architecture.notes: Satellite image encoder initialized from CLIP image encoder; trained to align with frozen CLIP. Two variants: image-level VLM and pixel-level VLM (patch-based ViT producing per-patch features).

pretraining.method: Ground Remote Alignment for Training (GRAFT) - contrastive alignment between satellite images and co-located internet ground images via frozen CLIP encoder
pretraining.method_details: Trains a satellite image encoder to align satellite embeddings with CLIP embeddings of co-located internet ground images, using ground images as an intermediary to transitively align satellite imagery with text. Uses a multi-positive contrastive loss accounting for multiple ground images per satellite image. Pixel-level model uses geotag-to-pixel mapping for sparse patch-level supervision.
pretraining.objective: Multi-positive contrastive loss (CLIP-style with multiple positives per satellite image)

pretraining_data.num_images: 10.2 million NAIP pairs and 8.7 million Sentinel-2 pairs

key_contribution: First large-scale vision-language foundation model for remote sensing trained without any textual annotations, by using co-located internet ground images as an intermediary between satellite imagery and language; achieves SOTA zero-shot classification, retrieval, segmentation, and VQA on satellite imagery, outperforming supervised VLMs by up to 20% on classification and 80% on segmentation.

title: DynamicVis: Dynamic Visual Perception for Efficient Remote Sensing Foundation Models

architecture.backbone: Dynamic Region-Aware State Space Model (SSM) with hierarchical multi-scale extractor and Feature Pyramid Network
architecture.type: State Space Model (Mamba-based) with adaptive token routing
architecture.notes: Two configurations: DynamicVis-Base (~186M) and DynamicVis-Large (~998M) per Table 12. Uses progressive small-stride patch merger (patch sizes [7,3,3,3], strides [4,2,2,2]), 4 hierarchical stages with Sparse Mixer blocks containing ATRIM (Adaptive Token Routing & Incremental Modeling) units with dual-path bidirectional Mamba scanning. Stage-wise token reduction ratios [7/8, 3/4, 1/2, 0]. FPN produces 5 multi-scale feature maps at d=256 channels.

pretraining.method_details: Treats each image as a 'bag' of instances with bounding box and categorical labels. Aligns regional visual embeddings (extracted via Generic RoI Extractor across all hierarchical scales) with categorical meta-embeddings initialized from pre-trained OpenCLIP text encoder using prompt templates (e.g., 'An aerial image of a [CLASS]'). Trained via MIL-NCE contrastive loss combined with classification cross-entropy. Gumbel noise injected into routing affinity scores, progressively annealed.
pretraining.objective: MIL-NCE contrastive loss + classification cross-entropy

datasets: fMoW (Functional Map of the World) - RGB version

key_contribution: A remote sensing foundation model explicitly tailored to spatial sparsity of RS imagery via a Dynamic Region-Aware SSM backbone that adaptively routes only task-relevant high-salience tokens through dual-path Mamba scanning while preserving background context via parameter-free integration. Combined with a novel Region-Level Meta-Embedding MIL pre-training paradigm, it processes 2048x2048 imagery with only 97 ms latency and 833 MB memory (~6% and 3% of a base ViT) while achieving SOTA on sparse-target tasks.

title: AlphaEarth Foundations: An embedding field model for accurate and efficient global mapping from sparse label data

architecture.backbone: Space Time Precision (STP) transformer with interleaved space (ViT-like spatial self-attention), time (time-axial self-attention), and precision (3x3 conv) operators; trio of teacher video embedding model with implicit decoders, student video embedding model, and text alignment model
architecture.type: Transformer (custom STP video encoder with implicit decoders)
architecture.notes: Trained ~1B and ~480M parameter variants; deployed the smaller 480M variant for inference efficiency. Outputs 64-byte embeddings (64-D) constrained to unit sphere S^63 via von Mises-Fisher distribution; quantized to 8 bits for release.

pretraining.method: Self-supervised contrastive video teacher-student with implicit decoder reconstruction, batch uniformity objective on S^63, and CLIP-style text alignment
pretraining.method_details: Teacher-student video embeddings share architecture; embeddings treated as mean direction of von Mises-Fisher distribution and decoded via implicit decoders conditioned on sensor metadata and timecode. Batch uniformity loss prevents collapse. Text alignment via CLIP-style contrastive loss between video student and text encoder using Wikipedia articles joined to GBIF species occurrences.
pretraining.objective: Implicit decoder reconstruction losses per source + batch uniformity (uniform distribution on S^63) + contrastive text-video alignment (f_CLIP)

pretraining_data.num_images: ~3 billion observations across nine gridded data sources and one unstructured text source, covering ~1.1% of Earth's land surface

downstream_tasks: Change detection (land cover change, land use change)
key_contribution: First EO featurization model with continuous-time conditioning that fuses multi-source/multi-modal spatial-temporal data via a Space Time Precision (STP) architecture, producing compact 64-byte 10 m global embedding fields. AEF consistently outperforms designed and learned featurization baselines (~23.9% mean error reduction) across 15 sparse-label evaluations and is released as annual global embedding layers (2017-2024) on Google Earth Engine.

title: TiMo: Spatiotemporal Foundation Model for Satellite Image Time Series

architecture.backbone: Hierarchical Vision Transformer (Swin-style, 4 stages) with Differential Spatiotemporal Gyroscope Attention (D-STGA) in early stages and MHSA in deeper stages
architecture.notes: Three variants: TiMo-Base (91M), TiMo-Large (298M), TiMo-Huge (675M). Replaces MHSA with novel Spatiotemporal Gyroscope Attention (STGA / D-STGA) in the first two stages to capture spatiotemporal correlations among tokens sharing spatial or temporal positions.

pretraining.method: Masked Image Modeling (Hiera-style MAE adapted to hierarchical ViT)
pretraining.method_details: Follows Hiera's MAE-like approach for hierarchical architectures with mask unit of 1x32x32 pixels (sparser temporal masking suited for SITS). Uses MHSA in all blocks during pre-training. Random temporal sampling of 3 frames per iteration from 10-timestamp sequences.
pretraining.objective: Reconstruction of masked spatiotemporal patches

pretraining_data.num_images: ~1,000,000 Sentinel-2 SITS samples from 100,000 geographic locations across 1,317 cities, each with 10 temporal phases over 5 years (2017-2022)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global, primarily Europe, North Africa, and West Asia (1,317 cities ranked among the 10,000 most populous globally)

key_contribution: A hierarchical vision transformer foundation model for satellite image time series, introducing Spatiotemporal Gyroscope Attention (STGA) and its differential variant (D-STGA) that compute attention only among tokens sharing the same spatial or temporal coordinate to efficiently capture multiscale spatiotemporal relationships, pretrained via masked image modeling on the new MillionST dataset (1M Sentinel-2 SITS from 100k locations x 10 timestamps).

title: CGEarthEye: A High-Resolution Remote Sensing Vision Foundation Model Based on the Jilin-1 Satellite Constellation

architecture.notes: Five backbone variants (ViT-S 22M, ViT-B 86M, ViT-L 307M, ViT-H 632M, ViT-G 1100M), totaling 2.1B parameters across all variants. Teacher-student framework with EMA momentum update.

pretraining.method_details: Multi-granularity SSL framework integrating augmentation-aware contrastive learning, seasonal alignment contrastive learning, and masked patch token contrastive learning. Teacher-student architecture inspired by DINOv2; 2 global crops, 8 local crops, 3 seasonal views, plus masked variants (15 augmented variants per image). EMA momentum 0.992. Trained 150 days on 16 NVIDIA A800 GPUs.
pretraining.objective: L = L_classtoken + L_classtoken_season + L_patch (cross-entropy contrastive losses)

pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global (8.06M quarterly samples from 2.015M Chinese locations + 7.985M annual mosaic samples from non-Chinese locations, 2023)

key_contribution: First high-resolution (sub-meter) remote sensing vision foundation model trained on the Jilin-1 satellite constellation, introducing JLSSD—the first 15-million-scale globally distributed multi-temporal SSL dataset with quarterly sampling—and a multi-granularity SSL framework combining augmentation-aware, seasonal, and masked patch contrastive learning, achieving SOTA across 10 high-resolution benchmarks under frozen-backbone fine-tuning.

title: MAPEX: Modality-Aware Pruning of Experts for Remote Sensing Foundation Models

architecture.notes: Pre-trained mixture-of-modality experts encoder with 6 experts (~360M params); after modality-aware pruning yields specialized models (~130M with 2 experts per modality, ~200M with 2 experts + 1 shared). 8-block transformer decoder for reconstruction. Includes learnable [MODALITY] tokens, modality-conditioned token routing, and load-balancing loss.

pretraining.method: Masked Image Modeling (MAE) with Mixture-of-Modality Experts
pretraining.method_details: Multi-modal masked autoencoding with 75% masking per modality, modality-wise decoding (separate decoder forward passes per modality), modality-conditioned token routing in MoE layers, modality dropout (50%), and a differentiable load-balancing loss. Trained 50 epochs with AdamW.
pretraining.objective: Mean-squared error pixel reconstruction of masked patches per modality

key_contribution: Introduces a multi-modal pre-training approach using a mixture-of-modality experts ViT with modality-conditioned token routing, plus a modality-aware expert pruning technique that turns a large multi-modal foundation model into smaller, efficient modality-specific models tailored to downstream tasks.

title: SkySense V2: A Unified Foundation Model for Multi-modal Remote Sensing

architecture.backbone: Unified transformer encoder with Swin Transformer V2 Blocks (SwinV2B) in stages 1-2 and vanilla Transformer Blocks in stages 3-4, with Adaptive Patch Merging (APM) modules and Mixture of Experts (MoE)
architecture.notes: Single shared backbone across HR optical, MS, and SAR modalities (except distinct tokenizers); integrates APM for resolution harmonization, modality-specific learnable prompt tokens, and MoE (L=6, M=8, k=1) in last L transformer layers. Token dimension C=352, window size 8 in first two stages.

pretraining.method: Self-supervised multi-modal contrastive learning with teacher-student EMA framework
pretraining.method_details: Teacher-student architecture (EMA) with Multi-Granularity Contrastive Learning (MGCL), Geo-Context Prototype Learning (GCPL), Image-Text Alignment (ITA) using OpenStreetMap labels, and novel Query-based Semantic Aggregation Contrastive Learning (QSACL). Trained for 600k iterations on 128 H20 GPUs with batch size 1024, AdamW optimizer, lr 2e-4 cosine annealed to 1e-6.
pretraining.objective: Weighted sum of MGCL, ITA, and QSACL losses

pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: HR optical 2048x2048; Sentinel-2 and Sentinel-1 64x64 (avg 65 S2 images and SAR sequences per set)

key_contribution: A unified MM-RSFM that uses a single transformer backbone (665M params) to process HR optical, MS, and SAR modalities simultaneously, with novel Adaptive Patch Merging for resolution harmonization, modality-specific learnable prompt tokens, MoE scaling, and a new Query-based Semantic Aggregation Contrastive Learning (QSACL) SSL strategy tailored to RS images' multi-subject semantic distribution; outperforms SkySense by 1.8 points on average across 16 datasets and 7 tasks.

title: TESSERA: Temporal Embeddings of Surface Spectra for Earth Representation and Analysis

architecture.backbone: Dual-branch Transformer encoder (4-layer multi-head self-attention) with GRU pooling and MLP fusion
architecture.notes: Dual-branch encoder processes Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 d-pixels separately; each branch uses linear projection + Day-of-Year positional encoding + 4-layer Transformer + GRU pooling to 128-D; outputs concatenated and fused via 2-layer MLP to 128-D fused embedding; quantization-aware training compresses to int8; deep projector MLP expands to 16,384-D during training (discarded at inference)

pretraining.method: Self-supervised redundancy reduction (Barlow Twins) with mix-up regularization and global shuffling
pretraining.method_details: Two augmented views generated per d-pixel by independently sampling 40 valid timesteps; trained with Barlow Twins loss plus mix-up regularization loss (lambda_mix=1.0, lambda_BT=5e-3); uses global data shuffling to decorrelate spatial neighborhoods; one epoch with global batch size 32,768; AdamW optimizer (lr=0.002, weight decay 1e-6) with linear warmup and cosine decay; trained on 16 AMD MI300X GPUs (192GB each)
pretraining.objective: Barlow Twins cross-correlation loss (invariance + redundancy reduction) + mix-up cross-correlation loss

pretraining_data.num_images: ~800 million d-pixels from 3,012 global MGRS tiles (2017-2024)

key_contribution: A pixel-wise self-supervised foundation model for multi-modal Sentinel-1/2 time series that learns label-efficient embeddings via temporal sampling invariance, introducing the d-pixel representation, global shuffling, and mix-up regularization; releases FAIR global, annual, 10 m pixel-wise int8 embeddings as analysis-ready 'embeddings-as-data' through the GeoTessera Python library, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy across classification, segmentation, and regression benchmarks with minimal computation

title: CRS-Diff: Controllable Remote Sensing Image Generation with Diffusion Model

architecture.notes: Two-stage training: fine-tunes SD 1.5 backbone (~0.9B params) for RS text-to-image, then stacks ControlNet-style conditional branch with multi-scale Attention Feature Fusion (AFF) for image/metadata conditions. CLIP ViT-L-14 (123M) text encoder fine-tuned on RSICD.

pretraining.method: Diffusion model fine-tuning with multi-conditional control
pretraining.method_details: Stage 1: fine-tune SD 1.5 U-Net on RSICD text-image pairs (10 epochs, AdamW, lr=1e-5, 512x512). Stage 2: train ControlNet-style conditional branch on 200k image-text pairs from fMoW + Million-AID with decoupled conditions (HED, MLSD, Depthmap, Sketch, Seg mask, Road map, Content, Caption, Metadata) using AFF for multi-scale fusion (5 epochs, lr=1e-4). Classifier-free guidance with random condition dropout.
pretraining.objective: Denoising score matching (noise prediction MSE) with classifier-free guidance

pretraining_data.num_images: ~210,921 (10,921 RSICD + 200,000 image-text pairs from fMoW and Million-AID)
modalities: metadata (year, month, day, GSD, latitude, longitude, cloud cover)
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: 224x224 (RSICD, fMoW) and varying (Million-AID); resized to 512x512 for training

downstream_tasks: data augmentation for downstream tasks (e.g., road extraction)
key_contribution: First multiple-condition controllable diffusion-based generative model for remote sensing imagery, supporting simultaneous text, metadata, and image (HED, MLSD, depth, sketch, segmentation, roadmap, content) conditions via a multi-scale Attention Feature Fusion mechanism over a ControlNet-augmented Stable Diffusion backbone.

title: SenPa-MAE: Sensor Parameter Aware Masked Autoencoder for Multi-Satellite Self-Supervised Pretraining

architecture.backbone: ViT-Base (12 encoder layers, 12 heads; 3 decoder layers, 12 heads)
architecture.notes: ViT-Base MAE-style encoder-decoder with embedding dim 768, patch generated per-patch and per-channel; includes a Sensor Parameter Encoding module (two 5-layer MLPs g^SRF and g^GSD) injecting spectral response function and GSD embeddings

pretraining.method: Masked Autoencoder (MAE) with Sensor Parameter Encoding
pretraining.method_details: Per-channel patch tokenization (N = C x HW/P^2 tokens), 66% masking ratio, sensor parameter encoding module added to positional embeddings before encoder (and optionally before decoder), Spectral Superposition Augmentation (SSA) and resolution augmentation; trained 400 epochs, Adam optimizer, lr 1e-4 cosine decay, weight decay 0.05, batch size 128, 144x144 inputs with 4 randomly selected channels
pretraining.objective: Masked mean absolute error reconstruction of unmasked input

datasets: Custom multi-sensor dataset (Sentinel-2, Planet SuperDove, Landsat-8/9)
pretraining_data.num_images: ~116k patches of 256x256 px from 26 training locations (50k S2, 50k SD, 25k LS)
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: ~3.5 m (SD), 10 m and 20 m (S2), 30 m (LS); resampled to 5 m pixel spacing

key_contribution: Introduces a sensor-parameter-aware MAE that injects per-channel spectral response functions and GSD into ViT embeddings via a versatile encoding module, plus a Spectral Superposition Augmentation strategy, enabling multi-satellite self-supervised pretraining and sensor-independent inference across Sentinel-2, Planet SuperDove and Landsat.

title: One for All: Toward Unified Foundation Models for Earth Vision

architecture.notes: Single shared Transformer backbone with modality-specific patch embedding layers for five modalities; inputs resized to 224x224

pretraining.method_details: MAE-style masked image modeling with modality-specific decoders to reconstruct masked patches; does not require spatially aligned multi-modal data; pre-trained for 100 epochs on 50,000 samples (10,000 per sub-dataset)
pretraining.objective: Masked patch reconstruction

pretraining_data.num_images: ~8.08M total curated (4,642,353 S1 + 977,774 S2 + 117,450 Gaofen + 2,332,351 NAIP + 11,483 EnMAP); 50,000 used for pretraining

key_contribution: A unified foundation model using a single shared Transformer backbone with modality-specific patch embeddings to handle multiple remote sensing modalities and spatial resolutions simultaneously, pre-trained via masked image modeling without requiring spatially aligned multi-modal data.

title: LeMeViT: Efficient Vision Transformer with Learnable Meta Tokens for Remote Sensing Image Interpretation

architecture.backbone: Hierarchical Vision Transformer with Cross Attention, Dual Cross-Attention (DCA), and Standard Attention blocks; learnable meta tokens (M=16)
architecture.notes: Three variants: Tiny (8.64M), Small (16.04M), Base (53.10M). Uses learnable meta tokens initialized via cross-attention, then DCA blocks for efficient information exchange between image and meta tokens (linear complexity O(2MN) vs O(N^2)).

pretraining.method_details: Pretrained on MillionAID for 100 epochs following RSP setting; ImageNet-1K trained from scratch for 300 epochs with AdamW and cosine decay, following DeiT recipe.
pretraining.objective: Supervised classification

key_contribution: Proposes a hardware-friendly hierarchical Vision Transformer using a small number of learnable meta tokens and a Dual Cross-Attention (DCA) block to reduce self-attention complexity from quadratic to linear, achieving ~1.7x speedup with competitive accuracy on remote sensing tasks.

title: SMARTIES: Spectrum-Aware Multi-Sensor Auto-Encoder for Remote Sensing Images

architecture.notes: Vanilla MAE-style ViT encoder-decoder with sensor-agnostic spectrum-aware projection layers and reprojection layers; spectrum-aware embedding size D=768 (ViT-B) / 1024 (ViT-L)

pretraining.method: Masked Image Modeling with cross-sensor token mixup
pretraining.method_details: Spectrum-aware projection layers tokenize bands per wavelength range into a shared spectrum-aware space; pairs of co-located images from different sensors are mixed via cross-sensor token mixup; masked tokens (75% ratio) are reconstructed via spectrum-aware reprojection layers using MSE loss
pretraining.objective: Masked patch reconstruction (MSE) in spectrum-aware space

key_contribution: A single sensor-agnostic foundation model that projects heterogeneous RS sensors into a shared spectrum-aware space, enabling unified pretraining across diverse sensors, scalable training without sensor-specific backbones, and generalization to unseen sensors via projection-layer interpolation.

title: TerraFM: A Scalable Foundation Model for Unified Multisensor Earth Observation

architecture.notes: Modality-specific patch embeddings with cross-attention fusion module aggregating modality-specific tokens via learnable spatial queries; shared transformer encoder; teacher-student framework with EMA-updated teacher and three-layer projection head.

pretraining.method: Self-supervised contrastive learning (DINO-style student-teacher with multi-crop, modality augmentation, cross-attention fusion, dual centering)
pretraining.method_details: Builds on DINO with teacher-student EMA distillation; multi-crop learning (2 global + 6 local crops); modalities treated as natural augmentations stochastically assigned to student/teacher; cross-attention fusion with N_q=5 learnable spatial queries; dual-centering scheme combining global center with class-frequency-aware center using WorldCover-derived LULC statistics to address long-tailed distributions.
pretraining.objective: Cross-entropy consistency between teacher and student softmax outputs across views (contrastive self-distillation)

datasets: Major-TOM (filtered subset enriched with ESA WorldCover, Köppen-Geiger climate zones, ESRI World Regions metadata)
pretraining_data.num_images: 18.7M modality-specific training tiles (from 1.5M curated 10.68km x 10.68km grid cells, each split into 534x534 tiles)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Globally distributed terrestrial regions sampled with land-cover, climate-zone, and world-region priors (98% of ocean tiles removed)

key_contribution: A scalable multisensor EO foundation model that unifies Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 L1C/L2A optical inputs via modality-specific patch embeddings and cross-attention fusion, treats sensing modalities as natural augmentations within a DINO-style contrastive teacher-student framework, and introduces a dual-centering mechanism with class-frequency-aware regularization (using WorldCover priors) to mitigate long-tailed LULC distributions, achieving SoTA on GEO-Bench and Copernicus-Bench.

title: Harnessing Massive Satellite Imagery with Efficient Masked Image Modeling

architecture.notes: Evaluated with both ViT-B (86M) and ViT-L (307M) backbones; uses asymmetric encoder-decoder with lightweight cross-attention decoder following CrossMAE.

pretraining.method_details: SelectiveMAE: efficient MIM with Progressive Semantic Token Selection (PSTS) module. Uses HOG (Histogram of Oriented Gradients) features to quantify patch semantic richness, encoding only ~15% of semantically rich tokens and reconstructing only ~25% of patches. Progressively shifts from semantically similar (easy) to complementary (hard) tokens during training via curriculum-style selection. Achieves 2x+ pre-training speedup over MAE.
pretraining.objective: Pixel reconstruction (MSE loss) on selected patch tokens

pretraining_data.num_images: 13.2M (OpticalRS-13M); also OpticalRS-4M lightweight version with 3.92M images
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: high-resolution visible light imagery (avg ~199,214 pixels per image in OpticalRS-13M)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Diverse global coverage curated from multiple publicly available RS datasets via exclusion, slicing, and deduplication; 12 main scene/object/event categories

key_contribution: Introduces (1) OpticalRS-13M, a large-scale (13M images) curated optical RS pre-training dataset 4x larger than prior RS datasets, and (2) SelectiveMAE, an efficient MIM method with Progressive Semantic Token Selection using HOG features that achieves >2x pre-training speedup over MAE while improving downstream RS task performance.

title: Pattern Integration and Enhancement Vision Transformer for Self-supervised Learning in Remote Sensing

architecture.notes: Dual-stream teacher-student architecture with Geospatial Pattern Cohesion (GPC) module and Feature Integration Projection (FIP) module. Teacher updated via EMA.

pretraining.method_details: Teacher-student framework where teacher network is updated via Exponential Moving Average (EMA, decay 0.99) of student. GPC module aggregates geospatially clustered patches based on cohesion scores; FIP module uses cross-attention blocks to refine masked token reconstruction. Combines image-level (CLS) and patch-level (MIM) objectives. Mask ratio 0.1-0.5.
pretraining.objective: Combined CLS-level cross-entropy loss and patch-level MIM loss between student and teacher predictions

key_contribution: Introduces PIEViT, a self-supervised ViT for remote sensing that combines a Geospatial Pattern Cohesion (GPC) module to aggregate geospatially clustered patches and a Feature Integration Projection (FIP) module to refine masked token reconstruction, enabling discrimination of internal patch features in scenographic remote sensing imagery and achieving SOTA on object detection, land cover classification, and change detection.

title: Self-Supervised Spatio-Temporal Representation Learning Of Satellite Image Time Series

architecture.notes: Patch embedding via Unet-based spectro-spatial encoder (SSE) with 4 down/up-sampling levels; Transformer block with 3 layers, 4 heads, d_model=64, d_hidden=128; positional encoding based on day-of-year (DOY). Temporal attention applied at full spatial resolution on pixel-level time series.

pretraining.method_details: Generative pretext task inspired by BERT: a percentage (M_rate=60%) of SSE-encoded patch embeddings across timestamps are masked/corrupted by replacing with embeddings from another date, another pixel in the batch, or another patch. A shallow MLP decoder reconstructs the original S2 patch time series from the latent representation. Loss computed only on corrupted patches and only on valid (non-cloudy/in-swath) pixels using a validity mask.
pretraining.objective: Masked patch reconstruction with MSE loss on valid pixels of corrupted patches

datasets: Sentinel-2 L2A unlabeled dataset over France (13 S2 tiles, 2018-2020)
pretraining_data.num_images: 13 S2 tiles x 10 ROIs of 1024x1024 over 2018-2020 (annual SITS, ~64x64 patch time series used during training)
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: 10 m (20 m bands resampled to 10 m); 4 bands at 10 m + 6 bands at 20 m

key_contribution: A novel Unet+Transformer spatio-temporal architecture (U-BARN) for irregularly sampled, multi-variate Sentinel-2 SITS, combined with a BERT-inspired generative self-supervised pre-training strategy that masks SSE-encoded patch embeddings and reconstructs valid pixels, enabling rich spatio-temporal representations that improve crop and land cover semantic segmentation, especially under label scarcity.

title: MAESTRO: Masked AutoEncoders for Multimodal, Multitemporal, and Multispectral Earth Observation Data

architecture.notes: MAE-derived architecture with modality-specific tokenizers (shared across time steps within a modality, distinct across modalities), 2D spatial positional encodings based on ground sampling distance (as in ScaleMAE), and sine-cosine temporal encodings (as in SatMAE). Uses inter-group token-based fusion mode: early fusion across time steps and within groups of similar modalities, with the last three encoder blocks replaced by fusion blocks enabling cross-group token interactions.

pretraining.method: Masked Autoencoding (MAE) with token-based early fusion across time steps and similar modalities, late fusion across dissimilar modalities, and joint-token early fusion for multispectrality with patch-group-wise target normalization
pretraining.method_details: Two-stage masking: structured masking per modality, spatial, and temporal dimensions, plus unstructured adjustment to reach 75% overall masking ratio. Reconstruction targets are normalized patch-group-wise, where spectral bands are partitioned into groups of highly correlated bands and normalized independently per patch and band group, injecting a useful spectral prior. Three-phase workflow: SSL pre-training, probing, and fine-tuning.
pretraining.objective: Masked patch reconstruction with patch-group-wise normalized targets (L1 loss)

pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: 0.2 m (aerial), 1 m (SPOT 6-7 resampled), 1.25 m (NAIP), 10 m (Sentinel-2), variable (Sentinel-1)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: France (FLAIR-HUB, FLAIR#2, PASTIS-HD), Germany (TreeSatAI-TS), United States urban areas (S2-NAIP urban)

key_contribution: A tailored MAE adaptation for EO data that orchestrates multimodal, multitemporal, and multispectral inputs via inter-group token-based fusion (early fusion across time steps and similar modalities, late fusion across dissimilar ones) combined with joint-token multispectral fusion using a novel patch-group-wise target normalization that injects a spectral prior at negligible computational cost; achieves SOTA on tasks relying on multitemporal dynamics while remaining competitive on others.

title: Geography-Aware Self-Supervised Learning

architecture.notes: MoCo-v2 framework with ResNet-50 query and key encoders, plus a linear geo-location classifier head.

pretraining.method_details: Extends MoCo-v2 with TemporalInfoNCE using spatially aligned temporal positive pairs, combined with a geo-location classification pretext task on K=100 k-Means clusters of (lat, lon). Joint loss L = alpha*L_temporal + beta*L_geo (alpha=beta=1). 200 epochs, batch size 256, SGD, lr 1e-3, dictionary queue 65536, temperature 0.2.
pretraining.objective: TemporalInfoNCE contrastive loss + cross-entropy geo-location classification loss

datasets: GeoImageNet (geo-tagged ImageNet subset via FLICKR API)
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: sub-meter to a few meters (fMoW high-resolution satellite)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global (seven continents for fMoW; worldwide geo-tagged for GeoImageNet)

key_contribution: Introduces geography-aware self-supervised learning that exploits the spatio-temporal structure of remote sensing data via temporal positive pairs (TemporalInfoNCE) and a geo-location classification pretext task, closing the gap between contrastive and supervised learning on remote sensing benchmarks and improving transfer to detection, segmentation, and land cover tasks.

title: TEOChat: A Large Vision-Language Assistant for Temporal Earth Observation Data

architecture.backbone: CLIP ViT-L/14 image encoder + 2-layer MLP vision-language connector + LLaMA-2 (Vicuna) LLM decoder
architecture.notes: Temporally-shared CLIP ViT-L/14 image encoder, Video-LLaVA initialization, LLM fine-tuned with LoRA and 8-bit quantization; supports temporal sequences up to 8 EO images

pretraining.method_details: Initialized from Video-LLaVA (which uses CLIP-pretrained ViT-L/14 and Vicuna/LLaMA-2). Vision encoder frozen; LLM fine-tuned with LoRA; vision-language projector fully fine-tuned. Trained for 2 epochs (~14k iters) on TEOChatlas using next-token cross-entropy loss on response tokens.
pretraining.objective: Next-token cross-entropy on instruction responses (visual instruction tuning)

datasets: TEOChatlas (245,210 temporal EO instruction examples + 308,861 single-image GeoChat_Instruct examples = 554,071 total)
sensors: 8 sensors spanning the EO datasets including Sentinel-2
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global (considerable geographic diversity across fMoW, xBD, S2Looking, QFabric)

downstream_tasks: change detection (building damage assessment, building change detection)
key_contribution: First vision-language model for temporal sequences of Earth observation imagery, along with TEOChatlas, the first temporal EO instruction-following dataset; outperforms prior EO VLMs (GeoChat) and video VLMs (Video-LLaVA), rivals specialist models, and surpasses GPT-4o and Gemini-1.5 Pro on multiple temporal EO tasks.

title: EarthMarker: A Visual Prompting Multi-modal Large Language Model for Remote Sensing

architecture.backbone: Mixture of Visual Experts (MoV: DINOv2-ViT-L/14 + CLIP-ConvNeXt-L) + Llama 2 13B LLM decoder
architecture.type: Multi-modal Large Language Model (MLLM) with visual prompting
architecture.notes: Sharing visual encoding mechanism processes visual prompts (boxes/points) as RGB images. Modality-align projection layer connects vision to LLM. LoRA adapters added to LLM in final tuning stage. Disjoint parameters strategy across training phases.

pretraining.method: Three-phase cross-domain progressive learning: (1) multi-domain image-text alignment, (2) spatial perception tuning, (3) RS visual prompting tuning with LoRA
pretraining.method_details: Phase 1 trains only projection layer on COCO + RSVP image-level data; Phase 2 unfreezes LLM attention layers using RefCOCO/RefCOCO+; Phase 3 applies LoRA adapters and trains on RSVP region/point-text pairings. MoV visual encoders kept frozen throughout.
pretraining.objective: Visual instruction tuning / next-token prediction conditioned on visual prompts and text instructions

pretraining_data.num_images: ~3.65M image-point-text and image-region-text pairings in RSVP
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: Mixed: from 0.05m (Potsdam) to 30m, including VHR (<0.5m) and medium-to-low resolution
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Broad global coverage (most raw data sourced from Google Earth)

key_contribution: First visual prompting multi-modal large language model for remote sensing, enabling multi-granularity (image/region/point-level) RS imagery interpretation via box and point visual prompts. Introduces a sharing visual encoding mechanism (visual prompts as images), a cross-domain three-phase training strategy with disjoint parameters, and the RSVP dataset (~3.65M visual prompting instruction pairs) — the first RS visual prompting instruction dataset.

title: SkySenseGPT: A Fine-Grained Instruction Tuning Dataset and Model for Remote Sensing Vision-Language Understanding

architecture.backbone: CLIP-ViT-L/14 (336x336) vision encoder + MLP projector + Vicuna-v1.5 LLM
architecture.notes: Follows mainstream LMM design (LLaVA-v1.5 style); input images resized to 504x504; pre-trained MLP projector from LLaVA-v1.5; LoRA used to fine-tune the LLM, vision encoder frozen, projector fine-tuned

pretraining.method: Instruction tuning (LoRA fine-tuning of LLM on FIT-RS plus public RS datasets)
pretraining.method_details: Vision encoder (CLIP-ViT-L/14) frozen; MLP projector fine-tuned; Vicuna-v1.5 LLM fine-tuned via LoRA on combined instruction tuning data (1415k from FIT-RS train/val + 365k from public datasets). Trained on 4x NVIDIA A100 (40G).
pretraining.objective: Autoregressive instruction-following (next-token prediction) for fine-grained remote sensing vision-language understanding

datasets: FIT-RS (built on STAR scene graph dataset)
pretraining_data.num_images: FIT-RS contains 1,800,851 instruction samples derived from 82,532 image patches (cropped from 1,273 large-size VHR RSIs); ~1415k FIT-RS train/val instruction samples + 365k from public datasets used for instruction tuning
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: Very-high-resolution (VHR); large-size RSIs ranging from 512x768 to 27,860x31,096 pixels, cropped via 512x512 sliding window with 100-pixel overlap
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Worldwide VHR remote sensing imagery covering complex semantic scenarios (airports, dams, ports, etc.)

key_contribution: Introduces FIT-RS, a large-scale (1.8M samples) fine-grained instruction tuning dataset for remote sensing built from scene graph annotations, plus the FIT-RSRC benchmark for relation comprehension; proposes SkySenseGPT, an RSLMM that excels at fine-grained relation reasoning and scene graph generation, surpassing prior RSLMMs on public benchmarks.

title: SatlasPretrain: A Large-Scale Dataset for Remote Sensing Image Understanding

architecture.notes: SatlasNet uses Swin-Base backbone with temporal max pooling across image time series, multi-scale features at 4 scales, and seven task-specific output heads (U-Net for segmentation/regression, Faster R-CNN for points, Mask R-CNN for polygons, U-Net + post-processing for polylines, pooling+linear for properties/classification).

pretraining.method_details: Jointly trains a single Swin-Base backbone with seven task-specific heads on 137 categories across 7 label types (segmentation, regression, points, polygons, polylines, properties, classification). Initialized with ImageNet weights, Adam optimizer, lr 1e-4 decaying to 1e-6, batch size 32 for 100K batches.
pretraining.objective: Multi-task supervised loss (cross-entropy for segmentation/classification, L1 for regression, Faster/Mask R-CNN losses for detection/instance segmentation)

datasets: SatlasPretrain (Sentinel-2 + NAIP imagery, labels from OpenStreetMap, NOAA lidar scans, WorldCover, Microsoft Buildings, C2S, plus expert and AMT annotations)
pretraining_data.num_images: 856K Web-Mercator tiles (zoom 13); 828K train + 28K test low-res tiles with 8-12 Sentinel-2 images each; 46K high-res NAIP tiles (8192x8192) with 3-5 images; 17M image megapixels; 302M labels under 137 categories
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global for Sentinel-2 (all continents except Antarctica, 21,320,000 sq km covered); United States only for NAIP high-resolution

key_contribution: A very-large-scale multi-task remote sensing dataset (40x more pixels and 150x more labels than prior datasets) with 137 categories across 7 label types and spatio-temporal image series, plus SatlasNet, a unified multi-head model. Pretraining on SatlasPretrain improves downstream accuracy by 18% over ImageNet and 6% over the next best remote sensing baseline.

title: LHRS-Bot-Nova: Improved Multimodal Large Language Model for Remote Sensing Vision-Language Interpretation

architecture.backbone: SigLIP-L/14 vision encoder + LLaMA3-8B LLM with MoE-based vision perceiver bridge
architecture.notes: Vision encoder: SigLIP-L/14 at 336x336 resolution. Vision perceiver uses learnable queries with cross-attention and MoE-FFN (4 experts, top-2 routed) over 6 layers. LLM: LLaMA3-8B with LoRA (rank 128, alpha 256). Multi-level feature extraction at layers {N_L/3, 2N_L/3, N_L-1} with query allocation {112, 96, 64}.

pretraining.method: Three-stage curriculum learning: vision-language pretraining, multi-task instruction fine-tuning, supervised fine-tuning
pretraining.method_details: Stage 1 pretrains vision encoder and vision perceiver on LHRS-Align-Recap image-caption pairs. Stage 2 unfreezes LLM with LoRA and trains on multi-task instruction dataset. Stage 3 fine-tunes vision perceiver and LoRA on LHRS-Instruct, LHRS-Instruct-Plus, and LRV-Instruct.
pretraining.objective: Vision-language alignment via image captioning and instruction-following (autoregressive language modeling)

datasets: LHRS-Align-Recap (feature-guided recaptioned via Share-Captioner using OSM features)
LHRS-Instruct-Plus (generated via GPT-4V from DOTAv2 and FAIR1M)
Multi-task dataset combining RSVQA-HR/LR, UCM, fMoW, METER-ML, RSVG, DIOR-RSVG, RSICD
pretraining_data.num_images: ~1.1M+ (LHRS-Align-Recap caption set; instruction-tuning includes 67K DOTAv2 + 253K FAIR1M conversations + 140K LRV-Instruct + others)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global (derived from public RS datasets paired with OSM)

key_contribution: An RS-specialized MLLM combining a scaled-up SigLIP vision encoder with a novel MoE-based vision perceiver bridge layer, trained on a new feature-guided recaptioned image-caption dataset (LHRS-Align-Recap) and an extended spatial-recognition instruction dataset (LHRS-Instruct-Plus), achieving state-of-the-art performance across RS scene classification, VQA, visual grounding, and the LHRS-Bench MCQ benchmark.

title: A Billion-scale Foundation Model for Remote Sensing Images

architecture.notes: Plain ViT scaled via parallel MHSA/FFN blocks; 12 layers, 4 parallelism, hidden size 2048, MLP size 8192, 32 heads. Also trained ViT-B12x1 (86M), ViT-L12x4 (605.26M), ViT-H12x4 (1.36B). Backbone converted to ViTDET with local/global attention for downstream object localization.

pretraining.method_details: MAE with 75% masking ratio, 224x224 input, 16x16 patches, 400 epochs (1600 for ViT-B), AdamW, batch size 2048, fp16 + activation checkpointing. Effective LR = batch_size * BaseLR / 256.
pretraining.objective: Masked image reconstruction (MSE on masked patches)

key_contribution: First billion-scale (2.4B parameter) foundation model for remote sensing, demonstrating that scaling vision transformers via parallel MHSA/FFN layers (rather than depth) improves performance and sample efficiency on rotated object detection and semantic segmentation downstream tasks.

title: Exploring Masked Autoencoders for Sensor-Agnostic Image Retrieval in Remote Sensing

architecture.type: Vision Transformer (multi-sensor encoder + cross-sensor encoder + multi-sensor decoder)
architecture.notes: Four CSMAE variants: CECD (sensor-common encoder & decoder), CESD, SECD, SESD; embedding dim 512; 8 transformer blocks, 16 heads in decoder; patch size 15x15; 50% masking ratio.

pretraining.method_details: Adaptation of MAE for multi-modal/multi-sensor RS image archives. Includes uni-modal reconstruction loss (L_UMR) and cross-modal reconstruction loss (L_CMR), optionally combined with inter-modal latent similarity preservation losses (L_MDE or L_MIM). Three multi-modal masking correspondences explored: identical, random, disjoint.
pretraining.objective: Masked image modeling with uni-modal + cross-modal reconstruction (MSE) plus optional inter-modal discrepancy elimination (MDE) or mutual information maximization (MIM)

datasets: BigEarthNet (BEN-270K subset over 10 European countries; BEN-14K subset over Serbia)

key_contribution: First systematic study of masked autoencoders for sensor-agnostic (cross-sensor) content-based image retrieval in remote sensing. Introduces four CSMAE variants exploring sensor-common vs sensor-specific encoders/decoders, three multi-modal masking correspondences, and combined uni-modal + cross-modal reconstruction objectives with optional inter-modal latent similarity preservation, enabling MIM-based representation learning across SAR and multispectral RS modalities.

title: GeoPixel: Pixel Grounding Large Multimodal Model in Remote Sensing

architecture.backbone: InternLM-XComposer-2.5 (IXC-2.5) 7B LLM (InternLM2 7B) with CLIP ViT-L/14 vision encoder (560x560), SAM-2 (Hiera MAE) grounding vision encoder, and pixel decoder
architecture.type: Vision-Language Multimodal Model with pixel grounding (LMM)
architecture.notes: 5 components: Adaptive Image Divider, Vision Encoder (CLIP ViT-L/14), LLM (InternLM2 7B), Grounding Vision Encoder (SAM-2 Hiera), and Pixel Decoder. Uses Partial LoRA (alpha=8), two-layer MLP vision projector, and text projection MLP. Supports dynamic resolution up to 4K HD via adaptive image partitioning into local patches plus global view.

pretraining.method: Instruction tuning / fine-tuning of pretrained LMM with LoRA on grounded conversation generation data
pretraining.method_details: Initialized from pretrained IXC-2.5 (7B) LLM, frozen CLIP ViT-L vision encoder, and SAM-2 pretrained grounding vision encoder (frozen). Trainable components: pixel decoder, LoRA params (alpha=8), vision projector, language projector. Trained with effective batch size 20 over 10 epochs, lr warmup to 3e-4 over 100 steps then cosine decay, on 2x NVIDIA A6000-48GB GPUs for ~3 days on the GeoPixelD dataset.
pretraining.objective: Grounded conversation generation (GCG) with multi-target pixel-level segmentation mask prediction and language modeling; <SEG> token embeddings decoded into segmentation masks

pretraining_data.num_images: 16,795 holistic image-level annotations; 36,793 instance-specific annotations; 17,023 group annotations; 600,817 objects; 53,816 grounded phrases
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: high-resolution aerial imagery cropped to 800x800 patches; supports dynamic input resolution up to 4K
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: derived from iSAID aerial imagery dataset (global aerial scenes)

downstream_datasets: GeoPixelD (RS-GCG benchmark, test set from iSAID validation subset, 5,427 referring expression-segmentation pairs, 61,384 annotated objects)
key_contribution: First end-to-end high-resolution RS large multimodal model (LMM) supporting pixel-level grounding with multi-target segmentation masks at up to 4K resolution via adaptive image partitioning; introduces GeoPixelD, a semi-automated visually grounded RS dataset for grounded conversation generation, and a benchmark for evaluating RS LMMs on fine-grained visual understanding.

title: MMEarth: Exploring Multi-Modal Pretext Tasks For Geospatial Representation Learning

architecture.notes: Fully Convolutional Masked Autoencoder (FCMAE) with sparse convolutions; adapted patch size 16x16 and modified first layer (kernel 3, stride 1 + depth-wise conv) for 112x112 Sentinel-2 inputs; shallow per-modality decoders

pretraining.method_details: Extends ConvNeXt V2 FCMAE with multi-modal reconstruction across 12 aligned pixel- and image-level modalities; shared encoder with per-task decoders; multi-task loss with task-uncertainty weighting; trained 200 epochs
pretraining.objective: Multi-task masked reconstruction (MSE for continuous, cross-entropy for categorical) over pixel-level and image-level pretext targets

pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global, 1.2M locations sampled across 14 biomes, years 2017-2020

key_contribution: Introduces MMEarth, a global 1.2M-location multi-modal Sentinel-2-aligned pretraining dataset with 12 modalities, and proposes MP-MAE, a ConvNeXt V2-based fully convolutional multi-pretext masked autoencoder that improves fine-tuning and especially linear-probing performance over ImageNet and Sentinel-2-only MAE baselines

title: S2-CGAN: Self-Supervised Adversarial Representation Learning for Binary Change Detection in Multispectral Images

architecture.type: conditional GAN with U-Net generator and patch-based discriminator
architecture.notes: Generator G is a U-Net autoencoder with six convolutional layers; discriminator D is a pixel-based network with two convolutional layers. Operates on 128x128 patches.

pretraining.method_details: A self-supervised conditional GAN trained only on pairs of unchanged co-registered patches. The generator learns to reconstruct the unchanged distribution while the discriminator learns to distinguish unchanged samples; the discriminator is then directly used as the change classifier (changes as outliers).
pretraining.objective: adversarial training on unchanged sample reconstruction

key_contribution: Proposes S2-cGAN, the first self-supervised conditional GAN that directly exploits the discriminator's likelihood to formulate binary change detection. Trained only on unchanged sample pairs with no labels for changed samples, the method achieves competitive performance against fully-supervised methods.

title: Self-supervised pre-training enhances change detection in Sentinel-2 imagery

architecture.type: shallow Siamese CNN with three convolutional layers per branch and a fusion layer
architecture.notes: Pretext task 1 uses Siamese with fusion layer + classifier; pretext task 2 uses Siamese without fusion. 32-channel feature maps.

pretraining.method_details: Two pretext tasks for change detection pretraining: (1) binary classification of whether two patches are spatially overlapping or not, and (2) triplet margin loss minimizing feature distance between overlapping patches and pushing apart non-overlapping ones.
pretraining.objective: overlap discrimination and triplet contrastive learning between patches

key_contribution: Introduces the Sentinel-2 Multitemporal Cities Pairs (S2MTCP) dataset of 1520 global urban image pairs and shows that self-supervised pretraining on this dataset (using overlap discrimination or triplet contrastive pretext tasks) enables state-of-the-art change detection on OSCD with minimal labeled data.

title: Self-Supervised Multisensor Change Detection

architecture.type: two-branch (optical/SAR) CNN with shared common layer; non-shared projection modules and shared prediction module
architecture.notes: L=5 conv layers (L1=4 separate per modality, L2=1 common). Kernel size 3x3, K clusters as final-layer output channels, K=4 used in experiments.

pretraining.method_details: Self-supervised training combining deep clustering, temporal consistency loss, and contrastive loss on a single bi-temporal optical-SAR scene. Projection modules are modality-specific, predictors are shared. Trained on 64x64 patches from the target scene only.
pretraining.objective: deep clustering + temporal consistency + contrastive loss

datasets: target bi-temporal scenes only (Las Vegas, Chongqing, Abu Dhabi, Montpellier)
pretraining_data.num_images: single bi-temporal scene per experiment (few hundred px)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: four scenes (Las Vegas USA, Chongqing China, Abu Dhabi UAE, Montpellier France)

key_contribution: Proposes a self-supervised method for multi-sensor (optical-SAR) change detection that requires only the bi-temporal target scene with no labels or additional unlabeled images. Combines deep clustering, temporal consistency loss, and contrastive loss in a Siamese architecture, outperforming prior unsupervised multisensor CD methods.

title: Self-supervised Change Detection in Multi-view Remote Sensing Images

architecture.type: pseudo-Siamese network (two branches with same architecture but unshared weights)
architecture.notes: Strider parameters changed from 2 to 1 in third and fourth layers to adapt to small input patches. Patch sizes p=8,16,24 for homogeneous; p=8,16 for heterogeneous.

pretraining.method_details: Combines a multi-view contrastive loss (heterogeneous images, with InfoNCE-style negatives from a memory bank) and BYOL-style implicit contrastive learning (homogeneous images via mean teacher with EMA). Trained on archived multi-temporal/multi-sensor image pairs.
pretraining.objective: multi-view contrastive (InfoNCE) + BYOL regression

datasets: augmented OSCD subsets (S2S2, S1S1, L8S2, S1S2)

key_contribution: Proposes a self-supervised change detection approach for multi-view remote sensing images using a pseudo-Siamese ResNet-34 trained with multi-view contrastive loss (heterogeneous case) and BYOL (homogeneous case). Introduces a multi-scale change scoring strategy and validates on five homogeneous and heterogeneous datasets.

title: Task-Related Self-Supervised Learning for Remote Sensing Image Change Detection

architecture.type: Siamese-style backbone with two FC layers for change classification
architecture.notes: Backbone composed of four conv layers + ReLU; uses contrastive Euclidean loss with margin m for SSL.

pretraining.method_details: Task-related self-supervised module generates pair-wised similar/dissimilar samples by unsupervised image segmentation (felzenszwalb-style) on a single bi-temporal scene, then trains the backbone with contrastive loss. Hard-sample-mining loss reweights hard samples; smooth mechanism removes pseudo-changes.
pretraining.objective: pairwise contrastive learning on segmented patches

datasets: target bi-temporal scenes (WV3 Site 1, WV3 Site 2, ZY3, QB)

key_contribution: Introduces TSLCD, an unsupervised change detection framework combining a task-related self-supervised module (contrastive pretraining on segmentation-derived pseudo-labels), a hard-sample-mining loss, and a smooth mechanism. Achieves state-of-the-art unsupervised CD across four datasets from different sensors.

title: Self-supervised Remote Sensing Images Change Detection at Pixel-level

architecture.type: Siamese encoder-decoder with vector quantization module
architecture.notes: ResUnet encoder has 3 residual blocks + Conv-BN-ReLU-Pool, decoder mirrors with skip connections. Vector quantization with codebook size 1024 using Gumbel-Softmax.

pretraining.method_details: Pixel-wise contrastive learning via shift-equivariance pretext task: cropped image pair with relative shift fed through Siamese ResUnet, with reverse shift applied to outputs to align features. Vector quantization with codebook loss augments learned representations. Optional teacher-student uncertainty-aware feature learning step using KL loss.
pretraining.objective: pixel-wise contrastive learning with shift equivariance + codebook loss + uncertainty estimation

key_contribution: First pixel-wise contrastive self-supervised approach for change detection in remote sensing. Combines a shift-equivariance contrastive pretext on a Siamese ResUnet with vector quantization and an uncertainty-aware teacher-student paradigm to suppress regular seasonal changes, improving over patch-based SSL CD.

title: Contrastive Multiview Coding with Electro-optics for SAR Semantic Segmentation

architecture.type: contrastive multiview coding encoder (also evaluated with ResNeXt-50 and ResNeSt-50)
architecture.notes: Base encoder with projection head (linear -> BN -> ReLU -> linear, output dim 2048). Tested with U-Net, BiSeNet, DeepLabV3 segmentation heads.

pretraining.method_details: Contrastive Multiview Coding using SAR, EO, and segmentation map as multiple modalities of the same scene. Full-graph optimization considers all modality pairs; positive samples are images of the same location across modalities and augmentations.
pretraining.objective: contrastive multiview coding across SAR, EO, and segmentation map

pretraining_data.num_images: scenes patched into 300x300 with 150 strides, resized to 448x448

key_contribution: First multi-modal contrastive multiview coding approach in remote sensing that uses an auxiliary EO sensor (and optionally segmentation maps) to pretrain SAR semantic segmentation models. Demonstrates that more modality views improve SAR building segmentation performance, sample efficiency, and convergence speed across multiple backbones and segmentation heads.

title: Continual Barlow Twins: continual self-supervised learning for remote sensing semantic segmentation

architecture.type: Barlow Twins encoder + projector with EWC regularization for continual learning; UNet++ for downstream segmentation
architecture.notes: ResNet-50 backbone trained with Barlow Twins loss; downstream UNet++ with Squeeze-and-Excitation.

pretraining.method_details: Combines Barlow Twins (cross-correlation matrix close to identity) with Elastic Weight Consolidation (EWC) regularization to enable incremental self-supervised pretraining across heterogeneous EO datasets without catastrophic forgetting.
pretraining.objective: Barlow Twins redundancy reduction + EWC regularization

key_contribution: First combination of self-supervised learning and continual learning for remote sensing. Continual Barlow Twins integrates Barlow Twins with Elastic Weight Consolidation, enabling incremental pretraining on heterogeneous EO datasets (drone, aerial, satellite) without catastrophic forgetting, and proposes a new mixed-domain segmentation benchmark.

title: Self Supervised Learning for Few Shot Hyperspectral Image Classification

architecture.type: shared encoder + MLP projector for Barlow Twins
architecture.notes: 2D CNN uses 9x9 patches; 1D CNN uses single-pixel inputs in 5x5 neighborhood for pair sampling.

pretraining.method_details: Pretrain encoder on unlabeled HSI pixels using Barlow-Twins via redundancy reduction. Views generated by spatial transforms (flip, rotation, crop) and spectral transforms (scaling, Gaussian noise, band dropping, pixel removal, band swapping, translation), plus partially overlapping patch pair sampling.
pretraining.objective: redundancy reduction (Barlow Twins) on HSI patches/pixels

pretraining_data.num_images: single HSI scenes (610x340 with 9 classes, 349x1905 with 144 bands and 15 classes)

key_contribution: Demonstrates that Barlow-Twins-based self-supervised pretraining on unlabeled hyperspectral pixels significantly outperforms supervised baselines for few-shot HSI classification. Introduces patch/pixel pair sampling strategies that exploit spatial regularity and analyzes the impact of spatial vs. spectral data augmentations.

title: Contrastive ground-level image and remote sensing pre-training improves representation learning for natural world imagery

architecture.notes: Replaces CLIP text encoder with another image encoder. Four variants: standard CRISP, CRISP-Aug (with RS data augmentation), CRISP-M2o (many-to-one positive pooling), CRISP-Par (parameterized loss weighting).

pretraining.method_details: ContRastive Image-remote Sensing Pre-training (CRISP) replaces CLIP text encoder with an aerial image encoder and learns joint representation between ground-level and aerial image pairs via symmetric InfoNCE loss. Variants include data augmentation, many-to-one positives, and parameterized loss weighting.
pretraining.objective: image-image contrastive (InfoNCE) between ground-level and aerial views

pretraining_data.num_images: >3 million ground-aerial image pairs from 1.75M observations

key_contribution: Introduces CRISP, an image-image contrastive pre-training approach pairing ground-level and aerial imagery for natural world representation learning, and the Nature Multi-View dataset (>3M pairs across 6,000+ California plant taxa). CRISP outperforms ImageNet, MAE, and MoCo baselines on fine-grained species recognition, especially in low-data regimes.
